meta.name: 221B Baker St.
meta.desc:
Up to four players compete against each other to solve various mysteries in and around Victorian London. Each player can assume the role of one of four characters from the Sherlock Holmes books—Sherlock Holmes, Dr. Watson, Irene Adler or Inspector Lestrade—and can select from 30 different adventures. Case file for each mystery are provided in the manual and give the particulars of the case, including key clues and suspects.
To solve the case, players roll dice to move among 15 different locales throughout London, collecting the clues necessary to unravel the mystery. Each player also visits Scotland Yard to collect a badge, which allows him to "lock" a particular location on the map, making access to it by the other players more difficult. Once a player believes he has solved the case, he returns to Baker Street where he must successfully answer a quiz. Play ends when a player successfully passes the quiz.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/221B Baker Street (1986)(Datasoft)(Side A)[cr Digital Gang]-manual.pdf

p: 3 in 1 College & Pro Football (1984)(Lance Haffner Games)(US).zip
n: 3 in 1 College & Pro Football (1984)(Lance Haffner Games)(US)

meta.path: ./3 in 1 College & Pro Football (1984)(Lance Haffner Games)(US).zip
meta.name: 3 in 1 College & Pro Football Game
meta.desc:
3 in 1 College & Pro Football is a text-based American football simulator.
An all-strategy football simulator done entirely in text. Players can use existing teams, draft players from existing teams, or create their own from scratch. Gameplay involves selecting offensive and defensive plays for teams, and watching a text-based readout of the action. A text-based scorecard and timer help inform the player at all times.
While the game contains real team and player names, they are not licensed.

meta.name: 3-D Docking Mission
meta.desc: 3-D Docking Mission is a game where the player has to steer a spaceship through an asteroid field and reach a docking station. The screen is split in two and shows both a top view and a side view of the asteroid field. In the side view the player can move the ship up, down, forward and reverse, while in the top view movement can only be done left and right. The game uses thruster controls, where one button press causes the ship to move until the opposite key is pressed. A collision occurs when hitting an asteroid in both the top view and the side view, and the game then has to be restarted. It is also possible to run out of fuel, which happens when using too much thrust. When reaching the docking station, a score is given based on the time it has taken

meta.name: A-E
meta.desc:
Robots developed for pollution control are running amuck, in this invasion contest. They swirl over the horizon and zoom hither and yon across the playfield, as they try to destroy the player's active cannon (three are available in all) as it moves back and forth along the bottom boundary. The idea is to push the crazed robots back wave by wave until, on the eighth screen, you move them more than 600 billion miles from Earth.
A. E.'s shooting mechanic is unorthodox.  The gamer pushes the action-button to launch a rocket, releasing it the instant at which detonation is desired.

meta.name: ABM
meta.desc: ABM is an EGA graphic remake of Missile Command. In this game you must stop missiles from coming down to destroy Shield Generators. You can use your mouse, or your arrow keys to aim where you want to fire, then click on the mouse buttons to fire and destroy the missiles. There are infinite levels and no high score table. When you get a game over their will be Statistics showing how many missiles you destroyed and how many interceptors you launched.

p: Abyssal Zone, The (1984)(Salty Software)(Side A)[cr Black Bag - Incorrigibles].zip
n: Abyssal Zone, The (1984)(Salty Software)(Side A)[cr Black Bag - Incorrigibles]

meta.path: ./Abyssal Zone, The (1984)(Salty Software)(Side A)[cr Black Bag - Incorrigibles].zip
meta.name: The Abyssal Zone
meta.desc: The Abyssal Zone is a graphics adventure game published by Salty Software and designed by Jeff Gray in 1984 for the Apple II. When the game begins, the player finds himself on a deserted island with a roaring volcano about to erupt.

meta.name: Acid Trap
meta.desc: Hector Cherny just dropped 600 micrograms of LSD, a trip that he will not come down from with ease. Your job is to help Hector back down to earth.

meta.name: Advanced Blackjack
meta.desc:
Advanced Blackjack is a blackjack simulator and teaching tool for the Apple II.
Designed to teach a player to improve their blackjack skills, the program allows the player to practice betting, counting cards, or a focus on difficult hands. The player is also able to play a blackjack game against the computer, or have the computer play for them. The computer can also assist the player by telling them the ideal play in each situation, and only allowing them to play the best moves. The player can also receive a summary of their total blackjack performance.

meta.name: Adventure
meta.desc:
A graphic dungeon quest inspired by the old mainframe game of the same name, Adventure is a graphic action-adventure game where you use your joystick to wander around, collecting keys to explore castles, labyrinths and everything in between, whilst slaying anything that moves (i.e. Dragons & Co.) to get treasures. 
The game has 3 difficulty settings:Level 1 (Easiest)Level 2: The Kingdom is larger than Level 1, containing secret rooms and hidden items to add difficulty.Level 3 (Hardest): Similar to Level 2 but all objects/items are placed randomly throughout the Kingdom.Gameplay revolves around picking up (and dropping) items, hacking your way though dragons, finding keys to open the castle gates and using magic (i.e. reincarnation) in case you get swallowed by failing-to-follow-their-diet-plan-dragons.

n: Adventure - Only the Fittest Shall Survive, The v3.5 (1985)(Green Valley Publishing)

p: Adventure 1 - Adventureland (1980)(Creative Computing Software)[h no splash screen].zip
n: Adventure 1 - Adventureland (1980)(Creative Computing Software)[h no splash screen]

meta.path: ./Adventure 1 - Adventureland (1980)(Creative Computing Software)[h no splash screen].zip
meta.name: Adventure 1 - Adventureland (1980)(Creative Computing Software)[h no splash screen]
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Adventure 1 - Adventureland (1980)(Creative Computing Software)[h no splash screen]-marquee.png

meta.name: Adventure #4 - Voodoo Castle
meta.desc: The Count of Monte Cristo was eventually consumed entirely by his drive for revenge.  Count Cristo, on the other hand, is merely the victim of a Voodoo curse an enemy hexed him with... an external plague more possible to reverse.  Starting the game standing over his coffin, you wander the length and breadth of his castle in pursuit of a recipe and the verbal and material components needed to cast an antidote to counter his occult menace.  It contains good-luck charms, the sorts of mad scientist chemical concoctions that might well yield a Jekyll-and-Hyde transformation, a flighty medium and her crystal ball, and no small quantity of juju, useful for all sorts of voodoo applications.  All that and a chimneysweep trapped in the flue!  The player will navigate all these and more with the trademark Adventure International VERB NOUN two-word text parser.

meta.name: Aeronaut
meta.desc: The player's task is to defend various cities from enemy armies. For this purpose, there is a modern weapon at your disposal in the form of a drone equipped with anti-tank missiles. During the flight it is necessary to avoid enemy missiles and rockets. In general, the destruction of their bunkers means victory in a given city. Thus, it is not necessary to eliminate enemy units, however, this allows you to reach your goal more easily.

p: Air Raid Pearl Harbor (1990)(General Quarters)(II+)(Side A)[64K].zip
n: Air Raid Pearl Harbor (1990)(General Quarters)(II+)(Side A)[64K]

meta.name: Air Raid Pearl Harbor
meta.desc:
Air Raid Pearl Harbor is a tactical naval warfare focused on the Japanese raid on Pearl Harbor.
The player can control either the Japanese or American forces based around Pearl Harbor during the raid. The player can choose the day of the initial invasion, and have to deal with historic weather conditions. Players control submarines, air forces, and their main naval forces, and must set the direction and speed of their vessels, as well as engage in combat with enemy forces. The player can split and merge their forces to choose whether they should travel in different directions, and must carefully manage their fighter craft compliment from their aircraft carrier. Ultimately, the victor is the player who destroy the other's forces.

meta.name: Air Traffic Controller
meta.desc:
Air Traffic Controller is a one player game written in ST-Pascal for high resolution where you take on the role as an air traffic controller. The goal of the game is to keep going as long as possible. The game ends when: 
A plane crashes, i.e. when it's altitude is zero in the wrong place.
A plane leaves the area at the wrong altitude or in the wrong place.
A plane lands instead of exiting the area and the other way around.
A plane lands at the wrong airport.
Two planes collide.
The radar displays jets as lower case letters and propeller planes as upper case letters, the digit attached to the letter is the planes altitude. Airports is marked with an 'a' and an arrow that shows the direction on which planes are supposed to take off or land. Beacons are represented by '*', you can tell a plane in advance to do something once it gets to a beacon. The active list shows you what planes are in the air, their altitude, where they are heading and whether delayed commands have been issued. Under 'holdings' you can also see what planes are waiting at airports fore take-off.
The game comes with 13 game files and since they are in plain ASCII (and explained in the manual) the player can easily construct his/her own game files.

meta.name: Airplane Simulator
meta.desc: Airplane Simulator captures the appeal of early flight simulation surprisingly well and places you right into the cockpit perspective. Its clearest strength is the well-organized display of flight data, which lets you keep close control over your aircraft at all times. Alongside the forward view, an additional map display helps with orientation, allowing navigation and control to work together smoothly. That blend of overview, precision, and immediate flying sensation makes it a compelling example of its kind.

p: Akalabeth - World of Doom (1980)(California Pacific Computer)[48K].zip
n: Akalabeth - World of Doom (1980)(California Pacific Computer)[48K]

meta.name: Akalabeth : World of Doom
meta.desc:
Once, the land of Akalabeth was peaceful and quiet. But then came Mondain, an evil and ambitious man who created dungeons and populated them with foul creatures. The noble warrior British was able to drive Mondain from Akalabeth, but the monsters he brought with him remain.
As either a fighter or a mage, it is your job to explore Akalabeth, descend into the dungeons and vanquish the beasts dwelling within. On an overhead map of the land, one can find the dungeons, towns where one may buy supplies, weapons and armor, and a castle, where one will be given quests by Lord British. To fulfill such a quest, a certain type of creature must be destroyed in a dungeon.
Inside the dungeons, the view switches to a first-person perspective. Fight, explore and find treasure on your way to complete a quest. Each successive quest will require you to destroy a stronger monster, which will reside on a deeper dungeon level than the one before it.
Akalabeth is the precursor to the Ultima Series.

p: Alcazar the Forgotten Fortress (1985)(Activision)[cr Disk Jockey][48K].zip
n: Alcazar the Forgotten Fortress (1985)(Activision)[cr Disk Jockey][48K]

meta.name: Alcazar the Forgotten Fortress
meta.desc:
In this adventure game you control an explorer who's on his way to fortress Alcazar. To reach Alcazar you are travelling through a countryside of fortresses and you need to collect several items that you can find along the way.
Once you enter the Alcazar fortress you have to search for the crown.
The strategy map gives you an overview of the countryside, it's fortresses and your location in it.
What perils lurk in these mysterious castles? Which path is 
quick and safe?
The Alcazar castle is three stories high, all other castles have only two stories. To enter a castle you just have to follow the path.
Each castle has it's own sought-after objects and these objects are always guarded by the castles main demon.
Objects to look for are for example: a rope, a gun, raw meat, water or a raft. Collect them and use them properly to master
the legendary Alcazar fortress.
The game gives you clues and tells,  for example, which monsters are nearby. Some monsters are really hard to kill.
The game can be played at four difficulty levels.

meta.name: Alesia
meta.desc:
A French language text adventure. You are in 52 BC in a small Gallic village near Alesia surrounded by Roman legions. Your supplies being almost exhausted, and the attack imminent, the chief of Tartopum (pronounced 'Tartopom' - Applepie!) charges you with the following mission:
You must infiltrate the enemy camp and steal the battle plan of general Micrus Ordinatus (- Microcomputer) so that the Gauls win the battle. The mission is very perilous and you will have to use your intuition and finesse to carry it out, because the traps are numerous... Maybe you will manage to change the course of history...

p: Alice in Wonderland (1985)(Windham Classics)(II+)(Side A)[cr Gadget Master][64K].zip
n: Alice in Wonderland (1985)(Windham Classics)(II+)(Side A)[cr Gadget Master][64K]

meta.path: ./Alice in Wonderland (1985)(Windham Classics)(II+)(Side A)[cr Gadget Master][64K].zip
meta.name: Alice in Wonderland
meta.desc:
Alice in Wonderland is an adventure platformer based upon the two Alice books by Lewis Carrol and created using the same engine as Below the Root.  However, whereas that game had more of a focus on role playing, Alice has more of a focus on puzzle solving.
Puzzles come in a variety of forms including many inventory based puzzles, and more appropriately, given the subject matter, riddles.  Also, there are many physical puzzles primarily taking the form of how to get from here to there through jumping and using inventory items to change Alice's size.  
The plot is more inspired by the Alice books than strictly adapting them.  Especially the game tries to adapt Carrol's sense of humor in its rhymes and puns. Interestingly, Alice's end of conversations are driven by selecting emotions rather than typing words, and it is possible to anger NPCs and have them stalk off, leaving you to wait until a pre-appointed hour when they will reappear.  The time can be discovered by looking at one of the various clocks throughout the game.  Time is important because the game must be completed before the Red King wakes, although this isn't much of an issue especially since the game can be saved whenever and mistakes easily taken back.

meta.name: Alien
meta.desc:
ALIEN is a game where you are put in command of a starship with a stowaway alien of unknown powers. It is up to you to capture, or if that is too dangerous, to slay the alien. The alien can be detected on your sensor screens, but many of the lab animals also escaped in the initial confusion, and your sensors cannot tell the difference between the life form readings. Under your command are the crew, with their special capabilities and skills. The alien is a deadly foe, and will metamorphose to more and more deadly forms as times goes on, so it is imperative to subdue it as soon as possible. As a final act of desperation, it is possible to self destruct the ship and escape in the shuttles.
In ALIEN, victory depends on careful planning and thought. Each crew member moves and acts separately, and only if you can learn to use your crew and their talents cleverly can you hope to defeat or capture the alien. While you attempt to capture lab animals, arm your crew, build weapons and search the ship, the alien will be stalking your crew and gaining strength. Good luck! (you will need it)

meta.name: Alien Typhoon
meta.desc: Alien Typhoon is an enhanced version of Apple Galaxian,with more aliens on screen. In essence it is a variant of the arcade game Galaxian. It is a shoot-'em-up where a formation of alien ships attack your lone starship at the bottom of the screen.  The aliens peel off from the formation and attack you in different patterns, depending on the color of the ship. The two ships at the top are the motherships and they bring an escort of two red aliens with them to guard them, but if you can shoot the escort and then the mothership, you'll score lots of points.

meta.name: Aliens
meta.desc: Activision's Aliens (1986) is a first-person tactical strategy game where the player remotely commands six Marines through a sprawling 255-room complex to locate and eliminate the Alien Queen. Acting as an operations commander, the player switches between characters in real time, using helmet-cam views and smart guns to navigate, fight, and survive. Each Marine occupies one room at a time and can scan in full 360°, requiring careful coordination and safe positioning before switching control. Doors can be sealed to slow enemies or destroyed to advance, while alien bio-mass must be eliminated to prevent facehugger spawns. Combat is risky—Aliens are drawn to gunfire and leave deadly acid pools when killed. Captured Marines can be rescued, but failure means permanent loss. Resources like ammo, health, and stamina are limited, requiring strategic trips to the armory and med bay, while maintaining power by protecting or repairing the facility's generator adds another layer of tension.

p: Alter Ego (1985)(Activision)(Disk 1 of 3 Side A)[cr Digital Gang].zip
n: Alter Ego (1985)(Activision)(Disk 1 of 3 Side A)[cr Digital Gang]

meta.path: ./Alter Ego (1985)(Activision)(Disk 1 of 3 Side A)[cr Digital Gang].zip
meta.name: Alter Ego
meta.desc:
Alter Ego is a game which tries to simulate life itself. The player creates a character with different personality statistics, either by assigning them randomly or by answering a few introductory questions. They then guide this character through seven life phases, from infancy to old age.
The gameplay consists of a series of situations where a decision has to be made. This is done by choosing one of multiple answer possibilities, sometimes accompanied with a mood. Then the player gets presented with the outcome and goes on to the next situation. Every decision has long-time consequences and influences the personality, health and which situations await further down the road. Some decisions may even result in an early death or instant game over.
Every life phase is represented by a life tree where the situations are lined up; the motive of the situation card shows the general theme of the situation. After reaching a certain age, additional life options can be undergone at any time. Here the player can change his lifestyle and manage his love and professional life.
The game exists in two variations which depict a male or female life.

p: Amazing Spider-Man, The v1.0 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)(Side A).zip
n: Amazing Spider-Man, The v1.0 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)(Side A)

meta.path: ./Amazing Spider-Man, The v1.0 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)(Side A).zip
meta.name: The Amazing Spider-Man
meta.desc:
The player takes the role of Spider-Man, with powers including super strength and the ability to climb walls. Unlike previous releases, this game was not limited to simple verb/noun input, it could accept complex sentences.
Spider-Man begins the game imprisoned in a skyscraper which he must explore. He encounters characters such as Sandman, Hydro-Man, and Ringmaster. The player must solve puzzles and collect gems. He also encounters Madame Web and Lizard.

meta.name: Andromeda Conquest
meta.desc: Elige tu raza y conquista el universo en este juego de guerra espacial en ASCII cuyas reglas están inspiradas en el juego de mesa Risk.

p: Ankh (1983)(Datamost)[cr Doctor Who - Merlin the Magician][48K].zip
n: Ankh (1983)(Datamost)[cr Doctor Who - Merlin the Magician][48K]

meta.name: Ankh
meta.desc: Ankh is a flick-screen arcade adventure where you control a Mindprobe in the "Metareal" world of Ankh. You have to explore the 64 room world to find a Treasure, Tool or a new mystery in the last room of the world. As you move around you will encounter various objects that move around the screen shooting at you but you are able to shoot back. There are doors that need opening by switches, other treasures to collect and objects that can be used elsewhere to help you in your quest. If you are shot, touch certain objects, touch walls or the doors then an energy bar decreases and when it is empty then it is game over.

meta.name: Ants
meta.desc: Mutant killer ants are gnawing at the underside of the earth's crust, trying to break through and invade our world.  Armed with your trusty electro-sonic disrupter, you must stop the ants from completing a passage up to the surface of the earth.  The Ant's leader, A dark figure amid a hell-like orange glare, may briefly appear.  Destroy her and the ants will wander off for a while, giving you a chance to repair the holes and passages dug by the ants.

meta.name: Apple Bowl
meta.desc: Played from a 1st person perspective at a bowling alley. A throw indicator scrolls slowly along the bottom of the screen. The player controls the power and spin of the ball using the game paddles, and the game attempts to accurately simulate the physics of bowling, and follows standard 10-pin bowling rules.

meta.name: Apple Cider Spider
meta.desc: Eres una araña en una sidrería y tienes que trepar para unirte a tus congéneres que están tres niveles más arriba. Deberá evitar la maquinaria de la sidra, así como los trozos de manzanas y los insectos que forman su curso. Este juego es quizás la alegoría del camino de una neurona en el cerebro de Steve Jobs después de tomar LSD.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Apple Cider Spider (1983)(Sierra On-Line)[48K]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Apple Graphics Games
meta.desc:
Apple Graphics Games are a series of 10 games with hi-res graphics designed to teach the player how to program for the Apple II. The series comes with a manual fully documenting the coding for each game.
There are 10 games in total:
Match - match 30 different pieces with the 20 on a board
Piano - The player creates a a song by typing notes onto sheet music.
Pairs - A matching card game played on a large 14 x 10 grid
Catch - The player moves horizontally to try to catch balls as they drop
Boxes - Two players compete by attempting to draw boxes on a grid
Poker - 2-player 5 card stud poker game
Equate - 2 player math game, search for 3 numbers that answer the problem A x B +- C =
Spiral - The player has the computer draw a spiral my entering drawing instructions and number of repetitions
Lander - A Lunar Lander clone, player tries to land on columns by controlling the thrust of their landing craft
Spider - Arcade game where player controls a fly attempting to eat spider eggs

meta.name: Apple Invader
meta.desc:
Micro Invaders recreates the classic Space Invaders formula with faithful, fast-paced arcade action. Players control a cannon along the bottom of the screen, moving horizontally to shoot upward at descending alien formations. Five rows of invaders advance while firing back, and players must eliminate them before they reach the bottom. Three alien types award different point values, while a rare flying saucer crosses the top for bonus points.
Defensive bunkers provide temporary cover but degrade from both enemy fire and the player's own shots, eventually leaving the cannon exposed. Only one bullet can be fired at a time, adding strategy and timing to each attack. Players start with three lives, earning an extra life at 1,500 points.
After each wave is cleared, a new one begins, increasing the challenge. With no true ending, the objective is simple: survive as long as possible and achieve the highest score.

meta.name: Apple Pairing
meta.desc: At the beginning of the game, there are 42 white boxes which totally contain 21 pairs of different letters.  Each letter corresponds to a score: A=300, B=200, C=190, D=180 and so on down to U=10.  You are suggested to overturn two boxes at each time.  If the two boxes contain the same letter, you will get the score of that letter.

meta.name: Apple Panic
meta.desc:
Apple Panic is a platform game based on the arcade game Space Panic (1980, Universal) and ported to the Apple II by Ben Serki. You control a digger, armed with his shovel, and you must kill your opponents - the evil legged apples.
Your character can only walk left/right and climb up/down the ladders - no jumping - and if the apples touch you, you'll lose.
To kill an apple you must dig a hole on the ground and wait for one of them to get stuck on it. Then you must hit it with your shovel to make it fall to the level below. The apples die if they fall deep enough. Depending on their color, they may require from 1 to 3-level falls, so you may have to dig several aligned holes. Besides, the deeper the fall, the higher you score.
Each level is timed so you must take out all the apples quickly, or you'll lose. If you're successful, the next levels will feature more and more enemies than the previous one.

meta.name: Apple Trek
meta.desc:
Apple Star Trek is a Star Trek variant for the first Apple computer. The player is tasked with shooting Klingons in a 64 quadrant large galaxy. Each quadrant is divided into 64 sectors. The player can use scanners (short and long range) to find the enemy and then engage them with phasers and photon torpedoes. Moving around the galaxy costs energy which can be replenished at star bases.
APPLE-TREK was a Star Trek game that appeared on many computers of this era, both mainframe and micro. Rather than “Klingons”, this game involved hunting “Klarnons”.

meta.name: Adventure to Atlantis
meta.desc:
Apventure to Atlantis continues Robert Clardy's early CRPG series, following Dungeon Campaign (1978), Wilderness Campaign (1979), and Odyssey: The Compleat Apventure (1980), each expanding gameplay from dungeon exploration to overworld and sea travel.
As a direct sequel to Odyssey, the game begins in the castle the player previously conquered. Its story builds on established lore: the Sargalo Sea was isolated by a wizard known as the High One to strengthen his people, but the barrier also trapped evil forces who eventually overthrew him. Like its predecessor, Apventure continues the narrative of reclaiming control from these usurpers while expanding the world and story.

p: Arcade Boot Camp (1984)(Besnard, John)[cr Black Bag].zip
n: Arcade Boot Camp (1984)(Besnard, John)[cr Black Bag]

meta.name: Arcade Boot Camp
meta.desc: You control a small helmet with legs aiming to improve his arcade skills by completing five mini-games, each awarding a military-style performance rank. In Driver Education, you steer along a fast, winding road. Survival Training challenges you to endure increasingly difficult environmental hazards while clearing each level. Chopper Practice tasks you with collecting numbered cargo in maze-like stages, avoiding enemy fire, managing fuel, and delivering loads to landing pads. Obstacle Course requires dodging bombs, rocks, and cannon fire while surviving to a count of ten, with bonus points for collecting flags in later stages. In Target Practice, you shoot targets within a time limit, with limited misses allowed, and are ranked based on accuracy and completion speed.

meta.name: Arcade Insanity
meta.desc:
Created using Brøderbund's The Arcade Machine
All three games involve flying a spaceship, and shooting a number of incoming alien vessels, which transform into a variety of strange shapes before being destroyed by your cannon fire. In all challenges, the player starts with 3 lives, but can gain extra ships. The three games are:
Alien Onslaught - Where the player controls a spaceship which can scroll left and right on the bottom of the screen. The player must fire vertically at the aliens as they invade from the top of the screen and fire upon the player.
Galactic Conquerors - In this game, the aliens will scroll vertically towards the player, and after falling off the bottom of the screen, will appear once again at the top, as they approach the player.
Andromeda Blitz - This time the player's spaceship is sandwiched between enemy craft on the top and bottom of the screen. The player can fire up or down to destroy alien vessels. The player must avoid retaliatory fire from both sides

meta.name: Ardy
meta.desc: Ardy the Aardvark is a maze-game inspired by, and a near clone of, the arcade game Anteater. The player navigates the tongue of the animal as it moves through an underground maze. The objective is to eat drops of food lying underground while trying to evade worms, stinging ants and spiders. While the worms can pass through the tongue and only cause aardvark?s death when being directly in touch with his tip of the tongue, other critters will instantly kill the player upon touching any part of the tongue. The lowest levels of the underground area usually have two special objects which clear out the whole screen from enemies if touched by Ardy?s tongue.

meta.name: Argos
meta.desc:
Argos is a shoot 'em up game made up of three stages, each with different gameplay. The player's task is to defend Earth from the alien race Znark who are leaving their doomed home planet Argos. In the first round the Argonians attack Earth's defense satellite from a base on Saturn. The player has to launch missiles from the satellite (which is located in the top left corner of the screen) and destroy all aliens as they swirl around the screen.
In the second round the task is to defend the last surviving city on earth which is being attacked by jet bombers. The player attacks them with two anti-jet guns on the ground and has to kill them off before the city's protecting force field is destroyed. In the third round the Argonians attack the city streets with missiles, bombs, spaceships and parachuters. The player controls a tank left and right and has to shoot missiles at the enemies.

meta.name: Arkanoid
meta.desc: Arkanoid es un juego de arcade desarrollado por Taito en 1986. Amplió los juegos Breakout de Atari de la década de 1970 añadiendo potenciadores, diferentes tipos de ladrillos, una variedad de niveles de tablero y profundidad visual. El título hace referencia a una "nave nodriza" condenada de la que escapa el barco del jugador, el Vaus. Arkanoid revivió el concepto de los juegos de romper ladrillos, generando numerosos clones y juegos similares para ordenadores personales incluso más de una década después.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Arkanoid (1988)(Taito America)(II+)(US)[cr Visual Eyes][t][64K]-manual.pdf

p: Armageddon (1986)(King Cruces Software)[cr Sinbad the Sailor].zip
n: Armageddon (1986)(King Cruces Software)[cr Sinbad the Sailor]

meta.name: Armageddon
meta.desc:
This is a space shoot 'em up along the lines of Galaxian. There are three lives and the objective is to achieve a high score.
The player takes the role of 'Terra Man', the sole survivor of mankind's galactic fleets. He must stop Ygothulu and his hordes of space demons from devouring earth by shooting waves of alien ships that scroll down the screen towards you. There is a boss at the top of the screen and this hides behind either earth or the moon. 
In the first level the space demons march down the screen in a manner similar to Space Invaders, all the player has to do is shoot them before being hit by one of them. In later levels the the player starts further up the screen and demons occasionally swoop come down and attack.

meta.name: Asteroids
meta.desc:
The Asteroid Field is a 1-player Asteroids clone for the Apple II
The player controls a small arrow-shaped spaceship which can rotate 360 degrees, and moves forwards via thrusters. Ship control is via keyboard or Apple Paddles. The player starts each level with a number of fast-moving asteroids, which when shot will break into two smaller pieces, and will third split a third time until destroyed. Intermittently, a small or large-sized flying saucer will enter the screen and fire randomly. This ship can be destroyed by player fire or by asteroids. The player is awarded points for asteroids or ships destroyed, and if the player destroys all objects on the screen, will advance to a faster moving level. The player starts with a total of 5 ships, and when all are lost, it's game over.

p: At the Gates of Moscow (1985)(Softgroup)(Side A)[cr Pete The Pirate].zip
n: At the Gates of Moscow (1985)(Softgroup)(Side A)[cr Pete The Pirate]

meta.path: ./At the Gates of Moscow (1985)(Softgroup)(Side A)[cr Pete The Pirate].zip
meta.name: At the Gates of Moscow
meta.desc:
At the Gates of Moscow 1941 is a turn-based division-level wargame set during the Operation Typhoon (World War 2), a German attempt to capture Russian capital. The two scenarios or the whole campaign (1 September 1941 - 6 March 1942) can be played from either German or Soviet side.
The game features variable weather and ground conditions (e.g. secondary roads made impassable due to mud), units hidden by "fog of war", intelligence reports, entrenchment, air power, railway transport, partisans and militia units (for Soviets). The hex-based map is displayed in either close-up or strategical scale.
This computer game can be also played alongside a board game (of the same name), released concurrently.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/At the Gates of Moscow (1985)(Softgroup)(Side A)[cr Pete The Pirate]-image.jpg

meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/At the Gates of Moscow (1985)(Softgroup)(Side A)[cr Pete The Pirate]-boxback.jpg

meta.name: AutoDuel
meta.desc:
Autoduel is a hybrid game based on Steve Jackson Games board game Car Wars. It incorporates elements of role-playing, driving and action-based combat. The game is set in a futuristic version of USA, where gangs and vigilantes rule the wilderness, and people's only protection are armored cars with mounted weaponry. The player is cast in the role of an average person who must at first earn enough money to buy an own car, and then perform courier missions throughout the country.
The game's progression is fairly open-ended: the player is free to visit different cities, explore highways, participate in car Arena battles to earn money, take on gangsters in the wilderness, salvage car parts, etc. A few courier missions must be completed in order to advance the story. The player can construct and customize vehicles using guns, mine-layers, smokescreens, oil slicks, rockets and other accessories, choosing between various body and chassis types for the car. The entire game is viewed from a top-down perspective.

meta.name: Aztec
meta.desc: Aztec is an Indiana Jones-type action game with adventure elements, featuring our hero's quest to recover a valuable Jade Idol from a maze. Armed with only three sticks of dynamite and a pistol, the hero must get by traps, animals (lots of snakes and panthers), and natives, and find a way to make it out alive.

meta.name: B-1 Nuclear Bomber
meta.desc:
B-1 Nuclear Bomber is a flight simulator game based on piloting a B-1 Lancer to its target and dropping a nuclear bomb. The USSR is one of the target countries. The game was developed by Avalon Hill and Microcomputer Games, Inc, and released in 1980 for the Apple II and other computers.
The game box details a sample scenario set in the then-future of a bombing run over Moscow on July 1, 1991, which turned out to be just months before the official dissolution of the Soviet Union on December 26 of that year.
In March 1983 B-1 Nuclear Bomber tied for eighth place in Softline's Dog of the Year awards "for badness in computer games", Atari 8-bit division, based on reader submissions. A 1992 Computer Gaming World survey of wargames with modern settings gave the game zero stars out of five, stating that "its play mechanics were embarrassing when it was initially released

p: Bad Dudes (1988)(Data East USA)(IIE)(Side A)[cr Blade][128K].zip
n: Bad Dudes (1988)(Data East USA)(IIE)(Side A)[cr Blade][128K]

meta.name: Bad Dudes
meta.desc:
La situación es grave: el Presidente de los Estados Unidos ha sido secuestrado por una temida organización terrorista: los Dragones Ninja.
Afortunadamente, el FBI ha encontrado a dos matones de artes marciales que se enfrentarán con sus propias manos a un ejército de ninjas para rescatar al presidente. Estos matones se convierten en la esperanza de la nación y tú, por supuesto, eres quien los interpreta.
Tendrás que cruzar la ciudad: el viaje no será fácil... En los callejones, en un enorme camión de mudanzas, en las alcantarillas o en el parque, la lucha es despiadada. El terreno está dividido en dos planos, y basta un simple salto para pasar de un plano al otro. Al final de cada nivel, por supuesto, un jefe intenta bloquear tu camino. Tendrás que encontrar la táctica ideal para triunfar cada vez. Al final de tu viaje, te encontrarás con el gran jefe que está a punto de despegar en helicóptero con el presidente en su equipaje.

meta.name: Ballblazer
meta.desc: En el año 3037, el deporte más competitivo del universo conocido es Ballblazer. Ballblazer es un juego de fútbol futurista en 3D, en el que el jugador (dentro de un Rotofoil) se sitúa en un campo de juego individual de 1155 casillas (la cuadrícula) presentado en una perspectiva en primera persona. El objetivo del jugador es lanzar una pelota flotante (Plasmorb) a la portería contraria (Goalbeams).  Un jugador puede competir con un oponente humano o con el ordenador. Se puede jugar contra el ordenador o contra otro humano.

p: Bandits (1982)(Sirius Software)[cr Nameless Cracker - Pirate Treck - Krakowicz][t +1].zip
n: Bandits (1982)(Sirius Software)[cr Nameless Cracker - Pirate Treck - Krakowicz][t +1]

meta.path: ./Bandits (1982)(Sirius Software)[cr Nameless Cracker - Pirate Treck - Krakowicz][t +1].zip
meta.name: Bandits
meta.desc:
Your job is to guard the supplies at the lunar base. Bandits of various sorts arrive in waves and descend, trying to get the goods. Armed with  a mobile laser gun and some shielding, you score points by blasting the bandits.
Bandits has 28 levels of play. You begin with 5 ships and acquire a bonus ship for every 5,000 points. The game is similar to Space Invaders.

n: Baron - The Real Estate Simulation v1.0 (1983)(Blue Chip Software)[cr Whip]

meta.name: Baron - The Real Estate Simulation v1.0 (1983)(Blue Chip Software)[cr Whip]
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Baron - The Real Estate Simulation v1.0 (1983)(Blue Chip Software)[cr Whip]-marquee.png

meta.name: Baseball Strategy
meta.desc: Computer Baseball Strategy is an adaptation of Avalon Hill's board game Baseball Strategy. The player is the manager of an unnamed home team and plays single matches against the visitor's team that is managed by the computer. When starting out, the player first selects the team's lineup by picking a pitcher and eight fielders. None of the players are named, all that is shown is a jersey number and some statistics. Once the team has been picked, the player gets to select the batting order. When all is done, the match begins, with the player in each inning being in the field in the first half and at bat in the second half. The gameplay works the same in both, with the player selecting which pitch or swing to use in each turn. The pitches and swings allowed depends on the pitching ability or batting average of the players.

meta.name: Batatlan
meta.desc:
Batatlan (Battle of the Atlantic – The Ocean Lifeline) is a naval strategy game. The game features five scenarios: Germany Attacks, England Alone, The Golden Days, Foreboding Horizons, and Victory at Sea, each with its own ending date.
Each turn represents a 14-day period. At the end of every two weeks, players may issue new commands to their fleet. Fleet size varies depending on the scenario selected. During each turn, orders can be assigned from the following commands: Patrol, Barrier, Join Group, Enter Escort Pool, Escort Convoy, Change Base, Rest in Port, and Become Group Leader.
Once orders have been issued, the scenario unfolds over the 14-day span, after which the computer provides an intelligence report detailing all major events and actions at sea. When the turn is complete, players are given another opportunity to issue new orders in response to the threats & developments described in the intelligence report. The game ends when it reaches the predefined completion date.

p: Batman - The Caped Crusader (1988)(Data East)(IIE)(Side A)[cr Blade][128K].zip
n: Batman - The Caped Crusader (1988)(Data East)(IIE)(Side A)[cr Blade][128K]

meta.path: ./Batman - The Caped Crusader (1988)(Data East)(IIE)(Side A)[cr Blade][128K].zip
meta.name: Batman
meta.desc:
The TurboGrafx-16 version of Batman: The Video Game is a top-down maze-like game, somewhat similar to Pac-Man or Bomberman.
The game consists of five stages based on the movie: the Streets of Gotham, Flugelheim Museum, AXIS Chemical Factory, the Parade and the Cathedral, where Batman finally faces the Joker. The first four stages are divided into 12 sub-stages or mazes. Each main level has its own objective, which basically consists of finding items in the mazes: collecting "smilex" components in the streets of Gotham, cleaning paintings at the museum, planting bombs at the factory and cutting off the ropes of Joker's balloons at the parade. Before each level, a cutscene with scenes from the movie is displayed.
Batman can attack his enemies with his Batarang. Through the levels, he will collect items which will upgrade his speed and give his Batarang a longer range and multiple shots.

meta.name: Battle of Hoth
meta.desc:
Battle of Hoth is a Star Wars themed 1-player arcade game for the Apple II.
Three columns of Imperial AT-AT walkers walk inexorably from the right of the screen towards the Rebel defensive positions on the left. The player controls a Rebel snowspeeder, which travels from the left to right of the screen, and can fire horizontally to the right. The player can move the snowspeeder vertically up and down, and must avoid fire from the AT-ATs and shoot them down. Whenever an AT-AT is destroyed, a new AT-AT immediately appears to the right of the screen in that row. The player's goal is to survive for 300 seconds to allow for the evacuation of Rebel forces. If an Imperial vessel reaches the left of the screen, the Rebel forces are routed, and the player loses the game. The player starts with a total of 9 snowspeeders, and if all are lost to Imperial fire, it's game over.

p: Battle of the Parthian Kings (1983)(Avalon Hill).zip
n: Battle of the Parthian Kings (1983)(Avalon Hill)

meta.name: Battle of the Parthian Kings
meta.desc: Parthian Kings is a role-playing game for the Apple II, which can be played by 1-4 people. It was published by Avalon Hill.The Emperor is dead and the Parthian Empire is in chaos. Every city state in the empire has declared independence as their Kings prepare to crush all opposition and be proclaimed Emperor. You as a King command an army, hire mercenaries, make alliances and levy taxes. Your strategy, tactics and leadership will gain you an empire. City states that you have conquered must be held as you make your thrust towards final victory. Up to 4 players can play at any one time or you can play against the computer as any or all your opponents.

meta.name: Battleship
meta.desc:
This is a shareware version of the classic board game Battleship by Milton Bradley.
Two players have a grid of boxes. You use your mouse to decide where your ships should go, and compete to try to find and sink your opponents ships.
There is a win record menu that tells how many time you won and lost or drew. Options include whether the game has a 10 x 10 or 15 x 15 board, and allow you to choose between a single shot or salvo shooting, and whether you can use Cluster bombs, Cruise missiles and Smart Bombs, and you can use Ship resources or Fleet resources. There are Beginner, Intermediate or Expert skill levels,  and you can decide who goes first, with the option of this being decided randomly.

meta.name: Battlesight
meta.desc:
WWIII has begun. At 0530 hours, the Warsaw Pact breached the West German border. From the high ground between Fulda and Schlitz, you command five M60A3 tanks tasked with stopping the Red Army.
The enemy utilizes T-62 and T-72 tanks, supported by reconnaissance aircraft and HIND-A helicopters. Across five difficulty levels—including night missions where units are visible only when firing—you must use a joystick-controlled crosshair to eliminate the advancing armor.
Your unit remains hidden until you fire or an enemy plane spots you. You must destroy as many tanks as possible while avoiding anti-tank missiles. Your platoon can survive five direct hits, though minor damage requires repairs. The game ends if all five of your tanks are destroyed or if five enemy tanks bypass your position and reach your rear.

meta.name: Beast War
meta.desc:
Beast War is for 1-4 players, however you always play against the computer. You direct the movement of six different semi-intelligent beasts on a circular, 43 space field; Just like the Star Wars chess scene on the Millenium Falcon. In each round of a battle, your goal is to max your score by destroying opposing beasts and capturing squares. 
Each beast has its own movement pattern, energy level, strength, and unique advantage over one other beast. (Dracon can burn the winged Aeroctus to cinders but fears the bite of the Saurus.)  Any move consumes energy, so it is a resource you need to manage closely. Combats are shown in a small action sequence where each beast may advance, strike, or retreat.

p: Below the Root (1984)(Windham Classics)(II+)(Side A)[cr Mr. Clean][48K].zip
n: Below the Root (1984)(Windham Classics)(II+)(Side A)[cr Mr. Clean][48K]

meta.path: ./Below the Root (1984)(Windham Classics)(II+)(Side A)[cr Mr. Clean][48K].zip
meta.name: Below the Root
meta.desc:
Below the Root is based on Zilpha Keatley Snyder's fantasy book series "The Green-Sky Trilogy". Following the events in those books, The Kindar and the Erdling societies have joined together. However unrest and distrust still remains. An old woman, D'ol Falla has heard the words "The Spirit fades, in Darkness lying. A quest proclaim, the Light is dying". Choosing one of five available people (of different ages and of either Kindar or Erdling descent) either she believes can help, D'ol Falla assigns the quest of discovering what these words mean and how to quell the racial and political tensions building between the races.
Below the Root is a adventure game and a platform game. Choosing one of five characters (each very different from one another), the player explores the tree-based lands Erdling and the underground-based lands of the Kindar. As an adventure game, goals are primarily accomplished by talking to people (though each character is treated differently by others) and finding the right items to use in specific situations, but also dealing with the challenges of level design in a platform game.
Each character can obtain different items to help with their movement as well as psychic abilities such as pensing (telepathy), kiniporting (telekinesis) and grunspreking (plant manipulation). Violence is abhorred among the Kindar and the Erdling and violent actions tend to harm the player's reputation. Kindar or Erdling factions may kidnap the player's character if they wander into the wrong areas, throwing them into a local "prison house" from which they must escape. The player must eat constantly to retain their energy, however Erdling characters do not eat meat.

meta.name: Beneath the Pyramids
meta.desc:
Beneath the Pyramids is a single-player adventure/RPG set in ancient Egypt. In 1932, you are sent by the British Museum to Giza to recover a priceless artifact rumored to rest in a hidden golden chamber within the Great Pyramid—a solid gold statue of the cat goddess Bast. Many have searched before you and failed. By night, the undead roam the freezing stone passages beneath the sands.
At the start, you must purchase supplies for the expedition. Weapons like rifles or crossbows are available, along with essential gear such as lanterns, matches, rope, grapples, pickaxes, bandages, and wool coats. Choices are limited by weight and cost, and you cannot return to the bazaar—though the Apple II version allows bartering to save coins.
Your objective is to explore chambers, collect clues, solve puzzles, battle monsters, avoid traps, and uncover hidden treasures deep within the pyramid.

meta.name: The Bermuda Race
meta.desc: Bermuda Race is a challenging simulation of the blue-water sailboat race from Newport, Rhode Island to Bermuda, some 635 miles across the open Atlantic. You're racing against those who set the actual Bermuda Race record and also against the top previous simulation players. To sail your best, keep a steady hand in control of your heading, sails, and centerboard, but watch out for the weather and the unexpected! By effective use of the highly precise information supplied by the computer, you may win a place in the Player's Hall Of Fame! For 1 or 2 players, beginner to expert, Bermuda Race is not only fun, it's educational. Interactive tutorials help you learn everything you need to know about sailing with this simulation. You can chart your progress on high-quality video screens that show each leg of the race.

meta.name: Beyond Beyond Castle Wolfenstein
meta.desc: This isn't actually a game, but rather an animated production created on the Apple II computer inspired by Beyond Castle Wolfenstein. The story follows the hero from Castle Wolfenstein.  After he is captured, he calls for help using his Von Fone Kall. Answering the call are the Rescue Raiders — a team of video game mercenaries made up of characters from numerous classic games, including a Pac-Man ghost, Choplifter, Joust, Space Invaders, Drol, and many others. In the end, the hero is rescued by a Ghostbuster ghost, while the castle itself is obliterated by a laser blast from the Starship Enterprise.  In total, it's an 8 1/2-minute animation short.

p: Beyond Castle Wolfenstein (1984-12-07)(MUSE Software)[cr Stainless Steel Rat][48K][no boot].zip
n: Beyond Castle Wolfenstein (1984-12-07)(MUSE Software)[cr Stainless Steel Rat][48K][no boot]

meta.path: ./Beyond Castle Wolfenstein (1984-12-07)(MUSE Software)[cr Stainless Steel Rat][48K][no boot].zip
meta.name: Beyond Castle Wolfenstein
meta.desc: Your mission is to infiltrate Nazi headquarters in disguise, find the bomb agents have hidden there, and plant it outside the door to Hitler's bunker.  If you go in shooting everything in sight, a guard is bound to raise the alarm and it's unlikely you'll complete the mission.  Use stealth and the occassional bribe to work your way to Hitler.  You might have to kill a few guards, but think before you do.  Oh, and did we mention you have to escape the way you came before the bomb explodes?

meta.name: Beyond Pinball
meta.desc: The player controls a either pinball paddle which spins in a 360 degree circle or a bumper that can be intermittently solid or transparent to control the ball's motion. This is used to direct a bouncing ball through a number of screens. Each screen has a number of small white bumpers which move about the level, and there are spaces in the wall that allow the pinball to move to another screen. The ultimate goal is to move the pinball through the maze and find the goal. The program contains a special lesson mode to teach the player how to play, and there is also a demonstration mode to watch the computer play. There are a total of 4 courses of increasing difficulty, and the goal is to minimize the number of bounces or time to complete the course to maximize score.

meta.name: The Bilestoad
meta.desc: In The Bilestoad, players control "meatlings" that hack and battle with axes and shields from a top-view perspective. The name is derived from the German words Beil (axe) and Tod (death). The odd spelling reflects Goodman's idea of a future language similar to A Clockwork Orange's Nadsat in which English has been modified by the borrowing of foreign words. Although the game may seem medieval, the backstory in the manual explains that the axe fighting is actually a future virtual reality game designed to reduce real violence.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Bilestoad, The (1983)(Datamost)[cr Dirty Dozen][48K]-manual.pdf

p: Black Belt (1984)(Earthware Computer Services)[cr Syndicate - Ware Lords - 202 Alliance].zip
n: Black Belt (1984)(Earthware Computer Services)[cr Syndicate - Ware Lords - 202 Alliance]

meta.path: ./Black Belt (1984)(Earthware Computer Services)[cr Syndicate - Ware Lords - 202 Alliance].zip
meta.name: Black Belt
meta.desc:
Black Belt is a 1-player vs. computer or 2-player Taekwon-Do sparring simulator.
The game closely follows official Taekwon-Do sparring rules. Matches feature adjustable computer opponents with varying speed, fatigue, form, and intelligence. Move availability is tied to skill level/belt rank—higher ranks unlock more advanced techniques. Players can perform 9 different kicks, 3 punches, blocks, and move in 4 directions.
A roster of multiple characters is available. As players defeat tougher opponents, they advance to higher belt ranks. Points are awarded for successful attacks and blocks. Warnings are issued for leaving the mat, turning your back on the opponent, illegal blows, or stalling. Every two warnings deduct a point; 6 warnings result in disqualification. Fighters can be knocked down and must rise within 10 seconds or lose the match. The player with the most points at the end wins.

meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Black Belt (1984)(Earthware Computer Services)[cr Syndicate - Ware Lords - 202 Alliance]-boxback.jpg

meta.name: The Black Sage
meta.desc:
The Black Sage is a humorous fantasy RPG that combines multiple-choice adventure gameplay with character development and combat. Trapped inside the Black Sage's castle, the player must explore 36 interconnected rooms in search of a way to escape. Before beginning, players generate a character and equip weapons, armor, shields, and magical spells.
Each room presents a unique encounter with several choices, ranging from clever solutions to instant death. Success often depends on character attributes, while some situations lead to turn-based combat where equipment and statistics determine survival. As players explore, they can gain gold, better gear, and improved abilities. The castle's layout remains fixed, allowing strategic exploration and multiple routes to freedom, including magical and unconventional escapes. Players may even encounter the mysterious Black Sage himself. Escaped characters can be saved for use in planned but unreleased future adventures.

meta.name: Blacksmith Market Falcons
meta.desc:
Blacksmith Market Falcons is a fixed shooter inspired by arcade classics like Phoenix. The player controls a spaceship at the bottom of the screen, moving left and right while firing upward at waves of enemies descending from above.
Enemies attack with missiles, diving charges, and other hazards, forcing the player to dodge carefully. A temporary force field can be activated for protection, but it only lasts a few seconds, so timing is essential.
The game features five repeating stages, with the fifth stage culminating in a battle against a large enemy spaceship. After completing the sequence, the stages loop endlessly with increasing challenge. Controls include paddle, keyboard, joystick, and Apple III keyboard options.

p: Blade of Blackpoole, The (1982)(Sirius Software)(Side A)[cr Trystan II][48K].zip
n: Blade of Blackpoole, The (1982)(Sirius Software)(Side A)[cr Trystan II][48K]

meta.path: ./Blade of Blackpoole, The (1982)(Sirius Software)(Side A)[cr Trystan II][48K].zip
meta.name: The Blade of Blackpoole
meta.desc: The Blade of Blackpoole is an illustrated text adventure with a medieval fantasy setting. Starting with only a pocket full of gold coins, the player's task is to recover the magical sword Myraglym, last seen near the lake at Blackpoole. On the way to the goal, there will be large forests, tricky swamps, a dangerous maze and high cliffs. Deadly creatures also abound, including a talking carnivorous plant, an aggressive lizardman and a hungry water beast. Some helpful characters may also be in the area.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Blade of Blackpoole, The (1982)(Sirius Software)(Side A)[cr Trystan II][48K]-image.jpg

meta.name: Blitzkrieg
meta.desc:
Blitzkrieg is a real-time strategy game with three campaigns during World War 2. During the campaigns the player keeps its core army of tanks and guns. In the missions there are additionally units provided by the game, including infantry and air units. There is a description of all units in the game, even if the units cannot be commanded by the player. The three campaigns are:
Allies (US, UK, French) fighting in Europe and Africa
German fighting in Europe, Africa and Russia
Soviet Union fighting in Russia and afterwards in Europe
The campaigns include historical battles like the battle of Kursk and optional bonus missions, which can be played to:
Get experience for the profile of the player to increase the rank of the profile. A higher rank leads to a larger core army.
Get experience for the units in the core army, so that the units become deadlier.
Exchange weapons to newer types.
During a mission the losses are never replaced, losses in the core army are replaced after missions. For the repairing and rearming of units depots have to be conquered, where the service trucks load their resources.  All depots have unlimited resources. 
The game itself has a 2D graphic, but uses a 3D terrain with a full destructible environment. Buildings can be occupied by infantry to use them as cover. The game includes maps for multiplayer matches over LAN or the internet.

meta.name: Blood Quest
meta.desc:
Blood Quest is a parody graphical adventure that mocks classic RPG tropes. Set in a vaguely medieval world plagued by famine and chaos, the game claims you seek revenge for your Great Aunt Phoebe's death at the hands of Einar the Off—but none of that actually occurs.
Gameplay begins with character creation, where meaningless stats are generated before you inevitably become a 335-pound female dwarf amazon. You start in an invisible maze of identical rooms where movement leads nowhere, eventually depositing you in a desert. Wandering aimlessly, you encounter defenseless enemies that are effortlessly defeated.
No matter which direction you travel, events repeat: you lose to a lizardman, get crushed by a giant chicken, and are resurrected each time. Ultimately, you are slain by a zombie—and somehow, that counts as winning the game.

meta.name: Bolo
meta.desc:
Bolo is a "Breakout" clone with a twist. You have to destroy all the bricks on the screen using your paddle to go to the next level but, in Bolo, your paddle is not restricted to the bottom of the screen -- it can move all around the screen, giving a whole new dimension to the "breakout" concept.
The game includes two different sets of 50 levels each, randomly selected when you begin a new game. It also features 46 different types of bricks with different properties, bonuses and penalties. 
The main menu allows you to access Training mode, from which you can select to play any of the 100 available layouts.
Bolo's graphics are black and white only, but the game can be played either on a monochrome or color monitor. The graphics are however much sharper on a monochrome monitor. The animations are fast and smooth, and the paddle reacts quickly to the mouse movements.

meta.name: Bongo's Bash
meta.desc: You are Bongo the gorilla and you're being pursued by evil robots. Your job is to collect the coconuts and leave the maze while avoiding the robots or dropping trees in their path leading them to a fiery demise.  Very much like Pac Man.

p: Bons Baisers d'Outre Tombe (19xx)(Herpin, Timothee - Gisselbrecht, Oliver)(FR)(Side A).zip
n: Bons Baisers d'Outre Tombe (19xx)(Herpin, Timothee - Gisselbrecht, Oliver)(FR)(Side A)

meta.path: ./Bons Baisers d'Outre Tombe (19xx)(Herpin, Timothee - Gisselbrecht, Oliver)(FR)(Side A).zip
meta.name: Bons Baisers d'Outre Tombe
meta.desc: Based on the 1963 French youth novel of the same name. The famous Vranstock manor trio embarks on a greeting card company, the company "Good kisses du vaborau". But what kind of card should be sent to an ex-girlfriend who threatens to publish Ignace Bronchon's love letters? And who should we write when the city of Livid City is overwhelmed by a wave of crimes and two escaped prisoners are hiding there? If you are Adele I. Vranstock, you will send a card to your old butler, T. Leeves, who will arrive just in time for tea... and trouble.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Bons Baisers d'Outre Tombe (19xx)(Herpin, Timothee - Gisselbrecht, Oliver)(FR)(Side A)-image.jpg
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Bons Baisers d'Outre Tombe (19xx)(Herpin, Timothee - Gisselbrecht, Oliver)(FR)(Side A)-marquee.png

meta.name: Borg
meta.desc:
Can you out run and out shoot the dragon's henchmen? Watch out for the wrath of the dragon if you do.
Borg is a slightly more complex version of Stern's Berzerk arcade game. The player navigates a number of one-screen mazes, attempting to shoot all enemies while avoiding enemy fire and touching the walls. If it takes too long, the evil Grud will yell, "BORG!" and chief dragon Borg will come out, yell "RUN!", and hunt and kill the player, just like Evil Otto in Berzerk.

meta.name: Borrowed Time
meta.desc:
Encarnas a un detective privado con ocho casos de diversa importancia, que puedes conocer leyendo los expedientes correspondientes. Una llamada anónima altera tu rutina diaria: ¡te avisan de que alguien intenta matarte! Hay veinte personas que pueden enfadarse lo suficiente como para decidir matarte.
En este juego tienes que escribir combinaciones de verbos y sustantivos para realizar una acción. Los lugares se describen en texto y se presentan mediante imágenes, estas últimas a menudo proporcionan información adicional no incluida en la descripción del texto.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Borrowed Time (1985)(Activision)(II+)(Side A)[cr First Class][64K]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Both Barrels
meta.desc:
Both Barrels contains two shooting games: High Noon and Duck Hunt.
High Noon is set in the old west and involves a shootout outside a saloon. Enemies will pop out from covers, doors and windows and the player simply has to shoot them before they shoot. The player is equipped with two revolvers and each is aimed and shoot individually using paddles.
Duck Hunt is a two player game that is played with just a single button. It's viewed from the side where the shooters are located on the ground while ducks fly across the screen above them. Each shooter fires from its own angle and all the player has to do is time the shot. The player with most hits after ten rounds wins the game.

meta.name: Boulder Dash
meta.desc: Eres Rockford, y tienes que cavar a través de cuevas infestadas de monstruos en busca de diamantes. En cada nivel debes reunir un número concreto de diamantes para abrir un portal al siguiente nivel. Los enemigos pueden ser aplastados por la caída de rocas, que quedan sueltas cuando se quita el suelo debajo de ellas o se empujan sobre un espacio vacío, pero ten cuidado porque tambien pueden aplastarte a ti. En los niveles más avanzados, la dificultad aumenta añadiendo más rompecabezas y límites de tiempo más cortos.

meta.name: Boulder Dash II
meta.desc: En la segunda parte de la serie Boulder Dash, el diseño general del juego no ha cambiado respecto al original, pero se han creado dieciséis nuevos niveles. La base del juego es la misma, sigues teniendo que extraer diamantes mientras evitas las rocas que caen y los monstruos de las cuevas de todo tipo. Al igual que en la primera parte, los niveles posteriores se vuelven más difíciles porque se han añadido muchos elementos de puzzle.

meta.name: Box World
meta.desc: BoxWorld, the sequel to MazezaM, is a puzzle game where the player has to rearrange boxes in each level in order to be able to teleport to the next level. The game is based on the classic Soko-Ban genre with a twist: new types of boxes and terrains. The latest version includes more than 525 puzzles.

meta.name: Break the Bank!
meta.desc:
Break the Bank! is a blackjack teaching tool and simulator.
Designed to teach players techniques for improving their skill at blackjack, the program offers tutorials in four levels of blackjack play, from best play without card counting to advanced card counting techniques. The program contains drills and flashcards designed to improve the player's blackjack play. The player can also input their own strategies, and see how they compete against the strategies in game.
Finally, there is an actual graphical animated blackjack game, where the player competes against the computer to put their blackjack skills into practice. Game rules follow typical Las Vegas blackjack play.

meta.name: Breakout
meta.desc:
In this updated version of Breakout, players take the role of "Bouncer", whose main objective is to rescue both his friends and Daisy and defeat the evil Batnix. 
Gameplay is similar to the original game from 1977, but with 3D graphics and multiplayer support. Play through exotic locations like a dark prison cell, a menacing dragon-owned castle, an animal infested farm, and outer space. The game also contains numerous challenging modes, secrets and bonuses.

meta.name: Bridge Challenger
meta.desc:
Bridge Challenger is a 1-player simulation of the card game bridge.
A game designed to allow a player to train their bridge skill. It is entirely text based, and all commands are inputted via text prompt. The computer deals at least 10 million different hands. The player and dummy player compete against the computer in regular contract bridge. Or the player can use the dealer subprogram to specify a criterion for high card points and Bridge Challenger will deal four new hands. The player can create customized hands to practice and can save specific hands for future replay. This even allows Bridge Partner to test itself against the bridge game from the local newspaper.

meta.name: Brimstone
meta.desc:
This real time all-text tale puts you in the role of Sir Gawain, a knight of the Round Table. It takes place in a dream that traps you in the underworld of Ulro, where you must learn five magic words in order to escape. The most unusual aspect of this game is probably the perspective: the story is described in the third person, with Gawain referred to by name, unlike most other text adventures that use the second person, referring to your character as 'he'.
The hardbound book that comes with the game includes a novella-length introduction to the plot, which is identified as the first chapters of the story, into which you jump when you commence game play.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Brimstone (1985)(Synapse Software)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)-manual.pdf

meta.name: Broadsides
meta.desc:
Broadsides is a tactical simulation of ship-to-ship combat between sailing ships of the Napoleonic era (late 18th and early 19th Century). The game can be played as two different games. The first game is an arcade mode where you can sail right into the action, set your course, maneuver, and fire your guns as fast as your crew can load. For authenticity and historical detail, the tactical game puts you on the quarterdeck where you will make all the decisions real captains had to make.
 If you wish to depart from the historical ships provided, you can design your own frigates and ships of the line from the keel up to rigging, arming and manning them. The game ends when either ship wins or at 6pm, whichever comes first. You will win if you sink the enemy ship, or accumulate enough points to force the enemy ship to surrender. You will lose if your crew size goes below one quarter of its original size or if 11 enemy crew members manage to board your ship and there are no standing friendly crew members occupying any of the first 10 boarding positions on your ship. If both ships sink, or no one wins by 6pm, the game is a draw. The game can be played by two players or against the computer.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Broadsides (1983)(SSI)[cr Ace]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Bronze Dragon
meta.desc: Commonwealth's first game, Bronze Dragon: Conquest of Infinity is a detailed text-based role-playing game. It comes with one full-length scenario called Seekers of the Storm, and twelve make-em-yourself castle adventures where the player chooses how levels and rooms per level it should have, how difficult it is, and how powerful the monsters to be.

meta.name: Bronze Dragon Side II
meta.desc:
Dragon Side II: The Twisted Speare is a set of 10 add-on scenarios for the text-based RPG Bronze Dragon: Conquest of Infinity. Gameplay is menu-driven, allowing players to create characters, form parties, build castles, or begin adventures. Characters can be Human, Elf, Dwarf, or Halfling, with classes including Knight, Assassin, Ninja, Elder, and Wizard. Stats include Strength, Agility, Intelligence, Constitution, and Endurance, along with limited hit points and currency.
Each scenario begins at the Twisted Speare tavern, where quests are assigned, while supplies must be obtained from the main game's Dragon Village. Players also design castles by choosing room counts and levels.
Combat is turn-based, offering actions like attacking, moving, searching, using items, or fleeing. Players can detect traps, find secrets, and loot enemies mid-combat. Success in battle depends on range, weapon type, agility bonuses, and dice rolls exceeding enemy defense.

p: Bubble-Head (1983)(Tellus Systems)[t +1 Hi-Res Hijackers - Ware Lords].zip
n: Bubble-Head (1983)(Tellus Systems)[t +1 Hi-Res Hijackers - Ware Lords]

meta.name: Bubble-Head
meta.desc:
Bubble-Head is a 1-player arcade game for the Apple II.
The player controls the aforementioned Bubble-Head, who moves in the 4 cardinal directions within a maze. A number of of brown tinged creatures run about the maze trying to avoid the player. When the player catches a brown creature, it turns blue and begins to hunt the player costing them a life if caught. The goal is to avoid the blue creatures while turning all the brown creatures on the stage. The maze includes blue and brown doors which can only be travelled by the correct colored monster. There are also dotted doors which only the player can cross. A bonus counter ticks down as the level progresses, with the player receiving more points for finishing a level quickly. The player starts with 5 lives, and when all are lost, it's game over.

p: Buck Rogers - Planet of Zoom (1984)(Sega)[cr Apple Bandit - Syndicate][48K].zip
n: Buck Rogers - Planet of Zoom (1984)(Sega)[cr Apple Bandit - Syndicate][48K]

meta.path: ./Buck Rogers - Planet of Zoom (1984)(Sega)[cr Apple Bandit - Syndicate][48K].zip
meta.name: Buck Rogers : Planet Of Zoom
meta.desc: Buck Rogers: Planet of Zoom es una carrera de obstáculos de ciencia ficción para un solo jugador, donde el jugador controla la nave espacial de Buck Rogers y debe guiarla a través de múltiples pares de pilones a lo largo del juego. Controlar la nave implica desplazarse lateralmente hacia la izquierda o hacia la derecha, detenerse, avanzar (y más rápido), y retroceder/frenar (y más lento). Además, la nave espacial también puede disparar su arma para liberar un par de rayos de energía.

meta.name: Bug Attack
meta.desc: Bug Attack es un colorido shoot-em-up donde eres asaltado por milpiés que lanzan dagas, hormigas y moscas de la fruta. El juego se parece al juego arcade Centipede, pero se juega de manera bastante diferente. Si no las disparas lo suficientemente rápido, comienzan a separarse del grupo, bailando salvajemente y haciendo llover dagas sobre ti.

meta.name: Calc-Man
meta.desc:
Calc-Man is a combination of Pac-Man and business software.
The player controls a flashing square on a 9 x 20 grid. The player is chased by four negative flashing numbers within a maze, and must attempt to gather all the numbers within. A number "50" located in each corner will temporarily make the flashing numbers vulnerable. If the player collects all numbers within the maze, they start a level where the attacking digits move faster.

p: California Raisins and the Cereal Factory (1988)(Box Office Software)(Side A).zip
n: California Raisins and the Cereal Factory (1988)(Box Office Software)(Side A)

meta.path: ./California Raisins and the Cereal Factory (1988)(Box Office Software)(Side A).zip
meta.name: The California Raisins
meta.desc: You control Tiny Goodbite, one of the California Raisins. All of your friends have been captured and brought to the cereal factory, and you need to come to the rescue! In this action platform game, you need to climb and jump your way through the many floors of the factory to rescue the raisins. On each level there is a key which needs to be collected so you can unlock the door to the next level. Each level is guarded by various bad guys which should be avoided; your raisin may also become energized and shoot the bad guys after landing on the green platform. Be careful when jumping around; if you fall too far or land on some machinery, your raisin will lose a life! The game ends when you have lost all of your raisins.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/California Raisins and the Cereal Factory (1988)(Box Office Software)(Side A)-image.jpg
meta.video: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/videos/California Raisins and the Cereal Factory (1988)(Box Office Software)(Side A)-video.mp4

meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/California Raisins and the Cereal Factory (1988)(Box Office Software)(Side A)-boxback.jpg

meta.name: Cannon Ball Blitz
meta.desc: The player has to reach the flag on the top. The guard sends the cannonballs down, so you must jump over them. Cannonballs roll on the floors which have hatches, that open randomly. Also cannonballs fall on the swings, so you have to use them for climbing to the upper floor. Finally you must catch the balloon to make your way up to the flag and to the next level. Moving options available are up, down, left, right and jump.

p: Captain Cuckoo (1984)(Kurt and Yan Software)(Side A).zip
n: Captain Cuckoo (1984)(Kurt and Yan Software)(Side A)

meta.name: Captain Cuckoo
meta.desc:
Captain Cuckoo is a graphical adventure game for the Apple II.
The player controls the eponymous Captain Cuckoo, as he attempts to become a secret agent. Gameplay is via text commands via VERB NOUN entries, and the scenes the character is in are graphically displayed. In game, the player must find something to eat at the bar. They then must discovery some money on the side of the road to buy a special elixir, which is drank to get a special letter from a spy. This letter can be given to the chief spy whom makes you a super hero.

meta.name: Captain Power
meta.desc:
Captain Power and the Soldiers of the Future is a 2D space shooter.
Based on the '87 TV series Captain Power and the Soldiers of the Future, this computer game of the same name sends you into the year 2147. Earth is a wasteland, and the evil Lord Dread rules with an iron hand. Only a small group of high-tech soldiers under the command of the charismatic Captain Power battles the nemesis and his band of Bio Dread henchmen.

meta.name: Card Sharks
meta.desc:
Based on a popular television show at the time it was released, Card Sharks is a question and answer guessing game in which you compete against a computer player or another "live person".
Questions are asked based upon a poll of 100 people. You must guess how many out of 100 answered Yes or No.  Your opponent then has the chance to guess whether the true answer is higher or lower than your answer.  If you are right, you are taken to the "Cards" screen to play a video version of the "High Low" card game. Guessing correctly lets you keep control of the board, and the next question.  If you lose, your opponent receives the next question.  If you win enough times, you'll go to the "Big Bucks" card screen where you can bet all or part of your winnings on a more elaborate board of "High Low".

p: Card Shark II (1985)(Zelman, Paul M.)(Side A).zip
n: Card Shark II (1985)(Zelman, Paul M.)(Side A)

meta.name: Card Shark II
meta.desc:
Card Shark II delivers an impressive collection of eleven card games, featuring eleven solitaire variations. Included in the package are Three Musketeers, Strategy, Perpetual Motion, Triple Alliance, Shamrocks, Nines, Aces Up II, Nestor, Treasure Trove, Troika, and Missing Link. One standout is Three Musketeers, an original solitaire creation designed by author Paul Zelman.
Spread across two disks, the game offers enough variety to keep card game enthusiasts entertained for hours. The cards are displayed with clear graphical representations of both suit and value, while keyboard controls make gameplay simple and accessible. Each game also includes its own instructions, making it easy for players to learn and enjoy every variation.

p: Cartels and Cutthroats (1981)(Strategic Simulations)(II+)[cr Bobby the Kid].zip
n: Cartels and Cutthroats (1981)(Strategic Simulations)(II+)[cr Bobby the Kid]

meta.path: ./Cartels and Cutthroats (1981)(Strategic Simulations)(II+)[cr Bobby the Kid].zip
meta.name: Cartels & Cutthroat$
meta.desc: Cartel$ & Cutthroat$ is notable for being one of the first business sims in existence. 1-8 players compete for profit in this simulation of cut-throat competition that is a simulation of the basic tenets in finance and economics. Understanding these concepts is key to winning the game, which involving setting your production level and price where marginal costs equal marginal revenues, borrow funds when prime rates are low to expand, and minimize cost per unit. You must also pay careful attention to the state of the economy and consumers' tastes, both of which change constantly. If you're unsure of what to do, your advisor in the game will guide you in the right direction.
