meta.name: Castle Wolfenstein
meta.desc:
Castle Wolfenstein is a two-dimensional action-adventure game that is played from a top-down 
perspective using a keyboard, joystick, or paddles. It has also been described as a maze game. 
There are eight difficulty levels in the game that are determined by the player's rank. 
The player takes the role of an Allied spy that has been captured by Nazis and imprisoned in a dungeon within 
Castle Wolfenstein for interrogation by the SS Stormtroopers. While the spy is waiting for 
interrogation, a dying prisoner emerges from a hiding place and hands the player a fully loaded 
pistol with 10 rounds, and three grenades before passing away. The objective is to escape from 
the castle and if the player finds the battle plans before escaping, they will be promoted and 
the complexity of the subsequent run will be increased, while the castle's layout changes and 
the game starts again.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Castle Wolfenstein (1981)(MUSE Software)[48K]-manual.pdf

p: Castles of Darkness (1981)(The Logical Choice)[cr Whip].zip
n: Castles of Darkness (1981)(The Logical Choice)[cr Whip]

meta.name: Castles of Darkness
meta.desc:
Castles of Darkness is an early Castles of Darkness graphic adventure by Michael Cashen for the Apple II. The story centers on the evil wizard Grimnacht, whose curse has plunged the land into eternal darkness after seizing two castles and possibly capturing a princess. Players must uncover and destroy the source of his power to restore light.
Developed over six months, the game pushed technical limits of early systems, using clever graphics compression to fit rich content onto a 5.25-inch floppy. Built partly with tools created by Steve Wozniak, Cashen even modified Apple's DOS to prevent copying. Released in 1981, it gained attention after Softalk magazine praised it as the first adventure game with animation, boosting sales and securing its place in early computer gaming history.

p: Cave of Time, The (1985)(-)(Side A)[cr Digital Gang].zip
n: Cave of Time, The (1985)(-)(Side A)[cr Digital Gang]

meta.path: ./Cave of Time, The (1985)(-)(Side A)[cr Digital Gang].zip
meta.name: The Cave of Time
meta.desc:
The Cave of Time is a multiple-choice adventure based on the first book in the popular Choose Your Own Adventure series. Players take the role of a child exploring the mysterious Cave of Time to recover four stolen artifacts taken by the mischievous Time Grouches: a golden crown, Abraham Lincoln's hat, a Loch Ness Monster egg, and a piece of flint.
From the cave, players can travel to different time periods or venture into the Time Grouches' lair, where they battle the creatures in simple sword-fighting sequences to reclaim stolen items. Each time period presents puzzles and choices that must be solved correctly to obtain the artifacts and return them to their proper places in history. Success often requires revisiting locations and learning from previous attempts. Combining puzzle-solving, exploration, and branching decisions, the game recreates the spirit of the bestselling book series in interactive form.

meta.name: Cavern Creatures
meta.desc: El jugador controla una pequeña nave, navegándola a través de una serie de sinuosas cavernas y túneles mientras dispara o evita obstáculos. Las cavernas se desplazan desde la parte inferior de la pantalla hacia la superior a una velocidad fija, por lo que el jugador siempre debe avanzar.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Cavern Creatures (1983)(Datamost)-manual.pdf

p: Caverns of Freitag, The (1982)(MUSE Software)(II+)[cr Untouchables][48K].zip
n: Caverns of Freitag, The (1982)(MUSE Software)(II+)[cr Untouchables][48K]

meta.name: The Caverns of Freitag
meta.desc:
The Caverns of Freitag is an action-based RPG where the player controls the warrior on his way to kill the dragon. The many monsters of the labyrinth can be destroyed using either a sword or a bow. Arrows are limited, however, and must be purchased at an inn from gold left behind by vanquished foes. Also at the inn, the warrior advances in levels and hit points according to experience gained from killing enemies. In addition to the weapons, the warrior can employ a magic spell which doubles his speed, and can only be used to run from enemies.
The game has three different display modes: in the standard mode, the screen displays the maze and the monsters in detail. It can be changed to an overview mode which shows the maze and monsters in a small window and adds detailed character statistics and messages. Finally, a magic map can be employed to display a larger section of the maze, with the disadvantage that monsters are not visible.

p: Caves of Olympus, The (1982)(Howard W. Sams & Co.).zip
n: Caves of Olympus, The (1982)(Howard W. Sams & Co.)

meta.path: ./Caves of Olympus, The (1982)(Howard W. Sams & Co.).zip
meta.name: The Caves of Olympus
meta.desc: Caves of Olympus is an illustrated adventure game. The player takes control of Anson Argyris, an egg-shaped 50 cm robot which can imitate a human being by using living cocoon masks. The game takes place on the planet Olympus and its caves which have been rebuilt into a system of deadly traps. The planet has been attacked by an alien race called Laren and the aim of the game is to find a way through the caves and join the rebel forces and counter the invaders. The game plays like any other text adventure with the player entering verb noun commands to interact with the world. By pressing the return key the player can toggle between a text screen and a graphical depiction of the scene.

p: Chalice of Mostania, The (1985)(Coastal Software)(Disk 1 of 4).zip
n: Chalice of Mostania, The (1985)(Coastal Software)(Disk 1 of 4)

meta.name: The Chalice of Mostania
meta.desc:
The Chalice of Mordania is a fantasy graphic adventure for the Apple II. After the evil wizard Rebor steals the legendary Chalice of Mostania and curses his brother, King Boswick, the player must recover the artifact before the king dies. With only seven in-game days to succeed, the quest spans forests, deserts, jungles, caves, and distant towns.
Using VERB NOUN commands, players explore the world, collect items, solve puzzles, and manage survival needs such as food, water, and sleep. Beginning in a small town, the hero is aided by Jadin the Kurumba and later Liphin the Tree Dweller. Along the way, they battle spiders, goblins, ogres, dragons, and other monsters using the proper weapons. The adventure culminates in an assault on Rebor's castle, where the wizard must be defeated with a magic arrow. Returning the chalice to the dying Boswick completes the quest and earns the player a rich reward.

p: Championship Gambler v3.6 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)[cr Bodyguard].zip
n: Championship Gambler v3.6 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)[cr Bodyguard]

meta.name: Championship Gambler
meta.desc:
Championship Gambler is a collection of three gambling games from Green Valley Publishing and it contains:
Draw Poker: A card game played with five cards where you must get the better hand than the computer while betting money which you start with $500. You are first shown your cards and you and the computer bet money on how you think the hand will go. Once you are both happy with your bets then you can get rid of any cards you don't want and receive the same number back. You can then carry on betting until once again you both are happy and both hands are shown. the player with the better hand wins the money bet.
Blackjack: A card game where the computer is the dealer and the idea is to beat his hand to receive money back that you bet which you start with $1000. You both have two cards and you have to get closer than the dealer to 21 or get 21 by either asking for another card or keeping what you have. Once you are happy with your hand then the dealer plays and you either win or lose depending what his hand is. If you or the dealer goes higher than 21 then the other player wins if lower.
One-Armed Bandit: A fruit machine with three reels where you start with $50 and each go costs $1. You can select how hard you pull the handle and once the reels have spun you get a combination of fruits. If any match the combinations below the reels then you win that amount of money.

meta.name: Championship Golf
meta.desc:
Championship Golf is a strategy game that allows you to manage a career as a golfer from amateur to professional by entering tournaments and making as much money as you can to be World Number One. After entering your name you are presented with a number of options which include, looking at your bank balance which starts at £20000 with the option to mortgage your house or get a loan, you can also look at any offers for sponsorships. Practising allows you to practise various shots from putting, driving, bunker, chipping or practise round and you have ten hours to spread between the shots. Once you are happy with your options you can select your next tournament.
You have the choice of two tournaments, a major or a minor with the expenses and prize money different and also the standards of the other golfers. Once a tournament is selected you can then hire a caddy and coach and also select your hotel from a B&B to a five star hotel. You watch your golfer and his partner now play two rounds of golf with each hole shown on the whole screen from above and your progress through the tournament. After two rounds you can see if you have qualified fro the final two rounds or if it is time to go home. During a tournament you can go to the bookies and bet on other golfers.
After your tournament you see the results and your bank statement and hopefully you made more money. The world rankings can be seen to chart your progress, then it's back to practising, selecting sponsors before selecting your next tournament.

meta.name: Championship Wrestling
meta.desc:
Arcade-style game about wrestling, featuring 8 different characters, like K.C. Colossus or Prince Vicious, and 25 different moves, like leg drops, spin kicks, body slams, rope bouncing, flying drop kicks and the so-called "infamous" atomic drop. You can even throw your opponent off the ring. Scoring is based on the complexity of your moves.
Two-player hot-seat feature available as well, and up to 8 players can play in the same championship.

meta.name: The Chessmaster 2000
meta.desc:
Esta entrega de la serie Chessmaster presenta un poderoso oponente de ajedrez con 12 niveles de habilidad, desde principiante hasta gran maestro. Entre sus muchas funciones se incluye una biblioteca de aperturas formada por 71.000 jugadas y la posibilidad de ver el historial de jugadas.
Para ayudar a los principiantes, hay un modo de aprendizaje que muestra todas las jugadas legales, una jugada de sugerencia que ofrece lo que el ordenador cree que es una jugada fuerte, y la posibilidad de que el maestro de ajedrez analice todas las jugadas anteriores de una partida. También se ofrecen famosas posiciones de juego reales y desafíos preestablecidos.

meta.name: Chez 21
meta.desc:
Chez 21 is a graphical 1 - 5 player blackjack game for the Apple II.
The players compete against the dealer at a graphical blackjack table. Allows 2 different card designs. Each player starts with $2500, and players set their bet at the beginning of each hand against the dealer. Follows basic Las Vegas blackjack rules, with the goal being to draw cards to see who gets the closest to 21 without going over.

meta.name: Choplifter
meta.desc: Choplifter es un juego de acción / arcade de desplazamiento lateral que te pone al mando de un helicóptero de ataque. Tu misión: ir tras las líneas enemigas y rescatar hasta 16 rehenes por nivel. Sin embargo, ten cuidado, porque los tanques y los aviones enemigos intentarán detenerte, ¡y no dejarán de disparar mientras rescates a esos rehenes!
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Choplifter (1982)(Brøderbund)(II-II+)[48K]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Chrono Warrior
meta.desc:
In Chrono Warrior you travel through different eras of time and with each age to travel to features a different style of gameplay.
- Stone age: collect all the pieces of your time portal and go through it. Beware of the dinosaurs.
- Roman empire: you ride a chariot in a race and must collect 5 gold coins to proceed.
- Medieval time: you are Robin Hood and must go through a maze to rob from the rich and give to the poor. Do this three times to proceed.
-
Civil war: you ride a balloon and must collect five energy ribbons to proceed. You must avoid the cannon fire as you do so.
- 1985: avoid cars and gather mail playing as a postman.

n: Chuck Yeager's Advanced Flight Trainer (1987)(Electronic Arts)(Side A)[cr Blade]

meta.path: ./Chuck Yeager's Advanced Flight Trainer (1987)(Electronic Arts)(Side A)[cr Blade].zip
meta.name: Chuck Yeager's Advanced Flight Trainer
meta.desc: In Chuck Yeager's Advanced Flight Trainer you have the chance to test pilot 14 different airplanes, such as the Bell X-1 and the Lockheed SR-71. It features 10 different viewpoints both inside and outside of the plane, with a zoom of up to 256x, and different aerodynamics for each plane. You can do different things with the planes, from racing against a computer opponent, to practising formation and stunt flights. You can follow the testing and development programme through its evolution. There is a "black box recorder" to replay your flights, and analysis of your performance compared to an ideal one.

n: Cite Perdue, La (19xx)(Excalibur)(FR)(Side A)[cr Cthulhu Mythos Software]

meta.path: ./Cite Perdue, La (19xx)(Excalibur)(FR)(Side A)[cr Cthulhu Mythos Software].zip
meta.name: La Cite Perdue
meta.desc:
As a bold young journalist, you answer the call of your friend, Professor Mac Dowell.
Hurry to join him—but from the moment you arrive at the airport, be very careful! The road leading to his town is already full of obstacles.
Did you make it there safely? Well done! Now truly set off on your adventure: cross the forest, explore the underground passages, equip yourself, and climb the mountain. Discover the underground river, dive into the siphon…
So, if you are still alive, enter the LOST CITY. There, you have a very specific mission to accomplish. What mission? We'll leave you the pleasure of discovering it yourself.

meta.name: Class Struggle
meta.desc: This is a direct translation to computer of a Monopoly-like board game, written by Bertell Ollman, Professor of Politics at N.Y.U. and author of Alienation: Marx's Conception of Man in Capitalist Society and other books on Marxism. CLASS STRUGGLE is also written from a socialist perspective. Though some will find the game simplistic, it could be an excellent springboard to class discussions.

meta.name: Close Assault
meta.desc:
From a time when the best graphics a computer game offered were on the front cover of the box, Avalon Hill's Close Assault offers a hybrid computer/board game experience by enclosing cardboard counters and a playing board. The result, per the manual, is a "visual impact... much greater than on even the best of computer graphics displays" while freeing for the processor, "a huge amount of limited resources" normally used to manage the graphical interface. Those resources instead are devoted to accounting for every soldier engaged in the scenario, and fulfilling a dream of the era's simulation board-gamers: realism in what you know and when you know it. In Close Assault, your opponent's units appear on the board only when they are spotted by yours, their morale is only evident by their behavior, and only your experience will tell you the odds of success. This is a far cry from the usual board simulation experience, where you could pour over combat success tables and footnoted modifiers to judge exactly where the chances break in your favor, and use the morale markers on your opponent's front line to decide where to push.
Close Assault's combination of three scenarios and three forces allows representation of a dozen different historical situations spanning northern Europe in World War II. Squad-level tactics are simulated on a common map showing a small village and three levels of topography. Commands are entered numerically or as single letters in response to computer prompts, as each player proceeds through six phases of hotseat turns -- units moving, firing, and reacting -- which represent two minutes of battle. As the computer kicks out the results of your gambles and guesses, counters come and go on the map, squads being spotted and routed or destroyed. In that way, the game is a genuine hybrid, flicking players' attention back and forth between the cardboard map and the computer screen. Units also come and go from your command: when they become demoralized or enter close combat, the computer takes over. Meanwhile, the manual comes straight out of the board-game simulation tradition, outlined by topic and case with occasional lessons in history and battle science, such as (regarding morale), "beneath the surface of any organized army lies a terrified mass of men who long for home and peace".
The game experience would be limited to the standard armchair-lieutenant's view, but for a simple number noted on the squad record pad (an analog device to help the humans remember casualties, equipment, experience, and leader placement). The number represents you on the battlefield. Somehow, this simple mechanism effectively engrosses players in the course of the battle. If you become a casualty, the computer finishes the scenario for you while you watch, possibly even carrying your soldiers to victory if you left your sergeants well-positioned.
Besides two-player hot-seat, solitaire is available, wherein the programmers put the computer's capable calculations to work on multi-turn tactics, nuanced with the historical characteristics of the opposing nationalities, from equipment to morale to battle tactics.
meta.map: ./images/Close Assault (19xx)(Avalon Hill)-map.png

meta.name: Club Casino
meta.desc: A compilation of casino games including Blackjack, Acey Ducey, Craps and Slots.

meta.name: Colonial Conquest
meta.desc: Colonial Conquest is a grand strategy game set during the late 1800's and early 1900's that puts you in charge of one of the six Imperialistic powers of the time (Great Britain, France, Germany, U.S., Russia and Japan). Using diplomacy, espionage, armies and fleets, these six countries vie for control of more than 120 minor countries and each other. Your goal in the game is to conquer as many minor nations as possible and set yourself up as the mightiest ruler on Earth. There are three scenarios, and up to six people can play each as a separate nation - or the computer can take charge of all the other nations.

meta.name: Colorix
meta.desc:
In this puzzle game you have to match a predefined colored pattern so that you fill a certain percentage with the rotatable fragments you have before you run out of matching fragments.
For example, in level 1 you have a 12x12 square, whereby the outer ring is purple, then blue, then red, then green, and finally a yellow block in the center.
Now you have a fragment for placing into the square, which you can rotate in any of the four directions. Once you placed it, those colored blocks will become gray and can't be used anymore. This also means that the next fragment must be placed totally into the colored area, overlapping isn't possible. And of course, you can only place them in a matching area of colored blocks.
And here comes the problem. Those fragments aren't just a square, they can be in unusual complex forms, and you have to carefully think where to place them, because once set, they can't be removed. And on top of this, you only have a few seconds to think. If the timer for a fragment runs to zero, you get a new fragment, but only if you have a bomb remaining, which is used when the time runs out. When you start a level, you have 5 bombs available.
Good news are that you normally don't have to fill all blocks, before you start the level, the game will tell you how much, in percent, you have to fill.
The game offers 100 different levels, one more complex then the other. This means, that the basic geometric figure will be more complex, but also the colored patterns inside the figure.
After completing a level, you get a level code for the following level, so you don't have to play all 100 levels in one row.

meta.name: Commando
meta.desc: Several levels await your super-tough Commando in this vertical scrolling game. Armed with only a standard rifle and a few grenades you must take on hordes of Nazis. Some are wandering around in the open, while others have picked out hiding places, which you must approach from certain angles. Trees, rivers and bridges create a varied combat-like terrain and must be incorporated into your thinking. Extra grenades can be collected, and will definitely be required, as they allow you to kill from distance and thus avoid some enemy shots.

meta.name: Competition Karate
meta.desc:
Competition Karate is a 1 - 2 player karate simulator and arcade game.
The player creates a roster of martial artists who can perform at either white, green, blue, or black belt level. Each character has their own individual statistics, including: strength, agility, quickness, stamina, perception, courage, arms, legs, and body. The player also chooses from a list of five sensei with their own individual strengths. Each character can improve themselves by practicing in the gym, and their sensei influences how the character develops.
Characters can be pitted against human or computer controlled opponents in tournament mode or single matches. The player can also have two computer opponents compete to see which characters are the strongest. The player can choose how many rounds are in each match. Combat occurs in turns, where the player selects a direction and action key to direct their martial artist.

meta.name: Computer Napoleonics
meta.desc: Computer Napoleonics: The Battle of Waterloo is a division level simulation of the Battle of Waterloo. It can be played by one or two players. One player commands the forces on Napoleon while the other player (or the computer) commands the forces of the Anglo-Allied-Prussia armies. Take turns by moving armies in position and engaging in combat. Turns continue this way for ten game turns or until one side has successfully fulfilled its victory conditions. Each game turn lasts about one hour of real time.

meta.name: Conflict 2500
meta.desc: It is the year 2500 and the galaxy has been at peace for many years. Until now! A fleet of planet pulverizers has invaded the sector of the galaxy under your command. Reports are telling you of a massive wave of destruction. It is up to you to rid the universe of this threat of utter destruction. Conflict 2500 is a strategic simulation of conflict in the 26th century. The game can be played solitary or with up to 10 other players by giving command of ships to individuals. The computer controls a fleet of planet pulverizers and adapts its strategy to your actions. The game can be stopped during play or can continue until one side or the other is destroyed.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Conflict 2500 (1981)(Avalon Hill)-manual.pdf

meta.name: Conflict in Vietnam
meta.desc:
The third and final installment in Microprose's Command series of war games simulates five key battles in Vietnam, beginning with the defeat of the French colonial army in 1954 and culminating in the 1972 victory of the Viet Cong against South Vietnam forces. 
Conflict in Vietnam adapts the well tried "accelerated real-time" game system of its predecessors to the jungle combat situation in Indochina, with its bizarre balance of power, specific technology (such massive use of helicopters) and unique strategic challenges. Impressively complex for its time, the simulation calculates a day-night-cycle, weather, terrain, supply lines, formations, unit organization and experience, to name only the most important factors. Two players can compete head to head on one computer, soloists may randomize the enemy priorities in some scenarios for added uncertainty on the battlefield.
Even more so than in the previous games, designer duo Sid Meier and Ed Bever stress the educational aspect of their well-researched historical simulation: the manual is designed as a complementary read that offers extensive background information to the five scenarios and their 'what if' variants.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Conflict in Vietnam (1986)(Microprose)(Side A)[cr Kristo]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Conglomerates Collide
meta.desc:
Conglomerates Collide is a game of multinational corporation management. Or basically, a monopoly sim. You take the role a high-ranking executive in charge of company acquisitions and your objective is to acquire the most companies in the market in order to propel you to the role of chairman of the board and defeat the opposing corporations.
You start the game with a base capital of 20 million dollars and through each turn you must decide whether to swallow companies or not depending on their current worth, which is determined by the ever changing market conditions. The trick is that you are not alone in the game as other corporations are on the hunt to acquire successful companies and it's your job to grow enough each year/turn so that you can take the poor workers away from the other corporations and ingest them into your own money-making monsters instead of the other way around. Remember! This is a game of multinational conglomerates so the bigger and badder the better.
Features 3 modes of play: single player, vs computer and vs human opponents (up to 4-players hot-seat). Uses a simple text-based interface with small still graphics to commemorate events such as acquisitions and such.

p: Congo Bongo (1983)(Sega)[cr 1200 Club - Midwest Pirates Guild][48K].zip
n: Congo Bongo (1983)(Sega)[cr 1200 Club - Midwest Pirates Guild][48K]

meta.name: Congo Bongo
meta.desc: Congo Bongo is an arcade platform game similar in many ways to Donkey Kong, but with an isometric perspective. The player takes control of a safari hunter who is searching for an ape named Bongo, determined to punish him for setting the hunter's tent on fire. The game consists of four one-screen stages, each with an objective to jump on platforms and reach the top. Various animals will try to stop the protagonist: for example, in the first level a large gorilla throws coconuts at him. The hero has no offensive abilities and must jump or otherwise avoid enemy attacks. Stages may contain obstacles or hazardous spots that would kill off the main character.

meta.name: Conquering Worlds
meta.desc:
Conquering Worlds is a space-based strategy game for 1 - 4 players involving planetary exploration and conquest.
The player and their opponents control a series of planets within a galaxy containing 4 - 32 star systems, which are represented by planets rapidly orbiting a central sun. Each turn is timed to 120 seconds, and the player must command as many troop actions as possible during this time. Troops orders are placed by sending troops in a direction, and they will arrive at whatever body lies that way. There are two routes to victory: Destroying all enemies, or conquering all planets.
When a player tries to invade an occupied planet, they are presented with a mini-game where they and their opponent take turns moving a series of lasers that run laterally across the screen. The goal is to shoot as many of the rapidly moving enemy's vessels as possible in a limited time period.

meta.name: Copts and Robbers
meta.desc: Copts & Robbers is similar to the Atari 2600 game Adventure with the player guiding a character (represented by a white square) through a maze from a top down view. The maze is located below an Egyptian pyramid that the player has fallen into when looking for the tomb of the Pharaoh Tuttut. The aim of the game is to escape the maze and to do so it is necessary to locate and collect four jewels and a vase and return them to the vault room.

meta.name: Court Circuit
meta.desc:
In Court Circuit (French for “Short Circuit”), players must navigate a twisting racetrack while avoiding the walls at all costs. Crashing into a wall ends the current run and costs one of your nine starting cars. The game begins with a relatively simple course, but the difficulty rises sharply on the second track with far more complex layouts.
Points are earned for every second spent surviving on the track, and reaching 1,000 points advances you to the next course. Even after a crash, your accumulated score carries over, giving you another chance to progress. Though simple in design, the game's increasingly difficult tracks make it surprisingly addictive as you strive to survive long enough to conquer each new circuit.

meta.name: The Coveted Mirror
meta.desc:
The Coveted Mirror was a graphical text-based adventure game published for the Apple II. It was created by Eagle Berns and Holly Thomason and released by Penguin Software in 1983.
The Coveted Mirror utilizes a text parser, in which the player types commands to perform actions in the game.Additionally, part of the challenge of The Coveted Mirror involves frequent, timed escapes from the jail tower. Within a limited timeframe, indicated by an hourglass, the player must search the castle, find objects, and talk to people and return to the cell by the time the jailer makes his rounds. This timed element adds another dimension to the exploration and puzzle solving.
he land of Starbury was taken over by Voar the evil. During his grasp for power, Voar tried to seize the magic mirror but broke it into five pieces; luckily, he was only able to grab four of the pieces. it is up to you, the hero, to find the fifth and final piece in order to break Voar's rule and save the people of Starbury.

meta.name: Crazy Maczy
meta.desc:
In Crazy Mazey, there's all kinds of cash hidden throughout the city streets, but someone has tipped off the killer goons, who are going to try to stop you. You can't outrun the goons, so you're going to have to try to outmaneuver them, using your wits and agility to gather all the cash and escape town.
You drive a small vehicle over a cityscape, attempting to avoid enemy vehicles. Your goal is to gather all the cash symbols on the screen. After doing this, an exit from the stage will appear in the top left corner, and the player can drive to this to complete the level. There a total of 9 different stages or maps, with 7 difficulty levels which set the speed of vehicles. The player gains 100 points for each piece of loot collected. The enemy vehicles will incessantly travel towards the player, and if two enemy vehicles come in contact, they will be destroyed. There is no other way to eliminate enemy vehicles. The player has a total of 3 cars, and will lose a vehicle when they run into another vehicle. Lose 3 cars, and it's game over.

meta.name: Creature Venture
meta.desc: A black-and-white, line graphics and text adventure, Creature Venture is the third game release by Highlands Computer Services. This time, the adventurer must explore a haunted mansion, rid it of evil creatures, and find lost treasure. Creature Venture was Highlands Computer Services' most successful title.

meta.name: Cricketeer
meta.desc: Cricketeer es una variante de Frogger en la que el jugador toma el control de un grillo que, como en el arcade original, primero tiene que cruzar una calle con mucho tráfico y luego un río antes de saltar a una de las cinco casillas en el otro extremo del río. A diferencia del juego de arcade, las secciones de la calle y el río tienen lugar en pantallas separadas. Un nivel se completa cuando las cinco casillas de inicio están ocupadas por grillos y el juego pasa a un nivel superior. Las vidas se pierden después de ser atropelladas por un vehículo o caer al río, el juego termina cuando nos quedamos sin vidas.

meta.name: Crime Stopper
meta.desc: The daughter of Millicent Hyacinth Sizemore, Cartier-Blanche Sizemore a construction heiress has been kidnapped and if she is not found by midnight then she will be murdered, so you, Al Clubs, a private detective has been given the job to find her. You must explore the city looking for clues and talk to people in your quest for the missing girl and you can use the subway to travel long distances around the city using a map to guide you. Crime Stopper: The Hijacked Heiress Caper is a text adventure where the screen describes your location and you type in commands with the keyboard to interact with your surroundings.

p: Crimson Crown, The (1985)(Polarware)(Side A)[cr Corsairs][128K][AKA Transylvania II].zip
n: Crimson Crown, The (1985)(Polarware)(Side A)[cr Corsairs][128K][AKA Transylvania II]

meta.path: ./Crimson Crown, The (1985)(Polarware)(Side A)[cr Corsairs][128K][AKA Transylvania II].zip
meta.name: Transylvania II - Crimson Crown
meta.desc:
In Transylvania we rescued Princess Sabrina, and met Crown Prince Erik. They return upon news of the King John the Good's death, and learn the crown has vanished. The Vampire Lord Drakul tries to uncover its secrets to become King, and threatens its land.
As their guide, they accompany you on their journey to reclaim the crown an amulet of immense power and rid the land of the Vampire. Certain tasks require their unique abilities, Princess Sabrina understands magic and Crown Prince Erik can use items that rightfully belongs to the heir of the throne, among other roles.
Some places seem familiar, and others are new. Recognizable ancient tree stump and the new mysterious Wizard's castle to underground caverns.

meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Crimson Crown, The (1985)(Polarware)(Side A)[cr Corsairs][128K][AKA Transylvania II]-boxback.jpg

meta.name: Crisis Mountain
meta.desc: Crisis Mountain es un juego de plataformas clásico. Tu tarea es desactivar las bombas dejadas por terroristas en las cavernas de una montaña cuando un volcán inactivo entra en erupción inesperadamente. El tiempo es limitado ya que las bombas detonarán pronto. El juego tiene nueve divertidos niveles que superar.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Crisis Mountain (1982)(Synergistic Software)(II+)(US)[cr Sowbug][48K][no boot]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Critical Mass
meta.desc:
On June 01, the United Nations received the message, "Good Morning. Just thought I'd drop a line to let you know that precisely at 8 pm on June 9th, I'll be destroying the world's five largest cities with thermal nuclear weapons".
A madman threatens to destroy the world's five largest cities with nuclear weapons unless stopped before June 9th. Chosen by the United Nations, the player travels across Paris, Rome, London, Miami, and San Juan, solving puzzles and uncovering clues through classic VERB-NOUN text adventure gameplay. The investigation reveals the villainous Count Stuportino, hiding in a secret base on Martinique, as the mastermind behind the plot. Along the way, the game features two arcade-style sequences: winning a water-skiing contest in Miami to obtain a needed item, and using a plasma beam weapon to shoot down incoming nuclear missiles before they devastate the targeted cities.

meta.name: Cross City
meta.desc:
Cross City is a 1-player driving arcade game for the Apple II.
The player drives a car which travels horizontally left and right, but can also jump. Portrayed from a side view, the player can jump between platforms and jump over other cars. The player picks up red tanks, which can be thrown at other cars to destroy them. The player also picks up blue tanks, and when ten tanks are collected, the player advances to the next level. If the player strikes another car, their vehicle is destroyed. The player starts with 3 cars, and when all are destroyed, it's game over.

meta.name: Crossfire
meta.desc: En esta adaptación del juego de arcade Targ, tu trabajo es defender tu ciudad de las bestias alienígenas merodeadoras. Puedes maniobrar tu nave y disparar simultáneamente en cuatro direcciones mientras patrullas la cuadrícula regular de calles: el enemigo ha rodeado la manzana de tu ciudad y los ataques llegan por todos lados mientras los alienígenas pululan con todas las armas encendidas. Para sobrevivir, tendrás que abrirte camino a tiros entrando y saliendo de la línea de fuego y derribando a los asaltantes con tus propias andanadas: una vez que todos sean eliminados, pasas al siguiente nivel, para enfrentarte a otra oleada más.

meta.name: Crossword
meta.desc: Crossword - An excellent game with 24 puzzles on a disk.  You can solve them, modify them or create your own.

meta.name: Crossword Magic
meta.desc:
Become a wizard with words: Create, play and print crossword puzzles effortlessly with Crossword Magic. Create puzzles to entertain and challenge your friends. Build puzzles to use as study aids, greeting cards, or invitations. Play puzzles on the computer or print them out. Let Crossword Magic work its spell on you.
Features
Create crossword puzzles easily. Simply select the puzzle words, then let the program do its magic as it creates the
puzzle grid automatically Play crossword puzzles.
Print puzzles, clues, word lists, and answer puzzles
Easy-to-use format.
Versatile creative tool: ideal for helping with homework for creating tests, and for designing cards and invitations.

p: Crush, Crumble and Chomp! The Movie Monster Game (1981)(Epyx)(II+)[cr Pirate].zip
n: Crush, Crumble and Chomp! The Movie Monster Game (1981)(Epyx)(II+)[cr Pirate]

meta.path: ./Crush, Crumble and Chomp! The Movie Monster Game (1981)(Epyx)(II+)[cr Pirate].zip
meta.name: Crush, Crumble and Chomp!
meta.desc: Crush, Crumble & Chomp lets you assume the role of a movie-style monster, becoming one of any number of giant crawling, swimming, or flying beasts. Technically CC&C is a strategy game; you have to keep an eye on several statistics, including your health, appetite, and even such minute details as the tilt of your head (helps in aiming breath weapons). Destroy the city before the human military destroys you!
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Crush, Crumble and Chomp! The Movie Monster Game (1981)(Epyx)(II+)[cr Pirate]-image.jpg

meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Crush, Crumble and Chomp! The Movie Monster Game (1981)(Epyx)(II+)[cr Pirate]-boxback.jpg

meta.name: Crypt
meta.desc:
The Crypt is a single player adventure game in which the player explores a series of dungeons collecting treasure and battling monsters.
The game is entirely controlled via the keyboard and has six levels, each level protected by a password. The objective of the game is to locate the Sacred Crucifix and return to the first room. The password for the first level is supplied both within the game and in the documentation, the password for the level two is supplied at the end of the first and so on. 
In each room in the crypt is a monster which must be disposed of. Once the monster is dead the player's sword becomes useless and must be disposed of, additionally, Satan is none too pleased at having his minions slaughtered and sends fire bolts to avenge them.
When travelling between rooms in the crypt the game may take the player into the Blood Cell Attack phase. Here diseased bats descend from numbered positions at the top of the screen and the player must defend themselves against their attack by pressing the appropriately number key.

meta.name: Crypt of Medea Save Disk
meta.desc:
Based loosely on the famed Greek character, Crypt of Medea is one of the earliest horror-themed games released for the Apple II series. The plot is simple: you are trapped in an underground crypt filled with a variety of ghastly creatures and traps, and you must find your way out before you become just another nameless corpse--just like the ones you encounter all throughout the maze-like mausoleum.
Crypt of Medea is a Hi-Res adventure game with color graphics, using the simple two-word "verb noun" parser that was common in these types of games. In its heyday, it was panned for its graphic depictions of gratuitous violence, but seeing as such material has almost become a standard in today's games, one could argue that Crypt of Medea was fairly ahead of its time.

p: Cubic Tic Tac Toe (1985)(Silver Bullet Systems)[cr First Class].zip
n: Cubic Tic Tac Toe (1985)(Silver Bullet Systems)[cr First Class]

meta.path: ./Cubic Tic Tac Toe (1985)(Silver Bullet Systems)[cr First Class].zip
meta.name: Cubic Tic Tac Toe
meta.desc:
This 'Tic Tac Toe' variant is flown in 3x3x3 cubic playfield. Player should align three 'X's either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally to score a Tic Tac Toe. To win the game, player should score more Tic Tac Toes than opponent. To win a match, player should win more games than opponent. In the case of a tie game, the tie is awarded to CATS as in the regular game of Tic Tac Toe.
The game is a single against the computer or two-player of three different variations. There are three difficulty levels defined by match lengths.
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Cubic Tic Tac Toe (1985)(Silver Bullet Systems)[cr First Class]-marquee.png

p: Curse of Crowley Manor, The (1980)(Adventure International).zip
n: Curse of Crowley Manor, The (1980)(Adventure International)

meta.name: The Curse of Crowley Manor
meta.desc:
The Curse of Crowley Manor is the second game in the Other Venture series and has been written by Jyym Pearson.
The story takes place in turn-of-the-century London. As a Scotland Yard detective, the player must solve a murder at Crowley Estate. As the investigation begins, occult elements come into the story. There are demons, ghosts and rodents to encounter. The blurb claims that the game contains many unique plot twists and strange devices to keep players on your toes, and recommends that only experienced adventurers apply.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Curse of Crowley Manor, The (1980)(Adventure International)-manual.pdf

meta.name: Cyberchess
meta.desc: A chess game with cyber-/Tron-like theme.  The layout of the board has been modified to have pieces rest on intersections instead of squares, but the gameplay is identical to chess.

meta.name: Cyborg!
meta.desc:
"Do you enjoy science fiction? Do you like getting thoroughly involved in a good story? Do you delight in a complicated plot, especially when all the pieces come together in a remarkable but totally logical conclusion?"
"If you can answer yes to any of these questions, you're apt to be as excited as we are about this new adventure... Cyborg introduces the most exciting advances in adventuring since the original Adventure began the whole wonderful thing." - Softalk Magazine
Cyborg is a unique approach to gaming with no treasures and no score. It contains a real-time graphics simulation, character development, animals that you can talk to, opinions from the Cyborg and a consistency found in no other adventure.
Cyborg was written by Michael Berlyn, creator of Sentient Software's Oo-Topos. He is the author of two science fiction books published by Bantam Books- The Integrated Man and Crystal Phoenix. He is an active member of Science Fiction Writers of America.

p: Daily Crossword Puzzles From the New York Times (1984)(Evolving Technology Company).zip
n: Daily Crossword Puzzles From the New York Times (1984)(Evolving Technology Company)

meta.path: ./Daily Crossword Puzzles From the New York Times (1984)(Evolving Technology Company).zip
meta.name: Daily Crossword Puzzles From the New-York Times
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Daily Crossword Puzzles From the New York Times (1984)(Evolving Technology Company)-image.jpg
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Daily Crossword Puzzles From the New York Times (1984)(Evolving Technology Company)-marquee.png

meta.name: The Dambusters
meta.desc:
Dam Busters is an early flight simulation, set in World War II. It focuses on Dam Busters who historically were used to heavily bombard strategic objectives, in this case dams.
This task is not easy, as en route you will face heavy opposition forces. Fortunately, you won't be a sitting bomber in the air. Your aircraft is equipped with machine guns (left and right) with unlimited rounds of fire power, so fire at will as the enemy approaches.
When the time comes, you will have to bomb the target manually. So, get ready to unload the cargo and give them hell!

meta.name: Dark Forest
meta.desc: A strategy/war game where you must defeat your opponents and take over their territories.  You select one of four territories to play as and up to 6 players can play.  The 'Gruds' play the remaining teams.  The game takes place on an overhead map of the land, with each country's territories color-coded.  Each turn you get more men, move them from territory to territory, and fight to take over new territories.  The goal is of course to defeat all your opponents and the Gruds, and own the entire land.

p: Darkhorn - Realm of the Warlords (1986)(Avalon Hill)[cr AAHZ].zip
n: Darkhorn - Realm of the Warlords (1986)(Avalon Hill)[cr AAHZ]

meta.path: ./Darkhorn - Realm of the Warlords (1986)(Avalon Hill)[cr AAHZ].zip
meta.name: Darkhorn - Realm of the Warlords (1986)(Avalon Hill)[cr AAHZ]
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Darkhorn - Realm of the Warlords (1986)(Avalon Hill)[cr AAHZ]-marquee.png

meta.name: Dawn Patrol
meta.desc: Having simulated modern-day planes with Flight of the Intruder, and World War 2 with Overlord, Rowan brought World War 1 to life in this flight simulation. It is structured around an illustrated book with pilot biographies, all of which modify as the game goes on and events unfold. 150 missions are featured, each with fully inter-linked briefings. There are 13 different fighter planes to fly, both Allied and German (Fokker Dr.I, Fokker Eindecker, Fokker D.VII, Albatros D.I, D.II, D.III, Royal Aircraft Factory SE.5a, Airco DH.2, Sopwith Pup, Sopwith Camel, Sopwith Triplane, Nieuport Scout, SPAD VII).

p: Death in the Caribbean (1983)(Micro Fun)(Disk 1 of 2)[f].zip
n: Death in the Caribbean (1983)(Micro Fun)(Disk 1 of 2)[f]

meta.name: Death in the Caribbean
meta.desc:
Death in the Caribbean is an old-style text/graphics adventure. You are an treasure hunter on an unnamed Caribbean island searching for buried pirate gold. You'll travel through numerous locales, a tricky maze, and past fierce alligators on your way to the chest of Doubloons. Puzzles range from "find object and apply to situation" to a doozy of a cryptogram.
As for mechanics, the graphic section of the screen draws itself in visible pen elements (i.e.: Outline then fill, move on to next shape). Below the graphics window is a simple command line with a tiny bit of room above for the brief written description of the area you are in. Like every good adventure game, the puzzles start easy and get more difficult as you progress, until they get just plain silly hard. Play time is about 30 hours. It comes on 4 disk sides, the front and back of two floppy disks. This game does not innovate upon the genre, and is built on the gameplay of its predecessors.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Death in the Caribbean (1983)(Micro Fun)(Disk 1 of 2)[f]-manual.pdf
meta.map: ./images/Death in the Caribbean (1983)(Micro Fun)(Disk 1 of 2)[f]-map.jpg

meta.name: Death Race '82
meta.desc: The year is 2082, and post-World War III atomic radiation has reduced the world's population to less than 10,000. You've been captured by corrupt land barons, and must escape their robot-protected castle. You hop into a turbo-car, and must navigate the 2 miles of twisting concrete jungle that surrounds the castle in every direction. Hiding out are robotic Death Squad cars armed with laserquark cannons, which will attempt to hunt down the player, and destroy them. The robotic lasers are so powerful, they can slice through solid walls to destroy the player.

meta.name: Deep Space : Operation Copernicus
meta.desc: In the year 2123, the genetically engineered warriors of the Andromedan Hegemony launch an invasion to seize the United Planets of Solaria's rich Asteroid Belt mines. On Earth, leaders scramble to organize defenses against the alien assault.nnnnA courier robot arrives at your door bearing a draft notice from the Solarian Armed Forces. Within hours, you are in the cockpit of a Katana interceptor, preparing to battle the invading Andromedans.nnnnIn Deep Space -- Operation Copernicus, you pilot a Katana warship through four dangerous missions set among the deadly debris of the Trojan asteroid cluster. Skill, strategy, and survival are entirely up to you.

meta.name: Defender
meta.desc: Defender puso a los jugadores a cargo de una nave enviada para proteger a la humanidad de oleada tras oleada de fuerzas alienígenas atacantes.nnArmada con bombas inteligentes y la capacidad de usar el hiperespacio para moverse rápidamente por el planeta, la nave del jugador debe luchar contra Bombers, Pods, Swarmers, Baiters y Landers, que pueden capturar a los humanoides y transformarlos en Mutantes mortales e implacables. Si no se logra salvar a los humanoides de la caída libre o de la transformación Mutante, el planeta es destruido.

meta.name: Dell Crossword Puzzles
meta.desc:
Dell Crossword Puzzles contains a number of crossword puzzles developed by Dell Magazine.
Contains a number of crossword puzzles of varying topics. The player must manually type the answers within the corresponding blanks. Includes word checks, letter answers, and clues to difficult puzzles. Game contains 30 puzzles of varying difficulties.

meta.name: Delta Squadron
meta.desc: Delta Squadron is a strategic space war simulation for the Apple II.nnnnThe player takes the role of Legion Alliance Squadron Commander, and must direct up to 34 small one or two seater fighter spaceships down a long trench and destroy the Cetusites' Main Power Induction Inverter. The Cetusites' base is protected by their own fighters and ground defenses.nnnnThere are a total of 8 scenarios: Slingshot, Confrontation, Point, Protect, Arrowhead, Sweep, Surround, Sandwich, and Genesis. The player can save a scenario in progress, and replay at a different time.nnnnEach vessel has their own pilot with individual experience. The goal is to navigate a fighter the length of the trench, and fire a photon torpedo into the Main Power Induction Inverter. If successful, the enemy structure is destroyed. The game contains 3D-animated cutscenes for combat results, the destruction of the enemy base, and other events.

meta.name: Demon's Forge
meta.desc: Demon's Forge is the first commercially published game made by Brian Fargo, who was well known for a variety of classic RPG's like Wasteland and The Bard's Tale. The game is a graphical adventure game which places the player in the caverns of Demon's Forge. The game was known for its difficulty due to its esoteric puzzles.

meta.name: Depth Charge
meta.desc: nnDepth Charge es un juego de pantalla única donde debes proteger cinco acorazados que navegan en la parte superior de la pantalla de un ataque de submarinos enemigos debajo de las olas. Cada acorazado está en pantalla por sí mismo y puedes moverlo hacia la izquierda o hacia la derecha, mientras que los diferentes números de submarinos se mueven de izquierda a derecha a través de la pantalla a distintas profundidades. Los submarinos liberarán varias minas que se dirigen a la superficie, pero estás armado con cinco cargas de profundidad que se mueven hacia abajo en la pantalla intentando golpear a los submarinos o las minas. Si golpeas un submarino, una mina o la carga de profundidad toca el fondo de la pantalla, se te rearma con otra. Si eres alcanzado por una mina, ese barco se pierde y aparece el siguiente. Antes de comenzar el juego, puedes seleccionar tu nivel de habilidad (1-4), siendo el uno el más fácil.

meta.name: Destiny
meta.desc:
The player is in charge of bringing a small group of prehistoric people through the changing world, from the first crude working with stone tools to space travel, developing them from a simple tribe into a successful modern society. Along the way the player races against up to seven other tribes, all reaching for the same goal. As the player progresses through the game, he must lead his tribe to victory using diplomacy and conquest. Options include real-time or turn-based play, traditional 2D or new 3D views, full campaigns or mini-scenarios, and military or scientific victory conditions.
Unlike previous "god" games where players are only able to focus on one invention and building project at a time, Destiny allows players to divide their resources among multiple activities in a real-time environment. Each city can be constructing multiple facilities at any time, and production of new materials must be balanced with the resources available. Research can be focused on a few key areas, or the player can elect to spread efforts into several fields. Once opposing races are encountered, the player can choose how to approach them. Destiny allows the player to send emissaries to opponents proposing any type of agreement, from trade arrangements to declarations of war. The computer-controlled players respond realistically to these offers. These offers can also be made in multiplayer games. Destiny includes built-in support for up to eight players on IPX and TCP/IP networks .

p: Diabolical Plot of Dr. Dracupig, The (19xx)(The Southern Pirate).zip
n: Diabolical Plot of Dr. Dracupig, The (19xx)(The Southern Pirate)

meta.path: ./Diabolical Plot of Dr. Dracupig, The (19xx)(The Southern Pirate).zip
meta.name: The Diabolical Plot of Dr. Dracupig
meta.desc:
The Diabolical Plot of Doctor Dracupig is an arcade shooter for the Apple II. Intelligence reports reveal that the mad scientist Dr. Dracupig has discovered a way to reanimate body parts and plans to use his bizarre creations to conquer the world. The player is tasked with stopping his monstrous army.
Players control a hypodermic needle that moves along the bottom of the screen and fires upward at wandering creatures. Each of the game's 10 levels consists of three combat rounds, with four monsters appearing in each round. Destroying at least one creature advances the player to the next round. New hazards are introduced as the game progresses, including barricades, moving obstacles, and enemy missiles.
The needle carries a limited supply of 150 shots, replenished after levels 3, 6, and 9. With only three lives available, players must survive all 10 levels to reach a special bonus stage and thwart Dr. Dracupig's scheme.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Diabolical Plot of Dr. Dracupig, The (19xx)(The Southern Pirate)-image.jpg

meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Diabolical Plot of Dr. Dracupig, The (19xx)(The Southern Pirate)-boxback.jpg

meta.name: Dig 'em
meta.desc:
Dig 'em is a 1-player Dig Dug clone for the Apple II.
The player needs to shovel their war through the dirt, and defeat the monsters which are found underground. Prohibiting this are two separate monster types, who will continually chase the player around. The player carries an air pump, which can be used to inflate the monsters to the point where they explode. Furthermore, rocks are scattered throughout the earth, which can be used to squash monsters. If the monsters do not find the player for several seconds, they will eventually get turned into ghosts, which are able to walk through the earth. They are invincible and cannot be killed. The player advances by killing all monsters on the level. The player has a total of 4 lives, and it's game over when all are lost.

meta.name: Dig Dug
meta.desc:
En Dig Dug, libra una guerra subterránea con tu fiel arpón. Tendrás que derrotar al duro Pooka y al feroz Fygar para avanzar al siguiente nivel.
Haz explotar a tus enemigos para que revienten o lánzales rocas. Conseguirás puntos por deshacerte de ellos o por recoger objetos de bonificación.
Este juego clásico es a la vez un juego de acción arcade de la vieja escuela y un juego de estrategia. ¡Atrae a tus enemigos hacia donde quieras que estén y aplástalos con las rocas!
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Dig Dug (1983)(Atarisoft)[48K]-manual.pdf

p: Dinosaur Dig (19xx)(CBS Software)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Apple Mafia][b].zip
n: Dinosaur Dig (19xx)(CBS Software)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Apple Mafia][b]

meta.name: Dinosaur Dig
meta.desc: Enlightening adventures of the prehistoric age! Explore the lost world of dinosaurs and test your knowledge with five exciting learning activities.

meta.name: Dnieper River Line
meta.desc: The Dnieper River Line is a fictionalized engagement between German and Russian forces in the Southern Ukraine in late 1943. As the Germans  you are challenged  to repel Russian efforts  to breach your critical defensive position. The Soviet units controlled by the computer seek to over run your thin German defensive line and capture sufficient objectives to assure victory. Dnieper River Line is a computer game which is played out board game style with a 8" x 11" mapboard and 240  5/8" illustrated counters.
meta.map: ./images/Dnieper River Line (1982)(Avalon Hill)-map.png

meta.name: Dominos
meta.desc: In spite of what the name might imply, Dominos is not an adaptation of the tile-laying game Dominoes, but in fact a multi-player Snake game. One player can take it up against the computer or play against another player. The snakes continuously increase in length, causing the screen to slowly fill up. The goal is to make the opponent run into something, which scores a point.

meta.name: Dragon Blaster
meta.desc: Dragon Blaster is a medieval-themed 1-player arcade game.nnnnThe player controls a small character, which can move horizontally on the bottom of the screen and fire vertically. In the background, an animated castle, dragon, and house can be seen. On each stage, a man on horseback, an alligator, and 4 birds which scroll horizontally across the screen appear. These creatures fire upon the player, and the player must avoid incoming shots. If the player shoots all four creatures, the player advances to the next stage, which is identical to the previous. The player has a total of 3 lives, and when all are lost, it's game over.

meta.name: Dragon's Eye, The
meta.desc: Dragons Eye is a fantasy RPG in which you are cast as a valiant warrior tasked with saving the lands by completing an epic quest. The lands in question are the "Seven Provinces" and the quest itself involves finding and retrieving the "Dragon's Eye" amulet before its evil power corrupts the entire countryside.

meta.name: Dragonfire
meta.desc: ¡Los tesoros del rey han sido robados! Como joven y audaz príncipe, tu objetivo es recuperarlos a todos. Los tesoros se guardan en diferentes castillos, cada uno custodiado por un dragón de fuego. Cada nivel del juego tiene dos partes, primero tienes que cruzar el puente levadizo del castillo. ¡Tendrás que saltar y esquivar las bolas de fuego de los dragones y evitar las flechas voladoras para llegar al otro lado! En la segunda parte, tienes que recoger todos los tesoros de la pantalla y ponerlos en un lugar seguro mientras evitas al dragón que corre por la parte inferior de la pantalla. Los dragones de cada nivel tienen diferentes patrones de movimiento y disparo, y a medida que avanzan los niveles se vuelven más rápidos y complicados.

p: Dragons of Hong Kong, The (1981)(Adventure International).zip
n: Dragons of Hong Kong, The (1981)(Adventure International)

meta.name: The Dragons of Hong Kong
meta.desc:
Dragons of Hong Kong is an early choose-your-own-adventure style interactive fiction game. Presented in black & white and entirely text-based, players are given branching story choices, with many wrong decisions leading to death. The game uses a context-sensitive parser that attempts to understand the player's intent by identifying key words and phrases in responses.
You are contacted by Nobel Prize-winning economist Dr. Goodman, who reveals that a secret religious organization called the Gregarines, led by Akbar D'Eville in Hong Kong, is deliberately causing worldwide inflation. Their goal is to destroy the value of global currencies so they can replace them with their own currency, the Liroon, and seize power. After Goodman is murdered with a jade dagger and you are framed for the crime, you must travel to Hong Kong, prove your innocence, and stop the Gregarines' sinister plot.

meta.name: Dragonworld
meta.desc: You have recieved word from a messenger that the "last dragon" has been kidnapped. You hold the scroll in your hand and read that you must travel with a special companion named Hawkwind. Only with him and yourself can you save the "last dragon" from never seeing the light of day again.

meta.name: Dreadnoughts
meta.desc: Dreadnoughts allows you to recreate all of the surface naval actions of the early years of WWII in the North Atlantic. The game is designed to be played on both the strategic and tactical levels by one or two players. In the single player game you will command the German Navy and attempt to wrest control of the seas from the British Royal Navy. Over 40 major ships are represented. You can recreate a historical battle or a what if scenario.

p: Dream Traveler Part 1 - The Legend of Singeom (1987)(-)(KR).zip
n: Dream Traveler Part 1 - The Legend of Singeom (1987)(-)(KR)

meta.path: ./Dream Traveler Part 1 - The Legend of Singeom (1987)(-)(KR).zip
meta.name: Dream Traveler Part 1 - The Legend of Singeom
meta.desc: Dream Traveler is the first Korean-made role-playing game, and also the first to fully use Korean script (Hangeul) for interaction. The game is heavily influenced by early Ultima games, having a very similar premise of a lone hero traveling to a fantasy land, where he meets the ruling king named after the programmer (in this case Inhwan Nam) and goes on a quest to rid the land of evil. Like in Ultima games, commands (including battle actions) are text-based. The player can freely roam the overhead map, and also gains access to faster transportation methods such as horses and ships.
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Dream Traveler Part 1 - The Legend of Singeom (1987)(-)(KR)-marquee.png

meta.name: Dung Beetles
meta.desc:
Sneak through unique maze after maze gobbling white dots while being hunted by the Mega-Bugs.  Their job is made easier because The Mouth you pilot leaves behind a trail of colored dots which they will follow relentlessly the moment they come upon it. This makes the maze's dead-ends truly treacherous.  Your job is made harder by the size of the maze (20x20), which requires a magnifier over your location to show the action, effectively creating a blind spot at a radius of three dots around you.  To succeed, you must watch not only where you're going, but scan each randomly generated maze to plan your route and avoid the Mega-Bugs.  If you do succeed, you're rewarded with a new maze and an additional Mega-Bug hunting you.  If you fail, the bugs dance gleefully and shout, "we gotcha!"
This ROM cartridge game was clearly inspired, but not limited, by Pac-Man.

meta.name: Dungeon Campaign - Wilderness Campaign
meta.desc: Released in 1978 for the Apple II, Dungeon Campaign is one of the earliest role-playing games for home computers, and in a way again, like Beneath Apple Manor of the same year, an obscure Rogue-like -- two years before Rogue actually coined the term. Dungeon Campaign is a basic fantasy dungeon crawl that doesn't even care about a background story -- enter dungeon, slay monsters, amass treasures, find exit. Not even a fabulous all-powerful item to be found here! Unusual is that you do not control a single character, but rather a fighting force of 25 men -- who, effectively, represent your "hit points".

p: E.T. Comes Back (1984)(Alliance Software)[cr Orient Express][m Nut Cracker].zip
n: E.T. Comes Back (1984)(Alliance Software)[cr Orient Express][m Nut Cracker]

meta.path: ./E.T. Comes Back (1984)(Alliance Software)[cr Orient Express][m Nut Cracker].zip
meta.name: E.T. Comes Back
meta.desc:
E.T. Comes Back is a brief unlicensed graphical adventure based upon the movie E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial.
The player plays as 10-year old Elliott, the young boy who had just helped E.T. return to his home. The player enters commands via VERB NOUN text commands, and can explore his home and the area immediately outside of the house. The player finds E.T. once again outside of his house in the shed, and can lure E.T. out with a geranium. The player briefly meets their brother and sister, whom appear to be characters from the comic Peanuts. The player then borrows their parents' car, and drives E.T. out to the rendezvous point with E.T.'s alien companions

meta.name: Eamon Adventure #001 - Main Hall & Beginners Cave (1990)(Brown, Donald)(US)(FW)[Eamon Utility Master by Andrews, Andy C.]
meta.desc: The Beginners Cave is an Eamon adventure written by Donald Brown and publicly released probably in late 1979. The adventure is the first of the Eamon series, Brown having included it on the original Eamon Master Diskette along with the Main Hall, and serves both as an introduction to the game for new players and as an easy means for beginner characters to obtain some modest treasure and experience.

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #002 - The Minotaur's Lair (1984-07-09)(Brown, Donald)(US)[Computer Learning Center Library].zip
meta.name: Eamon Adventure #002 - The Minotaur's Lair (1984-07-09)(Brown, Donald)(US)[Computer Learning Center Library]
meta.desc:
You've arranged to meet your girlfriend Larcenous Lil (or in the case of a female adventurer, your boyfriend Slippery Sven) in the town of Dunderhaven. When you get to town you learn that Lil, who arrived before you flat broke, tried to get some cash by burglarizing a nearby castle and hasn't been seen since.
You go to the castle after her, but once inside you're ambushed by bowmen under the command an ugly man on a throne who takes you for another thief and casts you down a shaft to meet the same fate as Lil. After a long drop and a "thud", you find yourself unhurt but with no way to climb back up the shaft, and so must venture into the tunnels beneath the castle.

Te dice que eres un aventurero especial preparado para un gran desafío, te entrega el grial con un guiño y desaparece. Bebes la poción y eres transportado mágicamente a una habitación de piedra tenuemente iluminada desde la que debes comenzar tus exploraciones.

p: Eamon Adventure #009 - Assault On the Clonemaster (1985-06-21)(Brown, Donald)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #009 - Assault On the Clonemaster (1985-06-21)(Brown, Donald)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #009 - Assault On the Clonemaster (1985-06-21)(Brown, Donald)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon Adventure #009 - Assault On the Clonemaster (1985-06-21)(Brown, Donald)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
You were walking away from the Main Hall when you felt that unpleasant wrench in your stomach again. You are in another universe. This time, you have your normal body and weapons. You just need to find your way back.
You came upon a group of people who knew of your plight. They are rebels, and can return you to your world. They will show you the way, if you will only first go with some of their men on a raid in the fortress of the dreaded Clone Master.
When they inform you that they will kill you if you don't go along, you agree.
Before the rebels will return you home, you must do two things — kill the Clone Master and destroy the device that keeps him in power — the Clonatorium!
Three of their men will accompany you.
As the game begins you're standing with a party of rebel bowmen near the Clone Master's mountain fortress.

p: Eamon Adventure #018 - Hogarth Castle (1980-10)(Nestle, Ken R.)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #018 - Hogarth Castle (1980-10)(Nestle, Ken R.)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #018 - Hogarth Castle (1980-10)(Nestle, Ken R.)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon Adventure #018 - Hogarth Castle (1980-10)(Nestle, Ken R.)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
Sam, my dear friend.
Thank you for coming.
I have summoned you here to beg for your help in rescuing my daughter, Rowina. She has been kidnapped and is being held for ransom by the evil Prince Hogarth.
I would gladly pay the ransom of ten chests of gold that he asks, if only I had it. However, since I do not have the gold, I must turn to you for help.
Since what I ask of you is so very dangerous, I will pay 1,000 pieces of gold for the safe return of my fair Rowina. This in addition to whatever treasure you can carry away from Hogarth Castle.
Now, my friend, the choice is yours. Will you attempt to rescue Fair Rowina from the castle of the evil Prince Hogath?
Remember the greeting 'howdy'. It is a code word that will identify you as my emissary in Hogarth Town.
Count Orloff is the one person inside the castle that you can trust. If you get into the castle, seek him out. He will help you.
God speed and good luck!

p: Eamon Adventure #023 - The Temple of Ngurct (1993-02-15)(Plamondon, James & Robert)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #023 - The Temple of Ngurct (1993-02-15)(Plamondon, James & Robert)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon Adventure #023 - The Temple of Ngurct (1993-02-15)(Plamondon, James & Robert)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
There is an old ruin of a small citadel on a cliff overlooking a rarely used pass. The road through the pass has become increasingly hazardous in recent years. Now, only rash and desperate travelers — or those travelling in very large parties — dare to use it. Those who have survived the crossing tell dark tales of fearsome beasts and strange happenings; in all these tales the citadel is the eye of the storm.
It is rumoured that the evil priests of Ngurct have occupied the citadel; their foul creed worships the black demon of the same name. Ngurct is the patron demon of exotic monsters — and he requires the regular sacrifice of human unbelievers.

meta.name: Eamon Adventure #025 - Nuclear Nightmare (1984-09-30)(Nelson, John)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
From the introduction:
You were just leaving the Main Hall about to investigate a rumor about a powerful new weapon that had been built by a local mad scientist.
As you walked out the door of the Main Hall, you met a man near hysteria. You calmed him down enough to get a more coherent story from him. He claims he is a technician in the employ of a mad scientist by the name of Dr. Applebaum.
He was contented to work in his lab until he overheard the doctor talking with a colleague. The colleague's name is Dr. Onstein. He is a specialist in some kind of unknown energy.

meta.name: Eamon 033 - The Orb of Polaris
meta.desc:
As you left the Main Hall, you felt that strange wrenching in your stomach and it seemed to make your vision go blurry. As soon as your vision clears again you see you have been teleported to the chamber of a mighty warlock!!
The warlock tells you he cast a spell to have the most highly qualified person in the land teleported to his chamber. That must mean you're the one!
He needs some assistance in obtaining an object that has been stolen from him. He claims it is an item of great power and consequence.

p: Eamon Adventure #036 - The Citadel of Blood (1993-11-14)(Hodson, Evan)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #036 - The Citadel of Blood (1993-11-14)(Hodson, Evan)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 036 - The Citadel of Blood
meta.desc:
"The place is cursed," said Olthen Bedwier about the Citadel of Blood. "I know of too many adventurers who set off for there and never came back." He drained his mug and changed the subject.
And yet you found yourself most curious about this strange place as you sat in the Main Hall with your comrades. You wanted to find out more...
The citadel, you discovered, was a huge castle that stood off quite a ways from town surrounded by forest. It was not known who built it or how it got there but everyone just accepted the fact that it was there.

p: Eamon Adventure #039 - Museum of Unnatural History (1985-11-15)(Volberding, Rick)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #039 - Museum of Unnatural History (1985-11-15)(Volberding, Rick)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #039 - Museum of Unnatural History (1985-11-15)(Volberding, Rick)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 039 - Museum of Unnatural History
meta.desc:
A short, stooped man walks up to your table and introduces himself as Professor Thomias X. Larkspur — a physicist.
"I was working on an interdimensional translocator at the university when my plans were stolen one night a few months ago. This morning I read in the paper that the palace police were baffled by the disappearance of the Eamon Museum of Natural History on 1st Street and Main Hall Avenue.
"My assistant, Yrn Zandro, a Loathian from the Eamon West Archipelago, is also gone. I believe he stole my plans, built a translocator, and warped the museum out of this dimension for evil purposes.
"I have finally finished my machine and located the dimension containing the museum. Unfortunately my translocator will only transport one person to the 'other side.' It is impossible to determine what effect the translocator had on the museum artifacts since other dimensions probably have different basic laws.
"I have talked to the museum board of directors and they said they will pay 5,000 GP for the return of the museum and also a reward for each artifact brought back.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Eamon Adventure #039 - Museum of Unnatural History (1985-11-15)(Volberding, Rick)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-image.jpg

n: Eamon Adventure #041 - The Caverns of Lanst (1995-06-21)(Volberding, Rick)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #041 - The Caverns of Lanst (1995-06-21)(Volberding, Rick)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 041 - Caverns of Lanst
meta.desc:
The noted archaeologist, Dr. Andrew Whitehead, walks up to your table in the Main Hall and asks if he could talk to you. Being between adventures and a little bored, you nod and invite him to sit down.
He says, "You have been well recommended as an experienced adventurer and I would like to hire you as a guide. I plan to explore the Caverns of Lanst in search of artifacts of the fabled Lanstians. My research indicates that the caverns were inhabited many centuries ago by a civilization of great capabilities! I've prepared a standard contract", and he passes over a legal-looking paper.

p: Eamon Adventure #045 - Swordquest (1992-09-13)(Pender, Roger)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #045 - Swordquest (1992-09-13)(Pender, Roger)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.name: Eamon 045 - Swordquest
meta.desc:
You are riding through King Arthur's kingdom seeking employment and adventure when your horse throws a shoe. You go to the blacksmith in Camelot and while there visit your old friend, Arthur.
You learn that disaster has befallen the kingdom. Morgan le Fay, the sorceress and Arthur's half-sister, has stolen his magical sword, Excalibur!
She has gathered allies and fled to her dreaded stronghold, Castle Dolorous where she is now plotting Arthur's destruction.
King Arthur asks if you will undertake the quest to return his wondrous weapon to its rightful owner?
You will have aid for many of the bold knights of the Round Table, aided by Merlin's magic, have infiltrated the dread fortress.
Arthur cautions you that this is a very dangerous quest; but the reward for success is equally great.
Arthur gives you one final piece of advice: Merlin may not recognize you. If he seems hostile when you meet, give him a weapon or gold to placate him.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Eamon Adventure #045 - Swordquest (1992-09-13)(Pender, Roger)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-manual.pdf

p: Eamon Adventure #046 - Life Quest (1985-05-15)(Crawford, David)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #046 - Life Quest (1985-05-15)(Crawford, David)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #046 - Life Quest (1985-05-15)(Crawford, David)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 046 - Lifequest
meta.desc:
You are sitting at the bar when the local centurion, a likable fellow, comes up to you. He buys you and him a mead to go, and tells you that he has been ordered to take you to the lord of the shire that the Main Hall is in.
As you and the centurion head over to the corral to get your horses, he gives you a useful briefing on the current situation, and why you are needed by the lord of the shire.
It seems that you are the most qualified adventurer in the Guild now, as others who have taken on this quest were too greedy, or disreputable to take on this quest. Those before you were either unsuccessful, or...
Well, you can draw your own conclusions on what became of them.

p: Eamon Adventure #049 - The Castle Kophinos (1993-09-28)(Doumakes, Don)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #049 - The Castle Kophinos (1993-09-28)(Doumakes, Don)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #049 - The Castle Kophinos (1993-09-28)(Doumakes, Don)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 049 - The Castle Kophinos
meta.desc:
The Castle Kophinos, once one of the most splendid houses in the land, has fallen upon hard times. Five years ago, it is said, the wealthy and reclusive Nikos Kophinos (owner of the castle) made an unheard-of trip to town. Furthermore, he was on foot, and was in a state of utter hysteria about "the devils" having taken over his castle. No one has visited the castle since — least of all Nikos, who is still "resting" at an insane asylum.
Naturally, this has hurt the Kophinos family reputation. Distant relatives have offered a reward to anyone who can "clean out" the castle completely. No one has collected.
The reward is more valuable than gold. The Kophinos family has commissioned the forging of a magical sword, a sword of awesome power and breathtaking beauty.
The sword has no name. It will be named by the adventurer who wins it.
But the family is very insistent that the castle be completely cleared of malevolent beings. If even one remains, you forfeit the reward.

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #050 - Behind the Sealed Door (1993-11-14)(Berge, Tim)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 050 - Behind the Sealed Door
meta.desc:
Hearing legends about a huge city, filled with strange, powerful weapons and priceless treasures, you make your way to a little used path going east.
From what you've heard, many adventurers have sought out this city only to never be seen again; some have even said (in the Main Hall) that each adventurer might've been the victim of foul play... though from whom no one is quite sure.
