meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #051 - The Caves of Eamon Bluff (19xx)(Berge, Tim)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 051 - The Caves of Eamon Bluff
meta.desc:
As you leave the Main Hall, a white horse comes to you and seems to nod in your direction. As you draw close to it you hear it say, "My master desires your services, come with me," and beckons for you to climb up.
The horse takes you for some distance to the south before stopping at a group of bushes, just in front of a large cave. As you get down from the horse, you wait for about a minute.

p: Eamon Adventure #066 - Dungeons of Xenon (1985-07-04)(Bhayani, Sam)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #066 - Dungeons of Xenon (1985-07-04)(Bhayani, Sam)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #066 - Dungeons of Xenon (1985-07-04)(Bhayani, Sam)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 066 - Dungeons of Xenon
meta.desc:
As you walk out of the Main Hall you see a beggar. He comes up to you and says, "An evil man is building up an army and is planning to try and take over the Eamon Main Hall. I was once a prisoner in his concentration camp. I escaped and came to the Main Hall to warn a brave and loyal Free Adventurer like you. You must stop this evil man. His name: Xenor. The way to find him is that you must venture into the Dungeons of Xenon. The dungeon leads to his hide-out. You go past the dungeon and through the Forest-woods of Eldar. Along the way you will meet 5 friends. Their names be Sir Angadon the knight, Lady Amrat the barbarian, Prince Castillon the son-of-a -err, the son of the king I mean, and Destroyer the ogre. Now remember, you must kill Xenor. Also remember that he has a whole army. The Main Hall is at stake and we're all depending on you. Good luck and Hermes' speed on your quest."
The beggar leaves and you realize that this will be one of the toughest quests of your lifetime.

meta.desc:
You head into the fog and feel the grip of strong magic. There is a hideous laugh and then you open your eyes, as if you had been sleeping, and you are back at the Main Hall.
Do you go B)ack the way you came, T)hrough the cemetery or I)nto the fog ?

p: Eamon Adventure #073 - The Deep Canyon (1984-10-07)(Blincoe, Kenn)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #073 - The Deep Canyon (1984-10-07)(Blincoe, Kenn)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #073 - The Deep Canyon (1984-10-07)(Blincoe, Kenn)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 073 - The Deep Canyon
meta.desc:
After three days of traveling you are on the last leg of your journey.
nnYou are walking on a high plateau. There are a few scattered trees and a lot of thick, dry grass. You can see a dense forest far up ahead. Off in the distance you can hear the rumble of rushing water.
nnYou come across a mysterious man in a light-weight robe, to whom you enquire about the deep canyon. The man replies, "I'll show you how to get there, but be forewarned. It'll be up to you to find your way out. It is said that Elzod, the local wizard, keeps some of his pets down there to prevent adventurers from walking off with his valuables. Now follow me..."nnnnYou followed him for a while, and watched him grin as he pushed you off a cliff.
nnFortunately, someone cast a power spell for you on the way down. The spell slowed your fall, but it was still a painful landing. You now get up to look around and to see how badly you're hurt.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Eamon Adventure #073 - The Deep Canyon (1984-10-07)(Blincoe, Kenn)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-image.jpg
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Eamon Adventure #073 - The Deep Canyon (1984-10-07)(Blincoe, Kenn)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-marquee.png
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Eamon Adventure #073 - The Deep Canyon (1984-10-07)(Blincoe, Kenn)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-manual.pdf

p: Eamon Adventure #074 - Dharmaquest (1989-11-05)(Pender, Roger)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #074 - Dharmaquest (1989-11-05)(Pender, Roger)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.name: Eamon 074 - Dharmaquest
meta.desc: "Sam," said Hokas in low, measured tones; "although a great warrior, you are a selfish, self-centered fool! Only my sense of honor and discipline prevent me from striking you dead where you stand. Instead, I will send you on a quest to the Land of Dharma. Perhaps you will learn to help others and not think only of yourself. Perhaps you may even find and conquer the warrior within."
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Eamon Adventure #074 - Dharmaquest (1989-11-05)(Pender, Roger)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-manual.pdf

You may leave the temple with permanently elevated hardiness, agility, or charisma, for example. Perhaps you will choose a new name. There are opportunities to win gold, and several ways to spend or lose it. There are also a few ways to get killed.

meta.desc: Baron Hador had thought that there was something odd about that man who had collapsed in front of his castle. But, being the kind soul that he is, he took the man into his castle to spend the night. The next morning it struck him — the man looked a great deal like Jeeves, the butler of Count Fuey. No one trusted the count or any of his men.

p: Eamon Adventure #080 - The Search for the Key (1984-11-03)(Brown, Donald)(US)[f standard conversion John Nelson][Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #080 - The Search for the Key (1984-11-03)(Brown, Donald)(US)[f standard conversion John Nelson][Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #080 - The Search for the Key (1984-11-03)(Brown, Donald)(US)[f standard conversion John Nelson][Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 080 - The Search for the Key
meta.desc:
You just were selected to undertake a very dangerous mission for the King. Several high officials were kidnapped from their beds last week. Your job — rescue them.
nnThe King's warlock has devised a plan that should make your job nearly easy. However, for it to succeed, you must first possess the fabled Key of Molinar! The key is a magical teleportation device. If someone holds it, then says the name of someone in sight who is willing, the person named is zapped to the speaker's home. This can also work for the speaker.
nnNobody knows where the key currently lies. However, the warlock has a spell that will teleport you to the key's vicinity. Getting back is up to you!nnnnYou are advised by the warlock to get the key as soon as possible, and use it to return. If you succeed at this leg of your assignment, a handsome reward will be included in your booty.

meta.name: Eamon 084 - Castle of Riveneta
meta.desc:
As you prepare for your next journey, a small young man comes up from around a building. He tells you of a castle to the north of town which was abandoned by the royal government when the Queen Rivineta mysteriously died in her bedroom. When he finishes his story of the castle he quickly crams a map into your hand and says, "Use this here piece of paper to find yer way to the castle yonder to the north."
Before you can say a word he dashes off to wherever he came from. You study the map for awhile and then decide to adventure in the castle.

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #089 - Super Fortress of Lin Wang (1986-01-12)(Bhayani, Sam)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 089 - Super Fortress of Lin Wang
meta.desc: As you walk out of the Main Hall, you see an orb. Just as you touch it the world shifts. You are now in Japan. A tyrant, Lin Wang, recently seized power there, by force. He built a huge fortress, so that no one could harm him. You must avoid his karate experts (ranging from white to black belt), his samurai and ninja guards, captains, and generals, and you must avoid his oriental and Komodo dragons. Finally you must kill Lin Wang and escape his fortress.

meta.desc: You dream you are floating in the lake near Eamon Hall. You hear a soft voice droning in your ear in some strange language. The words gradually become recognizable and you slowly open your eyes. However the floating sensation persists. You are strapped loosely into an acceleration hammock which is swaying gently. A young Terran marine drifts up to you and salutes smartly. (A difficult feat in 0 g.) He informs you that the captain, Lt. Commander Antare, requests your presence immediately.

p: Eamon Adventure #094 - Blood Feud (1989-07-27)(Krebs, Rick)(US)[f speedups][Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #094 - Blood Feud (1989-07-27)(Krebs, Rick)(US)[f speedups][Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #094 - Blood Feud (1989-07-27)(Krebs, Rick)(US)[f speedups][Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon Adventure #094 - Blood Feud (1989-07-27)(Krebs, Rick)(US)[f speedups][Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
While sitting at the bar at the local pub, you are approached by a gnome wearing a blue wool hat, glasses and smoking a cigarette.
nnHe says, "Ah, here you are! So you're the brave warrior who has volunteered for this hazardous job? I've been told to fill you in on Blood Feud Castle and the rivalry between the Pozzis and Spanners."nnnnThree  hours later he concludes his history of Blood Feud, the Pozzis and the Spanners.
nnHe now gets to his point. "The evil Suedo Guru has stolen the Rod of Stupidity from the royal depository and he has taken up residence at Blood Feud Castle. Should you succeed in recovering the rod you will be given the title of Knight of the Realm, and all will call you 'Sir'. You may keep all the possessions you find, and that includes an amulet that has the power of increasing all your attributes!! Since this mission is quite difficult you will have the advantage of leaving the castle and returning to the Main Hall to heal, without disturbing the castle."
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Eamon Adventure #094 - Blood Feud (1989-07-27)(Krebs, Rick)(US)[f speedups][Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-manual.pdf

meta.desc: Welcome to The Slave Pits of Kzorland. In this adventure you will enter the slave pits in the hope of getting to the treasury vault. meta.desc: One day while you are hanging around the Main Hall you receive a letter. It says that Heinrich, an acquaintance of yours from an earlier adventure, has died, and left you an inheritance. p: Eamon Adventure #112 - Hills of History (1988-06-16)(Smith, Dave)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #112 - Hills of History (1988-06-16)(Smith, Dave)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #112 - Hills of History (1988-06-16)(Smith, Dave)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 112 - Hills of History
meta.desc:
You have been up late for several nights in a row studying for your final exams at the College of Wizards and Wanderers. If you blow this test you'll be out for good. Your history test is the one that really worries you. There are so many names and deeds to learn.
nnYou suddenly remember an endurance potion given to you by a senior you once did a favor for. It smells awful, but you remember your predicament, hold your nose, and take a large swallow.
nnToo late you read the warning on the label. "Do not use after March 18, 1473." As the room begins to spin, your mind tries to focus on all of the facts you have been reading about. You must find your way out of this nightmare so that you can study some more for your test. Learn what you can while you fight history's villains and search for the treasures of our past.
nnThe room spins faster and you blackout. You are floating down, down, down until you land gently on a nearby road.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Eamon Adventure #112 - Hills of History (1988-06-16)(Smith, Dave)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-image.jpg

n: Eamon Adventure #116 - The Iron Prison (1992-09-06)(Ruby, Sam)(US)[f speedups][Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #116 - The Iron Prison (1992-09-06)(Ruby, Sam)(US)[f speedups][Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon 116 - The Iron Prison
meta.desc:
As a war is brewing, you are summoned by Manwe, King of the Valar, for a special mission. You must infiltrate Angband and retrieve the Silmarils. Of course, this is extremely dangerous, as Morgoth has a large number of monsters at his command. And none of those who have entered Angband have come out alive.
You will not be without help. Manwe sends his herald to help you, and Este, the goddess of healing, gives you a healing potion. It can only be used once, so use it wisely!
You travel for days through the forests until you reach the edge. In the distance you see Thangorodrim, and the entrance to Angband.

meta.desc: You are about to face your most trying task. A powerful and Irish landlady has raised the rent. Your friends are being evicted. You must help your friends! It is up to you. You must enter the Mattimoe Palace, defeat the landlady, and recover the rent cheques. However, Mrs Mattimoe does not like just anyone to barge in on her. She has set many obstacles to overcome before you find her. You will undertake many tasks, but this is probably the most difficult. Good luck to you.

meta.desc: And now you find yourself in a cold, desolate country, where the people are hard, the diversions few. p: Eamon Adventure #159 - The Bridge of Catzad-Dum (1992-05-18)(Segerlind, Nathan)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #159 - The Bridge of Catzad-Dum (1992-05-18)(Segerlind, Nathan)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #159 - The Bridge of Catzad-Dum (1992-05-18)(Segerlind, Nathan)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon Adventure #159 - The Bridge of Catzad-Dum (1992-05-18)(Segerlind, Nathan)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
Eres un hábil guerrero samurái al servicio de Tokugawa Nobunaga, un sabio y noble shogun que vivía en el castillo Azuchi en Japón. Nobunaga te entrenó en los métodos del bushido, te equipó con armas y armaduras y te enseñó a realizar los tres secretos pero devastadores golpes de espada de la muerte: el golpe desviador, la tenia escarlata y el golpe de hélice de motor fueraborda giratorio.
Después de un enfrentamiento reciente en el que aniquilaste al clan rival Takeda, le preguntaste a tu maestro si podías tomar unas breves vacaciones para visitar a tu hermano, y Nobunaga aceptó felizmente, diciendo que ningún enemigo se atrevería a atacar después de la caída de Takeda. A tu regreso al castillo te sorprende encontrarlo en ruinas, lleno de los restos del colosal ejército que lo atacó. Buscando entre los escombros encuentras la cabeza cortada de tu maestro, ¡y te sorprende descubrir que aún puede conversar! Nobunaga comparte los nombres y ubicaciones de quienes lo atacaron, y tú sales en busca de venganza.

meta.name: Eamon Adventure #168 - The High School of Horrors (1989-03-09)(Haney, Michael - Hunt, Aaron)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
You find yourself at the entrance to a very old high school: St. Marquis de Sade. It has no windows or other exits that you can see. The only door is the one before you. You recall what you've been told: the senior class is missing, a guest speaker gone, and the whole school overtaken by horrifying creatures.
Feeling a sense of duty, you enter the school. A cold shiver runs up your spine as you realize the doors have locked behind you. Your adventure has begun. Good luck, you'll need it!

meta.desc: It's the year 5688 and two interstellar empires — the Star League and the Procyon Confederation — are locked in a centuries-long battle for supremacy that's now playing out on the planet Apollonia. You're an ex-military engineer working on the Star League world of Varcar when you get the message that your military commission has been reinstated and you're needed to aid your side in the battle. After intensive training you join the elite Black Phoenix mercenary unit and now being dropped into a firebase on Apollonia to meet Colonel Tanz who'll give you your mission objectives.

n: Eamon Adventure #184 - Quest for Orion (1989-09-12)(Gise, P.E.)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #184 - Quest for Orion (1989-09-12)(Gise, P.E.)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon Adventure #184 - Quest for Orion (1989-09-12)(Gise, P.E.)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
The good King of Eamon summons you to the castle. He tells you of an unhappy kingdom, far away, where a mysterious plague caused all their animal life to die. They have sent a kindly emissary to Eamon to negotiate the restocking of their stricken kingdom.
nnDuring the negotiations all the animal life on Eamon mysteriously disappeared. The emissary was found in his chambers — in a deep sleep.
nnYou have been selected as the most trustworthy and cunning of the warriors. It is your moral obligation to venture forth, find, and return our missing animals so that Eamon can be happy once again. It is a dangerous task. Any delay could be deadly to our purloined pets, beasts, and other creatures!nnnnThe citizens of Eamon know this is a difficult and dangerous mission. They have come to wish you "good luck", especially the children.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Eamon Adventure #184 - Quest for Orion (1989-09-12)(Gise, P.E.)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Eamon Adventure #190 - Shift Change at Grimmwax (1990-01-13)(Sparks, David & Andrea)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
As you leave the Main Hall, a dark gentleman sidles up to you.
nn"Arrgh," he says, "there's them hereabouts that would pay plenty to know what goes on at the back building of the Grimmwax Waxworks! I wonder, are you [man/woman] enough to go find out? Of course, getting past the guards will be your first problem. And your last! Har! Har! Har!"nnnnHis cold, sardonic laughter lingers in your mind as you close the door on the Main Hall and step out into the mid-afternoon sun.

p: Eamon Adventure #192 - Mean Streets (1990-02-01)(Tetirick, Thomas)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #192 - Mean Streets (1990-02-01)(Tetirick, Thomas)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #192 - Mean Streets (1990-02-01)(Tetirick, Thomas)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon Adventure #192 - Mean Streets (1990-02-01)(Tetirick, Thomas)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
Welcome to Mean Streets. Your task is a valiant one. It seems that in the last year, a pimp has moved in on our fair city. He has full intention of making it his own. To ensure his wishes he has kidnapped my only daughter "Tina". He intends to turn her into one of his prostitutes.
nnAs mayor of this city I give you full power to rid our city of this vile man (pimp) by any means you see fit. You will be needing help so feel free to visit all the shops and businesses and try to recruit people to aid you in your quest. nnnnYou're going to need as much charisma as possible to win this one, and who knows — you may come out of this a very rich man/woman. Also it is rumored that a huge thug is hiding out in the subway killing people at any given chance. We would very much appreciate his extermination. Also be on the lookout for thieves, they are know to be lurking when you least expect them.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Eamon Adventure #192 - Mean Streets (1990-02-01)(Tetirick, Thomas)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Eamon Adventure #193 - The Creature of Rhyl (1990-02-09)(Knuth, Kevin - Parker, Robert)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc: The land of Rhyl has been raided nightly by a large creature that flies out of the mountains near the city of Asereht and steals livestock and the occasional farmer — and now has made off with much of the kingdom's royal treasure and King Namreh's son Prince Laechim. The king believes the creature may be under the control of a mad magician named Astylis who lives in the mountains and has commissioned several parties to seek him out and rescue the prince but so far none have succeeded. The mission is now yours to complete.

meta.name: Eamon Adventure #195 - The Training Ground (1990-02-13)(Hewgley, Chas)(US)[f DOS 3.3 TJZ][Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
You hear there's a place outside of town where beginning adventurers can gather loot and improve their skills, so you hitchhike to the site, ready your weapon, and venture in. A pamphlet laying inside the entrance gives the following welcome:
Welcome to the Barrow Downs Training Ground!
Well, you've been to the Beginner's Cave, but do you really feel ready to tackle that big world out there on your own? Maybe you don't, and that's why we've set up this practice facility for adventurers of little or no experience. To challenge you, the complex is stocked daily with beasts, non-humans, criminals, and other perils, not to mention $TREASURE$. NOTE: The adventurer enters at their own risk! The management assumes no legal responsibility for the safety of the adventurer. Good luck!

meta.name: Eamon 200 - The Lost Isle
meta.desc: The Lost Isle begins with the adventurer overhearing a pair of wizards debating the existence of an island off the eastern shore of a local sea. They suggest that this island was the island home of the wizard Castanamir, who disappeared along with his home—and treasure—two hundred years prior to the setting of the adventure. The wizards, satisfied that the island exists, set off to loot it; naturally, the adventurer recruits a pair of mercenaries—a dwarf and an elf—to accompany him or her, plotting to beat the pair of wizards to the island.

p: Eamon Adventure #201 - The Caverns of Vanavara (1990-04-29)(Hewgley, Chas)(US)[f DOS conversion, speedups TJZ][Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #201 - The Caverns of Vanavara (1990-04-29)(Hewgley, Chas)(US)[f DOS conversion, speedups TJZ][Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.name: Eamon Adventure #201 - The Caverns of Vanavara (1990-04-29)(Hewgley, Chas)(US)[f DOS conversion, speedups TJZ][Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
The king's beautiful daughter, Heleena, was abducted by a vile, scummy thief type person by the name of Sampson Gremonis. Rumor has it that he is holding her for ransom in an abandoned fort located somewhere in the Cunnamulla Foothills. The fort (Vanavara Outpost) is unusual in that it is built into the side of a hill, and is, therefore, totally underground. One village elder has told of an old secret hideout under the fort itself that the kidnappers may be staying in.
Rumor also has it that Gremonis has a couple of friends with him: one a powerful warrior, the other a necromancer, an evil mage with the power to raise the dead and use them as his slaves. Your mission is to enter the fort, find the secret hideout, rescue the princess, apprehend Gremonis and bring him back dead or alive. You will get 2000 GP for the safe return of Heleena. For bringing back Gremonis, you will get 2000 GP if he is alive or 1000 GP for his dead body.
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Eamon Adventure #201 - The Caverns of Vanavara (1990-04-29)(Hewgley, Chas)(US)[f DOS conversion, speedups TJZ][Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-marquee.png

p: Eamon Adventure #208 - Assault on Helstar (1991-09-15)(Schulz, Phil)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #208 - Assault on Helstar (1991-09-15)(Schulz, Phil)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.path: ./Eamon Adventure #208 - Assault on Helstar (1991-09-15)(Schulz, Phil)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
meta.name: Eamon Adventure #208 - Assault on Helstar (1991-09-15)(Schulz, Phil)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
Recently, there have been rumors of a large enemy city somewhere in the Eamon world. The city is supposed to be the legendary city of Helstar. You don't know if you believe this or not. The last recorded sighting of Helstar was over 1000 years ago near a small African village.
nnLegend has it that Helstar can only be destroyed by "the two jewels". nnnnBoth of the jewels have been guarded by powerful people for millennia. One of them is in a cave somewhere, inside an indestructible magical field. The other one was in a wizard's chest but about a year ago it was stolen. If you do not get it back, Helstar will be destroyed anyway... except that you'll still be in the city when it happens. And then you'll vaporize too.
nnYou will be transported to somewhere near the place where Helstar is rumored to be. Then you're on your own. Good luck.

meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Eamon Adventure #208 - Assault on Helstar (1991-09-15)(Schulz, Phil)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]-marquee.png

meta.desc: In the Main Hall you meet a strange pair of adventurers, a dwarf named Dworkin and Lightfoot, an elf. They tell you of a quest that they are about to start on in the city of Griswald. The jewel that serves as the eye of the statue of the god Barantu has been stolen and without its magic protection the ancient city will soon fall under the control of the evil magician Arach. Your friends plan to find the gem and to try to obtain the release of Griswald from his grasp. They ask you to help them in thier quest. Realizing that there is probably gold to be had in this venture, you accept their invitation.

p: Eamon Adventure #226 - Bookworm 3-D (1994-07-30)(Parker, Robert)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild].zip
n: Eamon Adventure #226 - Bookworm 3-D (1994-07-30)(Parker, Robert)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]

meta.name: Eamon Adventure #226 - Bookworm 3-D (1994-07-30)(Parker, Robert)(US)[Eamon Adventurer's Guild]
meta.desc:
The Bookworm, is a criminal responsible for decimating libraries across Eamon, including the Eamon Public Library. Encounter and Revenge each center around the adventurer seeking to capture the Bookworm at the behest of Hokas Tokas. 3-D is a direct sequel to Revenge, picking up precisely where Revenge concludes. After defeating and slaying the Bookworm, the ending of Revenge relates that the adventurer is charged with returning to Des Moines, Iowa to retrieve the corpse of the Bookworm, only to discover a monographed neckerchief in place of the Bookworm's body and concludes with the Bookworm still at large.
Bookworm 3-D begins with rumors of odd occurrences at The Beginners Cave and suspicions that it may be the fugitive Bookworm behind them. With the task of investigating and capturing the Bookworm, the adventurer thus enters the cave in pursuit.

meta.desc:
The adventure begins with the player's vacation being interrupted by a messenger from a Princess Catherine, entreating him or her to search for Mack. The "Mack" after which the adventure is searching is a Himalayan tricolor cat named McKenzie who has been taken hostage by the forces of the rival nation Sicilia. The ransom's being double of the Princess's royal treasury, Catherine has selected the adventurer to infiltrate Castle Charlast, home of the king and queen of Sicilia, in an attempt to rescue the feline.
nnThe adventurer is promised 5,000 gold pieces for the safe return of McKenzie. Though, as Zuchowski notes, the gold is never produced (some carnal knowledge of Princess Catherine is offered instead), the adventure is rare in providing detailed endings for both the case in which the player succeeds and that in which the adventurer fails.

n: Earthquake - San Francisco 1906 (1982)(Adventure International)(II+)(Side A)[b][48K][Apple Other-Ventures #4]

meta.name: Earthquake San Francisco 1906
meta.desc: Earthquake San Francisco 1906 is the fourth game in the Other Venture series, again by Jyym Pearson. This text adventure takes you back to April 19, 1906, San Francisco. One of the most violent earthquakes in recent history hit early that morning. Researched and historically accurate - the city is aflame, and you must escape and survive.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Earthquake - San Francisco 1906 (1982)(Adventure International)(II+)(Side A)[b][48K][Apple Other-Ventures #4]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Echelon
meta.desc: Take a bit of Wing Commander, and mix some of the fanciful parts of modern aircraft sims and you'll have an idea of what this game is like. Echelon puts the player in combat over vast landscapes and around cities as a member of an elite fighting force facing down an invasion. Battle takes place in a variety of aircraft with futuristic abilities using a variety of weapons. There is a branching campaign of around 50 missions, and over the course of play you can advance from Lieutenant to Commander and have greater control over how each mission plays out.

n: Eidolon, The (1986)(Epyx)(II+)[cr Lot - The Mad Mechanic][64K]

meta.name: The Eidolon
meta.desc: The Eidolon was one of two games that were part of Lucasfilm Games' second wave in December 1985. The other was Koronis Rift. Both took advantage of the fractal technology developed for Rescue on Fractalus!,further enhancing it. In The Eidolon, Rescue's fractal mountains were turned upside down and became the inside of a cave.

p: Elfhelm's Bane v1.0 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)(Side A).zip
n: Elfhelm's Bane v1.0 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)(Side A)

meta.name: Elfhelm's Bane
meta.desc: The Realm of Angbar: Elfhelm's Bane is a text-based RPG for the Apple II. Players arrive in the city of Elfhelm, where a sinister priesthood devoted to the evil Wormlord has established a temple at the city's center. As a warrior, priest, or mage, the player explores locations such as the mall, arena, clanhouse, pawnshop, and palace while battling hostile townsfolk and growing hordes of enemies. Combat and exploration are entirely text-driven, with experience and gold used to gain levels through costly training. The game features a single quest: defeating the Wormlord, though victory provides no reward or ending text. Elfhelm's Bane was also intended to support additional adventure scenarios, with inaccessible entrances to these unfinished expansions scattered throughout the city.

meta.name: The Eliminator
meta.desc: Esencialmente un juego básico de shoot-em-up, Eliminator te pone a cargo de una nave experimental flotante, recorriendo un gauntlet de varios circuitos, esquivando obstáculos y haciendo explotar oponentes en un intento de llegar al final antes de que una bomba a bordo te haga explotar y te saque del juego.nnnnA medida que avanzas en el juego, obtienes las opciones estándar de mejoras y los circuitos se vuelven más difíciles de navegar.

meta.name: Elite
meta.desc:
Pilota tu nave espacial Cobra en un fantástico viaje de descubrimientos y aventuras, una prueba suprema de tus habilidades de combate, navegación y emprendimiento.
La mayor parte del juego consiste en viajar a diferentes sistemas estelares, comerciar con sus habitantes, ganar dinero y reputación. También se puede ganar dinero mediante misiones militares, caza de recompensas, minería de asteroides, ..... Cuando el personaje del jugador gana dinero, puede mejorar sus naves con mejores armas, escudos, mayor capacidad de carga, sistema de acoplamiento automático, etc.
Independientemente de cómo gane el dinero, por las buenas o por las malas, tendrá que correr por el espacio luchando contra naves piratas y alienígenas hostiles.

p: Empire - Wargame of the Century (1987)(Interstel)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)[cr Glitch].zip
n: Empire - Wargame of the Century (1987)(Interstel)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)[cr Glitch]

meta.path: ./Empire - Wargame of the Century (1987)(Interstel)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)[cr Glitch].zip
meta.name: Empire : Wargame Of The Century
meta.desc: The object of the game is simple - gain control of the entire world by using your armies to crush your opponents. You'll start off with a city, which must then produce armies, that are used to conquer more cities. The array of unit types is limited to eight, including various ships, fighter planes, ground units and a submarine. This is more than made up for by the significantly different abilities each of the units possess, and the game will mostly likely appeal to the Chess and Risk player's of the world.

meta.name: Empire I World Builders
meta.desc: The gateway to the New York Rocket Field is your first step of a space voyage in which you may choose one of three career paths to follow, here at the dawn of the interstellar Empire.  Homesteaders must find a hospital planet on which to farm, raise a family and conquer the environment.  Miners must find a high-density planet in a search for material wealth.  Missionaries seek out populated planets to preach to the masses.  Each path into the space frontier has its dangers, and a misstep may have bloody consequences.

meta.name: Empire of the Over-Mind
meta.desc:
In starting Empire of the Over-Mind within a few short steps you are on a forest path with a red planet overhead, relishing the choice between a mountain trail and a cave. Adventure stretches out before you, forty characters of text per line, or maybe sixty.  As in all text adventures, that particular stretch of forest path in Empire of the Over-Mind may become over-familiar before you get yourself to the right place with the right items to travel finally to the red planet. 
Empire of the Over-Mind departs from typical text adventures, though, in its movement. Cardinal directions are not used. Instead, one references the avenue of movement: the road, door, or even cliff-face which leads to the next location. Also unusual is the ability to look ahead -- e.g. down the road -- and see what is there before moving. The objects you see ahead can vary from game to game: sometimes the key glints enticingly while the skeleton hides in the shadows, other times you can only see the key if you bravely confront the skeleton. Menacing though the perils are, your mistakes are gently handled: even the crocodile only "clobbers" you.
Typical of the era, the parser handles two-word verb-object combinations, or three words if an adjective is needed. Depending on the platform, item locations can vary from start to start, and getting clobbered means you have to hunt them all down again. Necessary clues are hidden in a long, printed poem which comes with the game. Avalon Hill's instructions are a fascinating artifact themselves. Besides the profoundly basic level -- covering the "prompt character," the back-space key, and the exigencies of programming in 48K or less -- a memory map is offered. No simple "Save" command or hotkey here: a little assembly language saves the game state.

meta.name: Escape From Arcturus
meta.desc: Escape from Arcturus is essentially two games in one. There is the Space Fortress game and there is the Escape game. In the Space Fortress game you play the commander of the last remaining defense satellite which, naturally, is surrounded by aggressive aliens bent on destroying you and your outpost

meta.name: Escape From Rungistan
meta.desc:
Mientras intentabas colarte por la frontera de Rungistán, te han capturado y te han metido en la cárcel. Deberá utilizar todas las herramientas a su alcance para escapar y emprender el largo y peligroso viaje de vuelta a través de la frontera.
Escape From Rungistan describe tus aventuras con gráficos en blanco y negro y breves descripciones de texto. La tecla RETURN permite alternar entre texto y gráficos. A pesar de su aspecto general de juego de aventuras, algunos de los puzles tienen lugar en tiempo real y hay algunas secuencias cortas de estilo arcade, como cuando tienes que esquiar colina abajo esquivando los árboles que se interponen en tu camino.

meta.name: The Escape From Traam
meta.desc: Escape from Traam is the third game in the Other Venture series, by Jyym Pearson. It uses an adapted version of the Scott Adams adventure engine used by the main adventure series. In this one, your space cruiser has crash-landed on a bizarre distant world, Traam. This dangerous world is replete with wonders human eyes have never beheld. Your goal - to escape home, alive.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Escape From Traam, The (1981)(Adventure International)[cr]-manual.pdf

p: Expedition Amazon (1983)(Penguin Software)(II+)(Side A)[cr Black Bag].zip
n: Expedition Amazon (1983)(Penguin Software)(II+)(Side A)[cr Black Bag]

meta.name: Expedition Amazon
meta.desc:
Expedition Amazon te pone al mando de un equipo de 4 exploradores en una expedición para mapear y estudiar (saquear) las ruinas de la civilización Inca. Su equipo está formado por un médico, un ayudante de campo, un operador de radio y un guardia. Después de equipar a su grupo con todos los suministros necesarios, viajará a su campamento base en el borde de la selva donde comenzará su viaje.
Rápidamente se encontrará con muchos peligros de la selva mientras explora, incluyendo nativos hostiles, mosquitos, enfermedades, animales salvajes y más. Cada uno de los miembros de tu equipo tiene una clasificación "EL" que indica lo cerca que están de la muerte. La muerte llega rápidamente para los nuevos miembros del equipo, pero a medida que tu equipo gana experiencia (mapeando más y más las ruinas amazónicas e incas), tu puntuación EL hace que tu equipo tenga más éxito.

Dispone de todas las sofisticadas herramientas de la guerra aérea electrónica moderna, incluidos el sistema de puntería asistida por ordenador y la pantalla de visualización. El armamento a tu disposición incluye ametralladoras, misiles guiados y bombas.

n: Fahrenheit 451 (1984)(Trillium)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)[cr Nocturnal Gnome]

meta.name: Fahrenheit 451
meta.desc:
Based on Ray Bradbury's classic science fiction novel Fahrenheit 451.
In a not so distant future, books have become illegal. As Fireman Guy Montag, the player's role is not to save houses, but to burn them for the books inside. However, Guy becomes passionate about books and becomes a rebel, pursued by the authorities. With the help of the Underground, he must survive and save books from complete extinction.
The game acts a sequel to Bradbury's novel.  Following the imposition of martial law Montag finds the young woman who inspired his resistance to the established order.  With her help he can now track down 34 microcassettes which hold the contents of the New York Public Library, then pass them on to underground members who will memorise the texts.

meta.name: Fall Gelb
meta.desc:
Fall Gelb: The Fall of France is a strategy game featuring Nazi Germany's 1940 invasion of France.
Designed for 1 - 2 players, each player controls the major land forces of either the Axis or Allied players. A computer opponent is available for 1-player games. The viewpoint is from a corps level commander, and the player assigns movement and combat tasks for those groups, and responds to operational events.
The game is text-only, and came with a board and physical pieces to allow the player to keep track of the location of theirs and enemy units. All games begin in on May 10, 1940, and game time lasts 10 - 20 turns, with each turn encompassing 2 days time. Each break allows the player to input new commands for their forces, but will stop to allow the player to react to enemy movements. Each phase has an intelligence gathering component and a second component where troop orders can be given. Different nations will be taken out of the war based upon the progression of historical events

p: Fantastic Four, The v1.0 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)(Disk 1 of 2).zip
n: Fantastic Four, The v1.0 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)(Disk 1 of 2)

meta.path: ./Fantastic Four, The v1.0 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)(Disk 1 of 2).zip
meta.name: Fantastic Four, The v1.0 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)(Disk 1 of 2)
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Fantastic Four, The v1.0 (1986)(Green Valley Publishing)(Disk 1 of 2)-marquee.png

meta.name: Fastgammon
meta.desc: Fastgammon is a computer adaptation of the board game Backgammon. The game follows the rules of the board game as can be expected with one player competing against an AI opponent, both attempting to move all their pieces of the board. It's entirely played with keyboard and to do a move the player simply enters the number of the starting point followed with the number of the destination. To move a piece of the bar the player enters B followed by the number of the destination point. To bear off a piece the player enters the number of the point followed by an H. Dice rolls are done automatically by the program (except for the Atari version where it's possible to turn on manual input of dice values). When starting a new game it's possible to reuse the dice rolls from the previous game.

meta.name: Fathoms Forty
meta.desc: Fathom's 40 is a World War II submarine simulation that puts the player in command of a German Mark XXI U-Boat during the final years of the war. Blending strategic map-based gameplay with tactical combat, the player must patrol Allied shipping lanes, locate convoys, sink freighters and tankers, evade destroyers and depth charges, and safely return to Brest, France. Tactical play involves managing speed, depth, engines, batteries, torpedoes, and periscope use while monitoring sonar displays and avoiding detection. Enemy escorts vary in weaponry and sonar capabilities, while damage to the submarine can cripple key systems and force repairs between missions. Each game features three patrols with limited torpedoes, multiple difficulty levels, automatic scoring, and a final ranking based on tonnage sunk and mission success.

meta.name: Fax
meta.desc:
Fax is a trivia game from Exidy. One or two players are asked multiple-choice questions that cover about four topics, including general knowledge, sports, history, and entertainment. There are more than 4800 questions and three difficulty levels. 
While players take the time to answer, two timers are shown. The timer at the top of the screen is shown as a bar and indicates the total time of the current round. The timer at the bottom of the screen consists of four digits, and indicates the score the player gets when they answer a question correctly. If this timer reaches zero, the player receives no score and the game moves on to the next question. The round ends when the top timer is empty.
Depending on whether the player has enabled it, a cartoon is shown regardless of a correct or incorrect answer. This cartoon shows a person receiving either a dunce cap or graduation hat.

meta.name: Fireground
meta.desc:
Fireground is an arcade/strategy game for the Apple II.
The player controls a fire truck in a large city. The player starts off as a rookie, but gains more ranks as they successfully fight fires. The player starts in their fire station, and they are given a verbal location of their fire station over the radio. There is no text description of the location. The player must decipher the purposefully garbled message, and then navigate the 3-dimensional grid-like streets to that location. If the player runs into a wall, they must return to their fire station.
After the player reaches the fire, they enter an arcade sequence where they must put out the fire.

meta.name: Fishies
meta.desc: Interactive Aquarium with no sound

meta.name: Flip Out
meta.desc:
Flip Out is a 1 or 2 player puzzle game where the players take turns dropping colored balls into rows with flippers. The goal is to have 10 of your balls reach the bottom of the screen before your opponents' do.
If the ball is dropped onto a flipper pointing up, it will become stuck. If a ball is dropped onto a flipper pointing down, it will pass through, and also knock free any ball stuck on the up side. If the ball is dropped onto a ball stuck on an upwards flipper, it will move into the adjacent row, and continue its descent without dislodging the stuck ball. The game ends when one player has cleared all their 10 balls from the screen. The are 9 different layouts, including: Cascade, Pyramid, Pyramid II, Board III, Split Board, Butterfly, Triangle, Hour Glass, and Hay Stack.

meta.name: Floppy
meta.desc: Move around the maze, avoiding the baddies and picking up all the treasurecarrots. Use boulders to block or destroy enemies or to unblock areas. Once you have all the carrots you can move the mystic blue orb and move to the next level. You start with 5 lives

meta.name: Fly Wars
meta.desc:
In Fly Wars you play as a spider attempting to gobble as many flies and other insects as possible.
The player controls, a spider which can move in the 4 cardinal directions. The player can move with or without leaving a web behind, but the goal is to trap the spiders and other insects which flit about the screen in a small web, so they can be eaten. During each life, there are a specific amount of insects on the screen. When all are eaten, the spider dies of starvation. If the player's web has extended throughout the screen, they can move onto an adjacent blank screen to consume the remaining insects. Points are awarded for insects consumed. As the spider eats more insects, they are rewarded with higher difficulty levels. On level 2, a can of bug spray will repeatedly appear on the screen to try to destroy the player. On level 3, a large red insect will try to kill the spider. The player keeps any experience levels gained throughout their 3 lives, and when all are lost it's game over

p: Fore! the Golf Game Simulation (1985)(Green Valley Publishing).zip
n: Fore! the Golf Game Simulation (1985)(Green Valley Publishing)

meta.path: ./Fore! the Golf Game Simulation (1985)(Green Valley Publishing).zip
meta.name: Fore! the Golf Game Simulation
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Fore! the Golf Game Simulation (1985)(Green Valley Publishing)-image.jpg
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Fore! the Golf Game Simulation (1985)(Green Valley Publishing)-marquee.png

meta.name: Fortress
meta.desc: Como la tribu amarilla en una isla remota, debes conquistar todos los territorios contra tus dos rivales en este juego estratégico de Dios. El aspecto tiene un borde no serio muy cursi, y toda la sensación del juego es informal. Tus hombres luchan con catapultas y montan a caballo. Debe mantener algunas tareas de guardia, equilibrando esto con la cantidad que envía para atacar otros territorios, en cuyo caso debe esperar y ver el resultado. A medida que avanza el juego, obtienes más hombres, lo que en última instancia aumenta el estado de tus bases, y esto no depende de cuántos se retienen, a diferencia del efecto reproductivo de Megalomania.

p: Fortress of the Witch King (1983)(Avalon Hill).zip
n: Fortress of the Witch King (1983)(Avalon Hill)

meta.name: Fortress of the Witch King
meta.desc: Fortress of the Witch King blends strategy and role-playing. The basic premise is to build up an army to assault the Witch King's Fortress (think Mordor). Encounters are played out in text, but there is a map screen which gives an overview of the landscape. One must raise enough gold to hire wizards, warriors, scouts, clerics and raiders by defeating bands of roaming monsters and minions. In addition, a number of artifacts and special items must be found before entering the fortress. Up to four players compete in hot-seat play.

meta.name: Fracas
meta.desc: Fracas is Stuart Smith's very first game, using the game system which would later develop into Ali Baba and the Forty Thieves and ultimately Adventure Construction Set. Like those games, it is combat-oriented turn-based RPG and one of its main features is the large numbers of characters, both good and bad, that can get involved in the battles at once. You may control one or more characters in the game, and move them about fighting bad guys and gaining treasures.

p: Fraktured Faebles (1985)(American Eagle Software)(II+)(Side A)[cr Sheik][48K].zip
n: Fraktured Faebles (1985)(American Eagle Software)(II+)(Side A)[cr Sheik][48K]

meta.name: Fraktured Faebles
meta.desc: FrakTured FaebLes is a short nursery rhyme themed graphic adventure game.nnnnThe player enters commands via text VERB NOUN commands, and most puzzles are inventory based. The player must visit a number of nursery rhyme characters to solve their problems and gather items. The player has a limited carrying capacity. Numerous dead end situations can be found by using items in the wrong order.nnnnAn evil witch has cast a spell upon the player, and they have been deposited into the middle of a strange forest. During their adventure, the player encounters Rip van Winkle, a pig playing golf, Goldilocks and the Three Bears, the Queen of Hearts, a prince, and other characters. Ultimately, you must find Sleeping Beauty who, if you find a breath mint, will ride off into the sunset with you after a kiss.

meta.name: Frogger
meta.desc:
Your task in this arcade game is to guide a frog across a treacherous road and river, and to safety at the top of the screen. Both these sections are fraught with a variety of hazards, each of which will kill the frog and cost you a life if contact is made.
The road is full of cars and trucks, at variable speeds. The river water itself is fatal, as are the snakes which hover within on later levels. Frogger must use the arrangement of logs, turtles (which are only there for a short time) and alligators (but stay away from their faces), and then jump into one of the open home-cells, ideally one containing a fly for extra points. Once all holes have been filled, you move onto the next, harder, level.

p: Frogger II - Three Deep (1984)(Sega)[cr Black Bag][48K].zip
n: Frogger II - Three Deep (1984)(Sega)[cr Black Bag][48K]

meta.name: Frogger II - Three Deep
meta.desc: En Frogger II: ThreeeDeep!, tienes que guiar a tu rana hasta la seguridad en tres lugares diferentes. Empezando bajo el agua, llega a la parte superior del estanque mientras evitas a los peligrosos caimanes y peces (¡puedes montarte en una tortuga para estar seguro!). Una vez en la parte superior del estanque, salta por encima de troncos, pájaros e incluso una ballena hasta el salvavidas que se encuentra detrás de un remolcador. En la tercera ubicación tienes que saltar sobre una bandada de pájaros para llegar a una nube en la parte superior de la pantalla. Cada rana tiene un límite de tiempo para llegar a salvo a una de las casas de cada una de las tres pantallas. Se pasa al siguiente nivel cuando una rana ha alcanzado con seguridad cada una de las casas en todas las pantallas.

p: Fun House Maze (1984)(Sunburst Communications)(US)[cr Armada - Green Hornet].zip
n: Fun House Maze (1984)(Sunburst Communications)(US)[cr Armada - Green Hornet]

meta.name: Galactic Saga - Tawala's Last Redoubt
meta.desc:
Tawala's Last Redoubt is the fourth and final game in the Galactic Saga. In the previous game, Galactic Revolution, the emperor Tawala was ousted from power by his former fleet commander, Julian du Buque. Now the fallen monarch has fled to the planet Farside. Due to unusual solar conditions, Farside is cut off from the rest of the galaxy. Now it falls to the planet's inhabitants, and the insurrectionist leader Benthi, to finish Tawala once and for all.
The player takes control of Benthi and commands her forces and agents. The ultimate goal is to attack and take Tawala's redoubt, located at the other end of the map from Benthi's camp. Resources at the beginning are meager, though: Benthi only has 100 credits, 100 troops and 15 weapons at her disposal. She must gather the resources of Farside's various chiefs to stand a real chance. Communication with the chiefs (or any friendlies not in Benthi's camp) is done by sending messengers. The chiefs can be asked for money, troops or arms, and be given money as an incentive. The messengers can be asked to threaten reprisals in the case a chief refuses a request - not acting on such a threat might damage Benthi's reputation with all of Farside's chiefs, though.
How a chief deals with an offer can be deduced from personality dossiers provided by Benthi's chief of intelligence, Paoli. Paoli also is responsible for sending out spies. They can be sent to any village on the map and will report on any movement of Tawala's troops. It is also possible to send them into the redoubt itself. Risk of capture is high, and an agent cannot be recalled from the redoubt, but he will give a full report on Tawala's overall strength. Next to troop strength and movements, agents will also provide additional information, like the locations of a hidden arms cache or the lair of independent war chief Vyl-ourmani, as well as the passwords needed to access those locations.
Regarding troops there are two options: they can be sent to only secure a town, meaning they only fight if Tawala's forces are present. In the case of uncooperative chiefs, they can also be sent to loot & pillage, in which case they attack the local militia, and, if successful, secure any loot they might find. 
Should Benthi's camp come under threat (as indicated by agents' reports), it can be moved. If Benthi is still present when Tawala's forces attack, the game is over. It is necessary to move the camp in any case, because the redoubt can only be attacked when Benthi has gathered her forces in the town closest to it.

meta.name: Galaxian
meta.desc: Galaxian is a shoot 'em up in which the player is at the bottom of the screen, with an arrangement of aliens at the top. The player moves left and right to aim at an alien, then shoots a bullet up the screen, and the alien it hits is killed. The aliens are classed as Drones, Emissaries, Hornets and Commanders, with those higher up the screen scoring more points when destroyed. At set intervals an enemy will move down the screen towards you, escorting a bomber, which is a moment of high danger. Clear a wave and another is generated.

meta.name: Galaxy Gates
meta.desc: An enemy starbase lies within 3 protective spheres. In Galaxy Gates, you have been tasked with destroying this base using fission bursts from your starcraft. Your goal is to whittle down the slowly regenerating enemy shields, while avoiding deadly energy pods which home onto your vessel. Playing on a 2-dimensional wrap-around screen, the ship is controlled by rotating the vessel, and accelerating in the direction it faces. Energy pods cannot be destroyed, but must be avoided. When a base is destroyed, the player advances to a more challenging stage. You have a total of 3 ships, and if all are destroyed, it's game over.nnnnThe game is essentially a clone of the of vector arcade game, Star Castle.

meta.name: Galaxy
meta.desc: As Fleet Admiral of a newly commissioned starship armada you send your ships from the home planet to explore the unknown resources of the stars. The planets circling those stars may be barren or they may have industrial capacity and will resist your colonization attempts. It is up to you whether to conquer and colonize those worlds, and convert them to producing new ships for continued expansion, and to bring the entire galaxy under your control. Galaxy can be played by as many as 20 players who compete against each other for control of up to 40 star systems. If you are playing multiplayer, the person controlling the most planets at game end is the winner. The computer produces a different galaxy every game.

p: Gelfling Adventure (1984)(Sierra On-Line)(Side A)[cr Apple Mafia].zip
n: Gelfling Adventure (1984)(Sierra On-Line)(Side A)[cr Apple Mafia]

meta.name: Gelfling Adventure
meta.desc: Gelfling Adventure is a computer game published by Sierra On-line for the Apple II series computers in 1984. The game included one 5 1/4" floppy disk, one booklet, and a special fold-out poster.nnnnThe game was a basic bitmap graphics/multiple choice first-person adventure, written by game designer Al Lowe. Often cited as a simplified version of the original Dark Crystal computer game intended for a younger audience. It featured many of the same graphics and storyline as the original, but the puzzles and options were more simplified and menu oriented.

meta.name: Gemstone Healer
meta.desc: The Gemstone saga continues in Gemstone Healer, the sequel to Gemstone Warrior, where you braved the horrors of the Netherworld dungeons to recover the pieces and restore the Gemstone to its rightful place in the Temple.  Now, to your dismay the Gemstone has lost its powers because the pieces have been damaged and you must return to the Netherworld to locate the healing tools and heal each part. Then you can fuse all the pieces together into a complete working Gemstone.

meta.name: Gemstone Warrior
meta.desc:
Gemstone Warrior es un juego de acción con elementos de aventura. Los jugadores se abren camino a través de una catacumba subterránea, haciendo tres cosas a la vez. Primero necesitan encontrar cinco piezas de una piedra preciosa, repartidas por todo el laberinto. En segundo lugar, deben evitar ser asesinados por los guardianes de la catacumba. Finalmente necesitan mapear el juego, porque el lugar es tan grande que es fácil perderse. Una vez que el Guerrero de piedras preciosas ha recogido las cinco piezas de piedras preciosas, necesita volver al punto donde comenzó el juego.
El héroe tiene que pasar a la clandestinidad debido a los demonios. Robaron la piedra preciosa de los humanos, porque estaban celosos de la buena magia que trajo a la humanidad. Lo rompieron y lo extendieron por todo su calabozo, para esconderlo.

meta.name: Gfl Championship Football
meta.desc:
This must be one of the earliest team sport games to use a first-person view ? the packaging mocks the Xs and Os used to represent players in contemporary Gridiron games. What you see is what the player sees ? control and viewpoint passes from the kicker to the receiver on a punt or kickoff, and the quarterback to the running back or receiver on a pass or kick. Releasing passes involves rotating the controls to select the pass direction. To take the ball, aim the visible hands in the centre of the screen.
There are 28 teams split into four divisions; their names are fictional (including the Seagulls, Mustangs and Geronimos), and each is rated out of 3 on 8 areas of skill. Matches last for 4 quarters of either 4 or 7 minutes' in-play time. Menus to select plays or teams involve using diagonal movements for selection. 
Standard Gridiron rules apply ? gain 10 yards in 4 attempts (downs) without fumbling or having a pass intercepted; kick a punt or field goal if you are about to fail at this.

meta.name: Ghost Town Gold
meta.desc:
Ghost Town Gold is a text adventure for the Apple II.
The player comes across the abandoned western town of Sandy Valley, population 0. Commands are via VERB NOUN commands, and the player's goal is to search the town for gold, and then leave. The player must explore a number of old buildings, including an abandoned hotel, barber shop, food store, jail, and clothing store.

meta.name: Giant World
meta.desc: A graphical text adventure, where the player inputs commands via VERB NOUN text entry. There is also an option for text-only mode. The player has been shrunk to a tiny size, and finds themselves within the basement of a house. The player continuously shrinks, and must complete the adventure before they shrink into oblivion. The player's ultimate goal is to navigate the house and restore themselves to normal size

meta.name: Global Thermonuclear War
meta.desc:
Set during the US-Soviet cold war, Global Thermonuclear War pits two players against each other in a simulation of just what the title says.  The first phase of the game is an arms race: both sides build up their arsenals by selecting weapons systems to develop, and then produce.  Investment can be planned according to given estimates, though the actual costs may vary; each side's budget and industrial capabilities impose limits on the final military inventory.
To end the escalation stage, each side may choose to start the conflict on any turn.  The open war phase takes place on a graphical world map, which provides an icon-based view of the opposing sides' units and bases.  Each player in turn chooses a weapon to launch, and a target (military or civilian); the game simulates "fog of war" by allowing a certain random chance for things to go wrong, like miscommunication or weapon malfunctions. The turn is then passed to the opposing player, which receives a summary of enemy strikes and detected launches, and gets to retaliate with a move of their own.

meta.name: Global War
meta.desc:
An early Risk clone for the home computer.  Players compete for 42 numbered territories on an Earth map.  Reinforcement armies are awarded every turn based on the number of territories and continents controlled.  Following the  reinforcement phase, players have the option to attack an enemy territory.  Simulated dice rolls (not shown on screen) determine the outcome of the attack.  The player's turn ends with the movement phase, in which armies can be moved into old territories or newly conquered ones.
There is no AI opponent, but the game supports up to 9 different human players.

p: Go (1982)(Hayden Book Company)(II+)[cr Computer Lord - Grimalkin][48K].zip
n: Go (1982)(Hayden Book Company)(II+)[cr Computer Lord - Grimalkin][48K]

meta.name: Go
meta.desc: Go is a digital version of the classic Chinese board game. The game is played with two players or against an AI. Like in the board game players take turns to place white or black markers on intersections of the game grid. The goal is to gain a majority control of the board. Players can remove an opponents markers by completely surrounding them with their own.

meta.name: Gobbler
meta.desc: Gobbler is a Pac-Man clone that is mostly identical to the original arcade game. The maze layout is slightly different but otherwise it offers the same gameplay. The gobbler has to clear the maze of all dots while avoiding the ghosts that chase him. Picking up a large dot energizes the gobbler for ten seconds, allowing him to go after the ghosts. Dots are worth five points, ghosts are worth 200-800 points and fruit is worth 200 points. The gobbler has three lives. The game can be played with either keyboard or joystick.

meta.name: Goblins
meta.desc:
The fourth adventure game from Highlands Computer Services, and the only one not written by Butch Greathouse and Garry Rheinhardt.
Set in Goblin Country, Goblins challenges players to collect fourteen treasures and return them to the treehouse before sunset while avoiding the creatures that roam the land under King Gobnarsk II's strict “no loitering” rule. Similar to Mystery House, the game combines black-and-white illustrated scenes with a text parser for movement and commands. Players use simple inputs like compass directions, GET, SCORE, and INVEN to interact with the world. Unique animated sequences help the game stand out, including goblins appearing unexpectedly and dramatic death scenes. The game also features a move limit tied to the setting sun, increasing danger as night approaches. Along the way, players discover magical spells that aid progress, including one that instantly transports collected items back to the treehouse, reducing the need for repeated travel.

meta.name: Golan Front
meta.desc: Golan Front recreates the 1973 Arab/Israeli war. The game is about this point of history: "During the first days of the war, Syrian artillery based in the Golan Heights pounded civilian targets in northern Israel. After dealing with Egypt, Israel decided to conquer the Golan heights, despite opposition and doubts of some in the government, including Moshe Dayan, who had been appointed defense minister. and despite the fact that the UN had already called for a cease fire. Israel agreed to a cease fire on June 10, 1967 after conquering Quneitra and completed the conquest of the Golan Heights. UN Resolution 242 called for negotiations of a permanent peace between the parties, and for Israeli withdrawal from lands occupied in 1967.

p: Golf's Best v2.0A (1985)(1 Step Software)[cr Sheik - Track Master].zip
n: Golf's Best v2.0A (1985)(1 Step Software)[cr Sheik - Track Master]

meta.path: ./Golf's Best v2.0A (1985)(1 Step Software)[cr Sheik - Track Master].zip
meta.name: Golf's Best v2.0A
meta.desc:
Golf's Best: St. Andrews: The Home of Golf is a golf simulation that recreates the legendary St. Andrews course with accurately scaled holes, realistic terrain, hazards, buildings, and the strong winds for which the course is famous. Players view each hole from overhead and side perspectives while monitoring distance, lie, club selection, and wind conditions.
Up to four players can compete, choosing their names, tee type, starting hole, and a custom set of 14 clubs. Before each shot, players select a club, swing strength, direction, and any hook or slice, with wind affecting ball flight. Gameplay follows traditional golf rules, including honors determining play order. Scorecards can be viewed throughout the round, and selecting hole 19 ends the game in the clubhouse. The simulation emphasizes strategic shot-making and realistic course management.

meta.name: Gorgon
meta.desc: Un clon muy temprano del juego de arcade Defender para el Apple ][ escrito en lenguaje ensamblador E-Z Draw. El jugador controla una nave espacial que debe proteger a los civiles en tierra de los alienígenas que caen desde la parte superior de la pantalla e intentan llevárselos.

meta.name: Grapple
meta.desc:
Steam Store Description (from Ad Blurbs)Grapple is a 3D puzzle platformer. You can fling, swing and climb across everything in Grapple. Different surfaces react uniquely to your gooball. Grapple is as fast paced as you want to play it. With multiple checkpoints and unlimited lives your skill is the only thing slowing you down.  
*90 levels
*Sandbox puzzles
*Cannons
*Speed run mode
*Secrets
Complete each level your own way.  With such powerful movement, each level can be completed in your own unique way.

p: Great Maine to California Race, The (1983)(Hayden Book Company).zip
n: Great Maine to California Race, The (1983)(Hayden Book Company)

meta.path: ./Great Maine to California Race, The (1983)(Hayden Book Company).zip
meta.name: The Great Maine to California Race
meta.desc: The Great Maine to California Race is an educational game that teaches geography and facts about the American states. The game is for two players (or one player against the computer) who both control a dragster. The aim of the game is, as the title says, to drive from Maine and reach California before the opponent does so. In each state there are a number of questions related to the state to be answered. Correct answers allow the player to drive on to the next state where new questions are asked. However a wrong answer can force the player to move back to an earlier state. The number of questions depend on the size of the state. When there are multiple borders the player can choose which state to go to next.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Great Maine to California Race, The (1983)(Hayden Book Company)-image.jpg
meta.video: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/videos/Great Maine to California Race, The (1983)(Hayden Book Company)-video.mp4
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Great Maine to California Race, The (1983)(Hayden Book Company)-marquee.png
meta.thumbnail: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Great Maine to California Race, The (1983)(Hayden Book Company)-thumb.jpg
meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Great Maine to California Race, The (1983)(Hayden Book Company)-boxback.jpg

meta.name: Grey Seas - Grey Skies
meta.desc:
Grey Seas, Grey Skies is a text-based naval combat simulation for one or two players, with an optional computer opponent. Players command fleets in historical and hypothetical conflicts across regions including the Baltic, Norwegian, Mediterranean, Indian Ocean, Western Pacific, Falklands, and Kuril Islands, or create custom scenarios.
Using radar, visual sightings, sonar, and helicopter reconnaissance, players search for enemy ships and submarines while directing each vessel's movement, speed, and attacks. Ships carry limited weapons with varying ranges and effectiveness, and changing weather conditions can affect operations.
The game uses a physical board and counters to track unit positions. Orders are issued each turn, then resolved simultaneously, with detailed combat reports provided afterward. Battles can end after a set number of turns or when one side's forces are destroyed, with players receiving a final score based on mission success.

p: Gruds in Space (1983)(Sirius Software)(Side A)[cr Freeze - Warlock][48K].zip
n: Gruds in Space (1983)(Sirius Software)(Side A)[cr Freeze - Warlock][48K]

meta.path: ./Gruds in Space (1983)(Sirius Software)(Side A)[cr Freeze - Warlock][48K].zip
meta.name: Gruds in Space
meta.desc:
This is a graphic adventure where you, as the pilot of the Earth vessel USAC 9400, are instructed to find fuel for the Earth warships that have exhausted their supply at the battlefront.  You need to find the fuel and bring it to the cargo ship on Pluto. Take a trip through the solar system solving the adventure while battling alien invaders!
Gruds In Space uses conventional interactive fiction commands, to EXAMINE, USE and TAKE objects, SHOOT Gruds, and travel around the ship and the galaxy.

meta.name: Guderian
meta.desc: Guderian simulates the critical campaign near Smolensk on the road to Moscow during WWII. As the German player you are at the head of the panzergruppe invading central Russia and must master blitzkrieg tactics to encircle and overrun Soviet divisions and drive deep enough into Russia to make the capture of Moscow possible. The Soviet player must work to counter the German threat and make attacks that will cut off the supply of the German forward units. The game can be played solitaire as either side.
meta.map: ./images/Guderian (1986)(Avalon Hill)[48K]-map.png

meta.name: Gulf Strike
meta.desc: Based upon the popular boardgame, Gulf Strike allows you to  examine every aspect of the complex Persian Gulf region. Complete air, land and sea orders of battle for more than a dozen nations allows you to fight each conflict to its unpredictable conclusion. The game will end when one of the following occurs: After 25 turns have been completed, the Soviet/Iraqi side gains 9 victory points, or the players choose to end the game.The game is for 1 or 2 players and usually takes 1-5 hours to complete. Avalon Hill rated this game a  7 on their 1(easy) -10(hard) complexity scale.
meta.map: ./images/Gulf Strike (1985)(Avalon Hill)[cr Digital Gang][48K]-map.png

p: Guns of the Fort Defiance, The (1981)(Avalon Hill).zip
n: Guns of the Fort Defiance, The (1981)(Avalon Hill)

meta.path: ./Guns of the Fort Defiance, The (1981)(Avalon Hill).zip
meta.name: The Guns of the Fort Defiance
meta.desc: Fort Defiance is a half completed stockade blocking the path of an invading army that heavily outnumbers you during the War of 1812. You command a lone Napoleonic era artillery piece and its crew and are guarding the weakest point in the forts defenses. Your performance will determine whether the fort will stand or fall. The game is played under a handicap system (from 1-63). The lower the handicap you choose at the beginning the faster the enemy will break and run. The handicapping system insures that each attack which is repelled is followed by a more determined effort. As commander of the artillery piece it is up to you to select the type of ammunition, fuse length, elevation and target.

p: Gunslinger (1986)(Datasoft)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)[cr Digital Gang].zip
n: Gunslinger (1986)(Datasoft)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)[cr Digital Gang]

meta.name: Gunslinger
meta.desc:
Your objective in Gunslinger is simply to shoot targets within various locations, keeping your skills sharp for when you have to take down one of the Wild West's most wanted. Among the levels are a typical shooting range, the open plains with bandit-shaped targets, and a high-risk saloon shootout. Bonus points are secured at the end of each level by not wasting ammunition, encouraging accuracy.
One of five Software Creations' games exclusively bundled with the Commodore 64 version of the Magnum Light Phaser gun peripheral, Gunslinger is available alongside Baby Blues, Cosmic Storm, Ghost Town and GooseBusters.

meta.name: Hacker
meta.desc:
Hacker is an adventure/trading game based on what was then a unique idea. You're starting into the adventure as a real hacker would do, lacking information of what is to come, only driven by your ambition. Consequently, the game opens with a plain text screen, prompting you for the login password. To break into the system is the first puzzle you've got to solve.
Once in, you'll soon find out that you've accidentally stumbled upon a conspiracy of global impact. From then on, you'll maneuver a robotic drone around the globe through a tunnel system deep in the earth. In major cities, you may surface and talk to contact persons, who'll trade items for cash. When delivered to the correct city, you can exchange these items for a piece of evidence. If all evidence is collected, you're to inform the FBI. As if figuring out the correct routes and trades wasn't hard enough, frequent system malfunctions and security checks test your skill as well...

p: Hacker II - The Doomsday Papers (1986)(Activision)(II+)[cr Digital Gang][64K].zip
n: Hacker II - The Doomsday Papers (1986)(Activision)(II+)[cr Digital Gang][64K]

meta.path: ./Hacker II - The Doomsday Papers (1986)(Activision)(II+)[cr Digital Gang][64K].zip
meta.name: Hacker II : The Doomsday Papers
meta.desc:
Once again you hack into a computer system, just to end up saving the world. Hacker II: The Doomsday Papers opens with the console to the "Actisource" computer hotline system (a nice self-ironic pun - although nobody knows why a serious hacker might want to crack a help service for computer games...). However, your quiet little hack is soon disturbed: The government wants you for a secret mission. It, again, involves a robot drone and you're the one at the remote control. Although this time, it's not the whole globe that's your playground, but a high security facility in Russia, where the plans for global domination are hidden.
In fact, all you've got to do is to guide your drone to the vault on a 2D map of the building. However, there are two major obstacles: Patrolling guards and security cameras. To avoid detection, you've got to trick the observers. By tapping the surveillance system you can see what cameras are currently active and which rooms they monitor. Your task is to bypass the video signal of the right cameras with a taped recording of an empty room, so that your drone is hidden from the view. You control four monitors, which you may freely switch to show the security monitor, the radar map, VCR output or one of the 38 cameras -- thus keeping an eye on guard movement while simultaneously controlling your drone and synchronizing recordings to cameras. Whew, sounds like a lot of work? It is.
