meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Murder On the Mississippi (1986)(Activision)(Side A)[cr Digital Gang]-boxback.jpg

meta.name: Mutant
meta.desc: After being awoken by your telephone, you have been summoned to meet someone to receive a mission reluctantly, seeing as you are called 'The Exterminator'. Atomic testing, 20 years ago, on a small South Sea island may be the cause of a strange dangerous creature roaming the island and you have to destroy it. On the island you will encounter various animals while searching for objects to help you in your mission. Mutant is a a text adventure that has text on screen that describes your location. You type in commands with the keyboard to interact with your surroundings.

meta.name: Hi-Res Adventure 1 - Mystery House
meta.desc:
This game is an historical piece of video gaming all by itself!
Mystery House is an adventure game released by On-Line Systems in 1980. It was designed, written and illustrated by Roberta Williams and programmed by Ken Williams for the Apple II. Mystery House is the very first graphical adventure game and the first game produced by On-Line Systems, the company which would evolve into Sierra On-Line. It is one of the earliest horror video games. 
The game starts near an abandoned Victorian mansion. The player is soon locked inside the house with no other option than to explore. The mansion contains many interesting rooms and seven other people: Tom, a plumber; Sam, a mechanic; Sally, a seamstress; Dr. Green, a surgeon; Joe, a gravedigger; Bill, a butcher; and Daisy, a cook.
Initially, the player has to search the house in order to find a hidden cache of jewels. However, terrible events start happening and dead bodies (of the other people) begin appearing. It becomes obvious that there is a murderer on the loose in the house, and the player must discover who it is or become the next victim.

p: Mystery Master - Felony! (1984)(CBS Software)[cr Mr. Krac-Man].zip
n: Mystery Master - Felony! (1984)(CBS Software)[cr Mr. Krac-Man]

meta.name: Mystery Master : Felony
meta.desc: There is a crime wave in the town of Huxley! As a mystery master, you need to help the police solve 12 different crimes that have taken place. Throughout each of the cases you can interrogate suspects, search for clues at various locations, or use police station resources to analyze clues. To win you need to find out who the criminal is within the given time limit, as well as collect enough evidence to convict him and send him to jail.

meta.name: Naval Battle of Tsushima
meta.desc:
Naval Battle of Tsushima is a strategy game developed by Avalon Hill and published by Avalon Hill in 1984 for the Apple II. The Japanese combined fleet and the Russian Baltic Fleet, sent over from Europe, fought in the straits between Korea and Japan near the island of Tsushima. The Battle of Tsushima (in Japan, it is called the "Sea of Japan Naval Battle") was the decisive sea battle of the Russo-Japanese war of 1904-1905. In this battle the Japanese fleet under Admiral Heihachiro Togo destroyed 2/3 of the Russian fleet under Admiral Zinovi Petrovich Rozhdestvenski. You take the side of one of these commanders and see how well you fare in the same situation. This is a "what if" game of tyring different strategies to effect the outcome.
The game is run in turns with the computer handling weather and executing your orders.

meta.name: Neptune
meta.desc:
Neptune is a side scrolling arcade game where the player controls a submarine while destroying or avoiding underwater sea creatures.
The submarine can move in all 4 cardinal directions, while the ground and monsters rapidly scroll by from the right. The submarine has an unlimited number of laser shots which shoot out horizontally from the vessel, as well as a limited number of bombs, which descend vertically. The are multiple levels, and each level has several stages, including underground components and differing enemies. The player receives 20 bombs per level. The player has limited fuel, but will refuel at the end of each level. Enemy creatures consist of various underwater creatures, including sharks, jellyfish, and more. If the player hits a creature or the ground, they lose a life. The player starts with 5 lives, and it's game over when all lives are lost.

meta.name: Night Driver
meta.desc: Night Driver is an action racing game which takes place at night! Your goal is to score as many points as possible by racing through one of four different tracks. Points are scored by passing checkpoints located throughout the track. If your car crashes into the side of the road, oncoming cars, or any other obstacles, you will lose all of your speed and a lot of time! The game ends when the 90 second time limit is up. The four tracks you can choose from are beginner, pro, expert, and random (this track is different every time and can not be memorized like the other three). Several game options are included which allows you to select whether or not other cars will honk before entering the screen, and choose between a 90 second or infinite amount of time for a game.

meta.name: Night Mission Pinball
meta.desc: Night Mission Pinball is a pinball game simulation. You can change many physical parameters of the machine to suit your playing style or your whim such as the number of balls, ball speed, the incline of the machine, the kick off the bumpers, etc. It supports 1-4 players and is controlled by the keyboard or joystick.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Night Mission Pinball (1982)(subLOGIC)[48K]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Nine Princes in Amber
meta.desc:
This interactive fiction game is based on Roger Zelazny's fantasy novels Nine Princes In Amber (1970) and The Guns of Avalon (1972).
The game begins with you as Prince Corwin forced to compete against your eight scheming noble brothers and sisters for the throne of Amber at the center of the one true world. Corwin must travel across the dimensions to this fascinating realm and from there rally forces against those who would oppose him. Unfortunately, things do not always proceed as planned.
When you wake up, you know little of this, as you have been knocked unconscious and pummeled with drugs. All you have is the address of your scheming sister.
The game is largely based around interaction with other characters, and can end in 40 different ways depending on how these work.

meta.name: Norad
meta.desc:
Norad is an action strategy game where the player has to defend the United States from a nuclear attack. The premise is similar to Missile Command but the gameplay differs. The game is played on a map of the United States and the player has access to ten missile sites that are used to defend the same number of cities. As enemy intercontinental ballistic missiles (ICBM) appear in the sky, the player launches an anti-ballistic missile (ABM) by pressing the number of one of the sites and steers it towards the target before hitting the space bar to detonate it when it is within range.
Each site has a limited amount of missiles but these are replenished between waves as long as its connected city has not been destroyed. A non-damaged city produces five ABMs for its site while one that has take one hit builds two. A city is totally destroyed when it has taken two hits and will not produce any more missiles but the site will still be able to launch those that are in its stockpile. A site that is hit will lose its entire stockpile. Cities that have taken only one hit will repair themselves between waves. On later levels a defensive MX site is available. It can be moved on a fixed roadway and is not dependent on a city. It is restocked from along its movement path and due to its mobility it can not be hit. There is also a nuclear submarine that works similarily but that is restricted to the Carribean.

meta.name: North Atlantic Convoy Raider
meta.desc: It is May 8, 1941 and you are Captain of the mighty German battleship Bismarck the single most powerful ship in the Atlantic. Hitler has given you the nearly impossible task of fighting through the British Home Fleet and sinking enough convoys to force Britain to surrender. Your level of victory will be determined by how many merchant ships and battleships you can sink. Your decisions will determine the fate of the Bismarck.

meta.name: Nukewar
meta.desc: En el siglo XX, usted y un país vecino desarrollan una fuerza de ataque nuclear. El único objetivo de su vecino es aniquilarle por completo. A medida que aumentan las tensiones mientras lleva a cabo operaciones de espionaje, usted o su vecino lanzan un primer ataque preventivo utilizando bombarderos, misiles, submarinos y misiles antibalísticos. La destrucción nuclear continúa hasta que se agotan todas las armas o se negocia una tregua.

meta.name: Odyssey, The Compleat Adventure
meta.desc:
Odyssey: The Compleat Apventure was a videogame written by Robert Clardy and released by Synergistic Software in 1980. It was created for the Apple II platform and is considered one of the first microcomputer-based role-playing video games. The title was intentionally misspelled; Apventure is a reference to the Apple computer.
A forerunner of Akalabeth and Ultima, Odyssey was a multi-part adventure game that placed the player in the role of the leader of an army who sets out to vanquish an evil wizard. Elements of Dungeons & Dragons can be found within the game, which combines elements of two earlier games written by Clardy: Dungeon Campaign and Wilderness Campaign.

meta.name: Oil Barons
meta.desc: Oil Barons is a turn based strategy game for one to eight players. The object of the game is to accumulate the most wealth by locating and drilling for oil. During the game you will need to survey different lands ranging from the arctic to the desert for oil potential, choose the best sites and drill! Congress may pass bills from time to time which forbid drilling in parks and other reserved lands, which may or may not affect a players strategy. Several modes of gameplay are featured, including  regular classic, reality mode (oil locations are more randomized at the beginning of the game, and costs are supposed to more closely resemble those in real life), and customized modes. Included with the game is a game board with a 50x40 grid of the landscapes you can drill in, and dozens of game pieces to keep track of which locations belong to a player and where oil has (or has not) been discovered. This makes Oil Barons resemble a board game, but with a computer to do the bookkeeping and add a random element instead of dice.

meta.name: Oil-Rig
meta.desc:
Oil Rig is a real-time 1-player managerial simulation for the Apple II.
The player takes control of an oil company for a period of 10 - 40 years, with the goal to maximize profit. Gameplay occurs in real time, with monthly changes in oil prices. The player can buy and sell resources including: crude, shale, refined oil, kerosene, gas, and petrol products. The player must also purchase equipment to run an oil company, including: drill rigs, oil rigs, a refinery, pipeline, tankers, trucks, and oil platforms.
The player can prospect for oil on the prospecting map, and when oil is located, can use their drill rig to drill in that location for oil, which can be sold for profit. Crude oil must be transported to market, and can be refined for profit.
If the player at any time attempts to purchase more than they can afford, their company immediately dissolves into bankruptcy, and the game is lost.

meta.name: Oil's Well
meta.desc: Guide your drilling bit through the underground tunnels and collect the dots of oil. Be sure to avoid the underground pests in the eight levels and beyond. With each completed level you get to see a small movie of Slater the "Petrosaur" in his antics in this remake of the original.

meta.name: Old Ironsides
meta.desc:
Old Ironsides is a two player game that simulates nineteenth century naval combat. The action takes place on a single screen in which each player controls a three-masted sailing vessel. The speed and movement of the ships is determined by the wind. The wind is always blowing from south to north, making southward movement difficult unless a player tacks and navigates his ship in a zigzag manner.
Each ship is equipped with twelve cannons, six on each side of the ship, and a supply of powder. The goal of the game is to defeat the opposing player in battle. A player loses if either their ship is sunk or if they get lost in the fog.
Sinking a vessel can be achieved by cannon fire or by ramming. Cannons fire perpendicular to the direction of travel. As a ship takes damage, a status indicator will slowly fill with red and a ship will lose cannons, thereby decreasing their firepower. Occasionally, an especially accurate broadside will hit an opponents magazine, instantly destroying the adversary's ship. As players fire cannons, their powder supply will deplete. Powder regenerates itself at a rate much slower than the rate of fire, so players must always be aware of their stockpile.
If a ship moves off the edge of the screen, it is said to be, "in the fog." A ship that  stays in the fog too long loses the battle. A series of increasingly higher pitched beeps warn the player that their time is running out. Navigation in the fog is aided by a compass display at the top of the screen. This indicator shows the ship's bearing at any given time and can be switched off if a player wishes to hide their movements in the fog.

meta.name: Oldorf's Revenge
meta.desc:
Oldorf's Revenge is an adventure game with RPG elements. Game play consists of typing verb-noun commands to explore a variety of locations. The player can choose from a total of 7 different classes, including: Cleric, Thief, Gladiator, Elf, Strongman, Magician, and Wizard. The player must change between different classes as they explore Oldorf's cavern, as different characters have different abilities. The player must explore a total of 100 individual locations, as they try to score 300 points by finding treasures within the caverns.
The player can switch between the 7 characters during game play, but each character can only be chosen 5 times. The player starts with 100 strength, and they lose 2 strength every time they switch class. The player also loses strength if they take damage from monsters or the environment during their exploration. If the player's strength reaches 0, then it is game over.

meta.name: One-on-One
meta.desc: Control either Julius Erving or Larry Bird in front of a single basket, in a game of one on one.  The user can select to play either to a certain score or for a certain amount of time.  When on offense, the player can rotate to keep away from his rival, take a shot from a distance or move in to the basket for special up-close slam-dunks.  When on defense, you can try to take your rival's ball or jump up to block a shot.  Particularly good set-ups get a slow-motion replay.  You can even smash the glass on the backboard, which will send an irate janitor onto the court to clean up the mess.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/One on One (1983)(Electronic Arts)(II+)[cr L.S.D.][48K]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Operation Apocalypse
meta.desc: Operation Apocalypse is war simulation game based on WWII land operations. There are four separate solitaire scenarios to choose from: Advance To Contact, Bridgehead, Invasion and Highway. You have four levels of difficulty to choose from. In each scenario, the computer assigns the forces for both opponents which are completely equal in size and strength. At the start of the game, you have to place your troops on each game hex. There are variable terrain hexes for each game that poses a challenge for each game.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Operation Apocalypse (1981)(SSI)-manual.pdf

meta.name: Pac-Man
meta.desc:
One of the most popular and influential games of the 1980's, Pac-Man stars a little, yellow dot-muncher who works his way around to clear a maze of the various dots and fruit which inhabit the board.
Pac-Man's goal is continually challenged by four ghosts: The shy blue ghost Bashful (Inky), the trailing red ghost Shadow (Blinky), the fast pink ghost Speedy (Pinky), and the forgetful orange ghost Pokey (Clyde).  One touch from any of these ghosts means a loss of life for Pac-Man.
Pac-Man can turn the tables on his pursuers by eating of the four Energizers located within the maze.  During this time, the ghosts turn blue, and Pac-Man can eat them for bonus points.  This only lasts for a limited amount of time, as the ghosts' eyes float back to their center box, and regenerate to chase after Pac-Man again.
Survive a few rounds of gameplay, and be treated to humorous intermissions between Pac-Man and the ghosts.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Pac-Man (1983)(Atari)-manual.pdf

p: Pacific 231 (19xx)(Ediciel - Spinnaker)(fr)[cr Association of Broadcasting Crackers].zip
n: Pacific 231 (19xx)(Ediciel - Spinnaker)(fr)[cr Association of Broadcasting Crackers]

meta.name: Pacific 231
meta.desc:
Pacific 231 is a educational strategy game for the Apple II.
The player takes on the role of conductor of a train from a top down perspective, and the goal is to pick up cargo at a location and bring it to the assigned destination. The player can accelerate and decelerate their train and change direction at a railroad switch. The player picks up milk, wood, fuel, and iron from farms, forests, oil pumps, and mines. These must then be delivered to the appropriate dairy, sawmill, foundry or refinery. The player receives money for completing their tasks. There are a total of 8 different tracks in different terrain

meta.name: Pandemonium
meta.desc:
The young sorceress Nikki, the court jester Fargus, and his side-kick Sid the puppet are the three main protagonists of this 3D platform game. While attending a "Wizards in Training" seminar, Nikki accidentally casts a powerful magic spell, which results in a giant monsters swallowing the whole village. The three friends must now travel to a place called "Wishing Engine", and be granted a wish to revert the world back to normal.
The characters each have a special movement to kill enemies with. The game uses floating camera angles through a vast Prince of Persia-style world. The goal of the game is to clear the various levels, avoiding spikes and enemies and collecting coins for extra lives.
Unlike most 3D platformers, Pandemonium offers only two-dimensional movement, rather than complete freedom of movement. The characters move along a curving 2D course within a 3D environment.

meta.name: Panzer Grenadier
meta.desc: Panzer Grenadier is a solitaire wargame of German armored infantry warfare on the Eastern Front during WW II. You command a regiment of Panzer Grenadiers (elite armored infantry) attached to the Grossdeutschland Panzer Grenadier Division. You will also be given elements of a panzer regiment and an artillery battalion. Your forces include mortars, artillery, Panzer IV, Panther and Tiger Tanks. There are five historical scenarios  provided with three levels of difficulty. One of four levels of victory attained is determined after every turn and at the end of the game.

meta.name: Pegasus 2
meta.desc:
Pegasus II is a Scramble variant. As such it is a side scrolling shoot 'em up where the player flies his ship over a mountainous terrain. On the ground there will be different enemy installations that the player can attempt to destroy by dropping bombs onto them. There are also rockets that launch from the ground and that the player can use his ship's laser beams to shoot down. As progress is made through the game various flying enemies (birds, saucers, dragons etc.) will appear. Any contact with an enemy or the ground will lead to a lost life. The player has five lives when starting out.
Also included is a terrain generator that allows the player to create his own landscape to fly over. This is done by the player defining twenty one separate frames of landscape that the program then throws together randomly.

meta.name: Pengo
meta.desc: In Pengo, you must defeat the enemy by pushing ice blocks together to crush them. You also can capture one if they are shocked and dizzy. There is also a time limit to make it a bit more difficult (you must do it in one minute).

meta.name: Penny Arcade
meta.desc: This Apple II+ software collection bundles games, utilities, and demonstration programs from Apple. Its standout title is LITTLE BRICK OUT, an AppleSoft BASIC version of Steve Wozniak's earlier Breakout adaptation. Unlike most brick-breaking games, it plays horizontally and allows players to customize options such as paddle size and colors.The disk also includes PENNY ARCADE, a set of Pong-style games featuring Bumper Pool, Hockey, Scramble, and Tennis, along with LEMONADE, an early business simulation where players manage a lemonade stand.Rounding out the package are a variety of demos and utilities, including COLOR DEMOSOFT, HOPALONG CASSIDY, PHONE LIST, BRIAN'S THEME, and RENUMBER/APPEND. An ALIGNMENT TEST TONE is also included, providing an audio signal for cassette and hardware calibration rather than an interactive program.

meta.name: Penqueriel Mazes
meta.desc: Penqueriel Mazes is a 1-player maze navigation game for the Apple II.A quadrail deposits you in a small building on top of a low cliff. Far below to the south, you see an identical building. This is your goal. The player controls a character in the cardinal directions, viewed from above from an isometric projection. There are two gameplay options, in the first, the player must navigate a maze which contains a number of invisible teleporters. The player has access to an overhead map at anytime, which can help them navigate the map. In this mode, there are no keys or barricades. In the second gameplay option, the map contains a number of locked doors and rooms. There are a number of keys which contain boxes, and the player can only hold 2 keys at a time. The player can only go through a door while holding the correct key, and the doors remain locked behind them. The player can pick up and drop off keys at various boxes found throughout the maze.

meta.name: Pentapus
meta.desc: Pentapus is a 1-player science fiction arcade shooter for the Apple II. The player controls a set of boxed in crosshairs, and must destroy alien vessels. The crosshairs can be moved in all directions to blast alien creatures. If the player is shot by the alien creatures, they are destroyed, and the player loses a life. There are 4 difficulty levels: K, 1, 2, 3 which control the number of enemies in each wave. Enemies can scroll off any side of the screen and appear on the opposite. The player's controller can wrap laterally but not vertically on the screen. If the player destroys all monsters they advance to the next level. On level 5, the player encounters the dreaded Pentapus, which serves as the final boss to the game. If the player loses their 5 starting lives, it's game over.

p: Perry Mason - The Case of the Mandarin Murder (1985)(Telarium)(Disk 1 of 4).zip
n: Perry Mason - The Case of the Mandarin Murder (1985)(Telarium)(Disk 1 of 4)

meta.path: ./Perry Mason - The Case of the Mandarin Murder (1985)(Telarium)(Disk 1 of 4).zip
meta.name: Perry Mason : The Case of the Mandarin Murder
meta.desc: The Case of the Mandarin Murder is a text adventure with graphics, based on the Perry Mason detective stories by Erle Stanley Gardner. A woman is in jail after her rich husband is found dead, and the skillful attorney Perry Mason must prove her innocence. The gameplay is influenced by Infocom's games in similar genre.

meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Perry Mason - The Case of the Mandarin Murder (1985)(Telarium)(Disk 1 of 4)-boxback.jpg

meta.name: Pest Patrol
meta.desc: Pest Patrol es un juego tipo Space Invaders. Hay 39 niveles de juego y cada nivel se compone de un patrón de ataque diferente. También hay una opción de práctica disponible que te permite practicar cualquiera de los primeros 29 niveles. Los últimos 10 niveles solo se pueden alcanzar en el modo de juego.

meta.name: Pharaoh's Revenge
meta.desc:
Pharaoh's Revenge is a fast action computer game in the arcade tradition. Using the keyboard or joystick the player must attempt to evade an opponent (computer or human) while solving a complex maze that, when solved, leads to safety.
There are 200 levels in the main game, with 50 advanced levels that become available after the player completes the original 200.  In addition, there is a construction set for creating additional levels which can then be shared with friends.

meta.name: PHM Pegasus
meta.desc: PHM Pegasus is a combination simulation and action game. You control a naval hydrofoil missile boat, and need to complete a variety of different missions. Some of the missions include destroying all enemy vessels in a given area, surveillance requiring you to identify the location of enemy ships (without sinking them!), or to escort a supply ship through hostile territory. As missions are successfully completed you earn points based on how successful the mission was, and with increasing points you can advance in rank, from deck mopper up to admiral. Your performance on one mission won't have an affect on future ones, and you can choose to play missions out of order as well.  Several missions include other craft you can control to help out (both ships and helicopters). A time compression mode is included, which can be used to reduce the amount of time waiting to arrive at a destination, or even left on to make the action sequences more challenging.

meta.name: Pick-A-Dilly Pair
meta.desc:
Pick-A-Dilly Pair is a 1 - 6 player tile matching game for the Apple II.
Players are presented with a series of 36 tiles, which each contain an animated graphic when they are selected. Players take turns selecting tiles to try to match them. When tiles are successfully matched, they are removed from the board, and the player who obtains the most matches wins. There are several variants to increase replay value. Flash shows the underlying graphic for a brief time period, making it more difficult to remember positions. There options to match 3 or 4 tiles at a time, and one option includes jokers and villains, which will act as bonus cards or cause a player to lose their turn.

p: Pillage Village (1982)(Software Marketing Services)(Side A)[cr Black Bag].zip
n: Pillage Village (1982)(Software Marketing Services)(Side A)[cr Black Bag]

meta.path: ./Pillage Village (1982)(Software Marketing Services)(Side A)[cr Black Bag].zip
meta.name: Pillage Village
meta.desc: Pillage Village was a graphics adventure game written by Stuart and Rick, and published by Software Marketing Services in 1982 for the Apple ][. It's a typical treasure hunt adventure, in which the player needs to find several items in the game and drop them at a specific spot. I found this game interesting because it seems to an independent game created by two guys and powered by a very primitive parser written in BASIC, yet it has a complex map with 129 rooms and provides the ability to operate vehicles (car, boat, and raft) in the game, an unique feature for adventure games at that time.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Pillage Village (1982)(Software Marketing Services)(Side A)[cr Black Bag]-image.jpg
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Pillage Village (1982)(Software Marketing Services)(Side A)[cr Black Bag]-marquee.png

p: Pinball Construction Set (1983)(Electronic Arts)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Cat].zip
n: Pinball Construction Set (1983)(Electronic Arts)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Cat]

meta.name: Pinball Construction Set
meta.desc:
Pinball Construction Set is considered the first construction set game that launched the concept and genre.  
Construct a pinball game by dropping parts onto the table (or alter the table itself). Alter world physics like gravity, bounce, kick, and speed. Paint custom backboards. Wire together targets for specials and bonus points. Finally, save creations for later, or generate a self-loading version of the table to distribute to friends (original program not needed to play).
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Pinball Construction Set (1983)(Electronic Arts)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Cat]-manual.pdf

meta.name: The Planet Miners
meta.desc: Es el 1 de enero de 2050 y, tras décadas de investigación y desarrollo en el campo de los viajes espaciales, la humanidad está lista para viajar a los planetas del sistema solar para establecer bases mineras. El jugador tendrá que asegurarse valiosas concesiones mineras por todo el sistema solar, mientras el consejo minero ha estacionado naves patrulla por todo el sistema para asegurarse de que todo el mundo cumple las normas. Comienzan así varias semanas de intensa exploración, maniobras políticas, sabotajes y adjudicación de concesiones que decidirán el curso de la explotación económica del espacio exterior durante los próximos cien años.

meta.name: Planet of Crawlers
meta.desc:
Planet of Crawlers is a 1-player science fiction themed arcade game for the Apple II.
The player controls a spaceship on the bottom of the screen which moves horizontally and can fire vertically. The player has to avoid mushrooms, as well as a white and orange spiders. The spiders move towards the player, bouncing off the edges of the play area. The white spider moves more vertically, while the red one prefers a larger angle. These identical two monsters appear repeatedly. The player has 20 ships, and it's game over when all are lost. the player is awarded points for spiders killed.

meta.name: Planetary Construction Set
meta.desc:
Planetary Construction Set is an educational world-building simulation that combines astronomy, planetary science, biology, and physics. Through tutorials and quizzes, players learn about star systems, planetary composition, atmospheres, orbital mechanics, and animal physiology.
The program features two mission modes. In Cadet Missions, players must design a planet that meets specific requirements, balancing factors such as year length, day length, temperature, star type, atmospheric makeup, and orbital distance. In Captain's Missions, players are given an alien species whose home world is no longer habitable and must create a suitable replacement planet. Success requires experimentation, note-taking, and careful adjustment of environmental conditions. After each attempt, the program evaluates the world and highlights any problems, allowing players to refine their design until the ideal habitat is achieved.

meta.name: Planetoids
meta.desc: Planetoids es un clon temprano de Asteroids para el Apple II.nnLa jugabilidad es la misma que en el arcade original, con el jugador teniendo el control de una nave que tiene que destruir los asteroides circundantes. La nave puede ser rotada y se mueve usando el impulso para enviarla hacia adelante. También existe la posibilidad de entrar en el hiperespacio, lo que envía la nave a una ubicación aleatoria en la pantalla. Los asteroides más grandes se rompen en 2 piezas más pequeñas si se les dispara, y estas 2 piezas más pequeñas vuelven a romperse. Estas piezas finales diminutas pueden ser disparadas y destruidas. Además de los asteroides, aparecen acoplamientos estelares que valen puntos de bonificación si se les dispara.

meta.name: Platoon
meta.desc:
The film Platoon featured a squad of five soldiers on their mission for the US Army in Vietnam. In this action game based around the film, you take control of these five guys in turn, with the chance to switch between them as they each take one of the 3 hits that would kill them.
The game recreates each of the sections of the film, starting with a side-scrolling journey through the jungle, with paths to cross both horizontally and vertically, and the ability to jump or duck hazards. You will need to blow up a bridge and then locate a village, which contains a torch, a map and a trap-door. The enemy has booby-trapped the area and are launching airstrikes, so the danger is not all from enemy ground soldiers.
The next section is in first-person 3D, as you move through a network of tunnels in search of flares and a compass. There are 3 distinct control methods, changing from the standard motion when an enemy appears (this puts you in control of a cross-hair for more accurate targeting), and when you enter a room to search.
After escaping this you settle in a bunker overnight, and have a limited amount of flares with which to locate incoming enemy, and must then shoot them before they can shoot you. The jungle airstrike from the film is the next moment - you have two minutes to rush into a safe position to the north, using your compass for guidance.
Finally you face the treacherous Sergeant Barnes, and must hit him with grenades 5 times whilst trapped in a foxhole to prevent him wiping you out.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Platoon (1988)(Data East)(IIE)(Side A)[cr Blade][128K]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Police Artist
meta.desc: Police Artist is an educational memory game that challenges players to recognize and recreate faces. As a police sketch artist, your job is to remember suspects and help identify criminals. The game includes a facial-composite system with customizable eyes, hair, noses, lips, and chins, allowing more than one million possible faces. Each generated character is also given a random name.Three game modes are included. In Police Lineup, players briefly study a suspect before identifying them from a lineup of increasingly similar faces. In Police Artist, players must recreate a suspect's face from memory using the composite-building tools, with higher difficulty levels adding more facial options. Off Duty serves as a free-form mode where players can create faces from all available features and generate unique identities. Success depends on observation, memory, and attention to detail.

meta.name: Popeye
meta.desc: As Popeye, you are trying to win Olive Oyl's love! She is at the top of the screen dropping tokens of her love, and you need to collect them before they hit the ground. After you have collected the required number of items, you can move on to the next, more difficult level. There are many obstacles trying to stop you from completing your task, though! Brutus wanders around the screen and is constantly trying to catch you. If you collect a can of spinach, you can temporarily knock him out, otherwise Brutus will knock you out. Other objects such as bottles or birds are also flying around the screen and will cause you to lose one of your lives if you are hit. Each level features a different layout of platforms and ladders, and will have you collecting different items as they float towards the bottom of the screen!

meta.name: Power Bots
meta.desc:
Power Bots is a science-fiction strategy/RPG for the Apple II. The player controls an android exploring a 5×5 grid in search of a hidden power module needed to advance through four levels. At the start of each level, the android equips two of four weapons: a flamethrower, rocket launcher, electrode gun, or goo gun.
Each room is either empty or occupied by a robot creature. Four enemy types appear, each resistant or immune to certain weapons, requiring players to choose their arsenal carefully. Combat is turn-based, with both sides exchanging fire simultaneously. Some creatures are secretly friendly and will provide clues to the power module's location if left unharmed.
The power module is hidden in an empty room and must be found through searching. Every action—moving, searching, or taking damage—consumes energy. If energy reaches zero, the game ends. Energy is restored between levels, but efficient exploration and combat are essential for survival.

meta.name: Prisoner 2
meta.desc: High-resolution graphics remake of the text-based adventure game inspired by the cult-classic British television show about a former spy who is abducted and sent to a resort-themed "prison" where his captors attempt to get him to reveal why he resigned from his classified job. The game takes place on "The Island," where the player travels from building to building, each hosting a metaphorical quest in which the player's creative thinking skills are tested. Players demonstrating individual thinking eventually gain access to the Island's "Caretaker," and their ensuing conversation (using a language parser) can lead to the player's freedom.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Prisoner 2 (1982)(Interactive Fantasies)-manual.pdf

p: Pro Football - The Gold Edition (1982)(System Design Lab)[cr Copycatter].zip
n: Pro Football - The Gold Edition (1982)(System Design Lab)[cr Copycatter]

meta.path: ./Pro Football - The Gold Edition (1982)(System Design Lab)[cr Copycatter].zip
meta.name: Pro Football - The Gold Edition
meta.desc: PRO FOOTBALL POINTSPREAD PREDICTION SYSTEM.  1978: IN THE BEGINNING...... Program developed and tested in West Virginia where enraged bookies run prediction service out of town! 1979: HEADING FOR WESTERN SKIES..... Prediction service resumes operations in California where local TV coverage increases sales and rights to program nearly sold. 1980: IN THE MONEY..... After discovering program predicts certain games more accurately than others, computer closes 1980 season by winning 73% of its 60 predicted upset games against the Las Vegas Line!
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Pro Football - The Gold Edition (1982)(System Design Lab)[cr Copycatter]-image.jpg
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Pro Football - The Gold Edition (1982)(System Design Lab)[cr Copycatter]-marquee.png

meta.name: Pro Golf
meta.desc:
Sunningdale and Pebble Beach golf courses are provided in this strategic golf game. Up to four players can take part in single round, tournament (featuring 18 players over four rounds), or practice modes.
The game is viewed from overhead, with the entire hole on a single screen. The player must choose a club, then a shot power, and then a shot direction, before executing the planned shot by pressing space once to start the swing, and again to execute it ? imprecise timing can impart draw or fade onto the shot. On the green you must choose the shot power before the direction, taking an overall slope into account. There is no indication as to how far the ball is from the hole.

meta.name: Profession Detective
meta.desc: Profession Detective is the French translated and adapted release of the educational adventure game "In Search of the Most Incredible Thing" (originally published by Spinnaker Software).  is the French translated and adapted release of the educational adventure game "In Search of the Most Incredible Thing" (originally published by Spinnaker Software). The game was introduced to the Apple II in 1984. In this interactive mystery, players take on the role of a private detective and must gather clues to find a missing, prized object (often summarized as "Who stole Lily?"). Educational Focus: Designed for younger players, it teaches deductive reasoning, map navigation, and careful reading. Clue Gathering: Players travel to different locations, interview quirky characters, and collect physical evidence. Notebook Mechanics: You use a detective's log to keep track of statements and narrow down the list of suspects.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Profession Detective (19xx)(-)(fr)-manual.pdf

p: Pure Stat Baseball (1986)(Quest)(Disk 1 of 3)[cr First Class].zip
n: Pure Stat Baseball (1986)(Quest)(Disk 1 of 3)[cr First Class]

meta.name: Pure Stat Baseball
meta.desc:
Pure-Stat Baseball is a sports simulation that allows player to replay individual games or entire baseball seasons, from the past or present, while keeping a permanent record of your team's stats using the built in Stat Compiler. In this simulation. The player can relive 8 major Major League Baseball teams from the 1927 New York Yankees to the 1976 Cincinnati Reds.
Gameplay allows for player versus player, player versus computer, and computer versus computer games. Player can choose to play at Yankee, Dodger, or Sublogic Stadium. The player on defense sets the type of pitching and outfield position. Players at bat set the type of batting they wish to perform. The computer calculates the resulting play, which is displayed in animated form on the screen.

meta.name: Pyramid
meta.desc: Pyramid is a real-time puzzle game similar to Tetris. Stones of various shapes and sizes fall from the top of the screen, and you need to move and rotate the stones so they fill complete lines at the bottom of the screen. Should the stones reach the top of the screen the game is over. If you get in trouble, there are a limited number of bombs that can be used to clear a small section of stones at the bottom hopefully giving you more room to work with. Unlike the square and rectangular shaped pieces found in Tetris, each of the shapes in Pyramid have one or more angles to them making complete lines more difficult to form.

p: Quest of the Gem (19xx)(-)(Side A)[cr Dark Prophet].zip
n: Quest of the Gem (19xx)(-)(Side A)[cr Dark Prophet]

meta.path: ./Quest of the Gem (19xx)(-)(Side A)[cr Dark Prophet].zip
meta.name: Quest of the Gem
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Quest of the Gem (19xx)(-)(Side A)[cr Dark Prophet]-marquee.png

meta.name: The Quest
meta.desc:
A dragon is terrorizing the kingdom of Balema, and King Galt send his champion, named Gorn, to solve the problem. The player takes the role of a nameless advisor, who has to help the strong, but not very intelligent Gorn to get rid of the dragon and save his homeland.
The Quest was one of the first graphic adventures that incorporated full sentence parsing. Unlike most other adventures, the commands the player inputs do not refer to the playable character. Rather, as the playable character, the player gives those commands to Gorn. Instead of just entering short verb commands, the player is able to type relatively complex instructions, that have to be understood by Gorn in order to perform the necessary action

p: Question of Scruples, A (1987)(-)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Space Pirate].zip
n: Question of Scruples, A (1987)(-)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Space Pirate]

meta.path: ./Question of Scruples, A (1987)(-)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Space Pirate].zip
meta.name: A Question of Scruples
meta.desc:
This computer game is based on the popular adult board game. Three to ten people can play, any of which can be computer controlled. Before the game starts, the human players determine their personality by giving scores to certain attributes. The computer's personalities are pre-determined. You can choose a picture of a face.
Each player has three to five dilemma cards and a single answer card. The responses are limited to yes or no. The objective of the game is to get rid of all your dilemma cards. You select an opponent to answer a card. If the player's answer and your answer card match, then they are both discarded. You are given a new answer card. Your strategy is to pick a person you know will give the answer you are looking for. If the answer doesn't match, the player is challenged. The remaining players vote on whether the answer given is true to their character or not. The winner of the challenge gives a dilemma card to the loser.
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Question of Scruples, A (1987)(-)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Space Pirate]-marquee.png

p: Questprobe featuring The Hulk v2.3-126 (1985)(Adventure International)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Syndicate - Whip].zip
n: Questprobe featuring The Hulk v2.3-126 (1985)(Adventure International)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Syndicate - Whip]

meta.name: Questprobe featuring The Hulk v2.3-126 (1985)(Adventure International)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Syndicate - Whip]
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Questprobe featuring The Hulk v2.3-126 (1985)(Adventure International)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Syndicate - Whip]-marquee.png

p: Quizagon (1983)(Springboard Software)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)[cr Whip].zip
n: Quizagon (1983)(Springboard Software)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)[cr Whip]

meta.name: Race for Midnight
meta.desc:
Race for Midnight is a graphical text adventure set in 14th-century Europe. After waking with a mysterious wound and learning a werewolf was seen near your village, you realize you may be cursed yourself. Rumors tell of a cure hidden in the dungeon of the wizard Evro, so you set out to find it before the next full-moon transformation. When the ground collapses beneath you, you become trapped in the dungeon's depths. With only 500 turns until midnight, you must locate the potion and escape or remain a werewolf forever.
Gameplay uses a VERB-NOUN parser and allows players to switch between graphical and text displays. Exploration involves collecting items, solving puzzles, and battling dungeon creatures. Combat is determined by dice rolls: the player attacks first and may defeat an enemy with a high roll, while successful enemy rolls inflict damage. The hero can survive only three hits before the adventure ends.

meta.name: Rainy Day Games
meta.desc:
Rainy Day Games is 1 - 3 player series of 3 card games for children.
The player competes in card games against their opponent Ted Bear. There are three difficulty levels: beginner, smart, and genius. The three included games are: Concentration, where the player must memorize the location of a number of animated card, Old Maid, where the players choose matching cards from each other with the goal not to be the last holding the Joker, and Go Fish, where the players attempt to pick pairs from their opponent's hand.

meta.name: Raster Blaster
meta.desc:
Raster Blaster is one of the very first computer pinball simulations, created by Bill Budge. It was the foundation for Budge's later hit Pinball Construction Set and is an unofficial adaptation of the Williams pinball table Firepower.
The game has two difficulties - in Easy mode a ball shield is always present, but it must first be activated in Hard mode - and up to four players can play the game, taking turns.

meta.name: Regatta
meta.desc: Regatta is a boat racing game. Four players play on a single PLATO terminal and race their boat along three numbered buoys in numerical order. The first who crosses the finish line wins the race.

meta.name: Rendezvous
meta.desc: In four phases, Rendezvous simulates a real space shuttle flight from lift-off from Earth, through orbital rendezvous and approach, to alignment and docking with a space station.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Rendezvous v1.2 (1982)(Edu-Ware)(II+)-manual.pdf

p: Rendezvous with Rama (1984)(Trillium)(II+)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)[64K].zip
n: Rendezvous with Rama (1984)(Trillium)(II+)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)[64K]

meta.name: Rendezvous with Rama
meta.desc:
Based on Arthur C. Clarke's classic science fiction novel Rendezvous With Rama.
After an uncatalogued giant asteroid is discovered approaching Earth, scientists soon realize that it is in reality an alien starship. As commander of the spaceship Endeavor, and being the nearest to the gargantuan ship, it is up to you to rendezvous with it and explore it before it leaves the Solar system.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Rendezvous with Rama (1984)(Trillium)(II+)(Disk 1 of 2 Side A)[64K]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Renju
meta.desc:
PC translation of the popular board game you may know as Pentem, where the objective is to place 5 consecutive stones in a vertical, horizontal or diagonal line.
Includes 1 or two player gameplay and numerous single player options such as 10 difficulty settings, the choice to see the computer's thought process, undo moves, suggest moves, save positions, etc. Graphics consist simply of a 2d top-down view of the gameboard with your choice of a color background.

meta.name: Repton
meta.desc: Un shoot 'em up de desplazamiento lateral inspirado en Defender, pero añadiendo mucho al concepto. Vuelas a través de un paisaje que se desplaza horizontalmente en bucle, con el objetivo de proteger tu base. Hay un total de ocho enemigos en pantalla en un momento dado. Los Quarriers intentan construir la base enemiga robando partes de la tuya.

p: Revenge of Lode Runner (1986)(Brøderbund)[cr Mad Man].zip
n: Revenge of Lode Runner (1986)(Brøderbund)[cr Mad Man]

meta.name: Revenge of Lode Runner
meta.desc:
The Bungeling Empire has stolen a huge cache of gold from its rightful owners, and your mission is to infiltrate its treasury and recapture it. This entails progressing through 150 screens of platforms, ladders and ropes.
nnThe Empire has sent robotic guards down to protect the gold, and contact with any of these will cost you a life. Your method of escaping them is to press fire to dig a hole in their line of movement, thus causing them to fall in briefly, allowing you to move across the gap safely. Once all the gold has been collected, a ladder allowing you to move onto the next screen is added. Completing these screens often requires forward planning and precision.
nnThis was one of the earliest games to include a level editor, allowing the creation of new level designs with no programming skill.

meta.name: Reversi
meta.desc: Reversi is a computerized version of the board game Othello. During the game, two players take turns placing game pieces on the board in an attempt to trap pieces of their opponents color. Any trapped pieces will then be reversed in color. When the board becomes completely filled up, whomever has the most pieces of his own color on the board wins. The game can be played by two players, or one player against the computer. There are three skill levels the computer can use, and three different board sizes are available, 6 by 6, 8 by 8, and 10 by 10.

meta.name: Ribbit
meta.desc:
Ribbit is a Frogger variant which in most ways works just the same as the arcade original. The player controls a frog that first has to cross a interstate highway without being hit by a vehicle and then a river without falling into the water. The aim of the game is to reach the holes on the opposite end and get a frog into each hole. When successful the game moves on to the next level where traffic moves faster and the river has less logs and turtles to jump onto. The player starts out with three lives and one is lost every time the frog is hit, falls in the river, moves outside the screen or doesn't move quick enough. Each frog has about 35 seconds to reach a hole. Later levels have some surprises including a stomper trying to get the player.
In addition to the regular mode, there is also a demo mode. The demo mode has no limit on the number of lives but there is neither any scoring shown on the screen.

p: Ring Quest (1983)(Penguin Software)(Side A)[cr Black Bag].zip
n: Ring Quest (1983)(Penguin Software)(Side A)[cr Black Bag]

meta.name: Ring Quest
meta.desc:
Es un juego de aventura fantástica basado en texto que también incluye imágenes de la mayoría de los lugares. Contiene unas 100 localizaciones y 80 ilustraciones.
El Anillo del Caos ha esclavizado a la noble bruja Lisa, y todo el espacio-tiempo está amenazado por su influencia destructiva. Como portador del Anillo del Orden, tu misión es liberar a Lisa antes de que la realidad quede tan alterada que ya no pueda recuperarse.
Ring Quest es la secuela de "The Quest". Ring Quest te lleva de nuevo a la tierra mágica de Balema, acompañado una vez más por Gorn, tu brazo de la espada. Muy al norte se encuentra tu objetivo final, la ciudadela montañosa de la bruja. Por si su escudo de rayos verdes no fuera suficiente, pronto te encontrarás con otros obstáculos, como trolls, mantícoras, ogresas amorosas y videntes codiciosos.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Ring Quest (1983)(Penguin Software)(Side A)[cr Black Bag]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Rings Of Zilfin
meta.desc:
Rings of Zilfin is an unusual, but relatively unknown early fantasy solo RPG by Ali N. Atabek, who went on to create the Magic Candle series. It was meant for beginners to computer RPGs, featuring no character creation, simple game play and movie-like cut scenes, concentrating more on atmosphere than on character stats.
The background: Long ago, the Zilfins (mighty wizards) established an enchanted realm of peace and property in Batiniq. They created two rings of power which, worn together, made the wearer invincible. Unfortunately, the evil necromancer Lord Dragos has found one of these rings and has come to great power. The only hope to prevent him finding the second one is Reis, a young boy possessing some innate magical ability. In the introduction, Reis barely escapes Dragos' minions, and leaves his home village.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Rings of Zilfin (1985)(SSI)(Disk 1 of 3)-manual.pdf

meta.name: Robocop
meta.desc:
Patrolman Alex Murphy was killed on the streets of Detroit. The major corporation there, Omni Consumer Products (OCP) saw an opportunity to sell a new kind of law enforcement officer to the troubled city. They took what was left of Murphy, encased it in titanium armor, wiped his memory and created RoboCop. Now it's up to RoboCop to clean the streets of Detroit and eliminate the one responsible for his murder, Clarence Boddicker. But it looks like Clarence might not be the kingpin of this town...
Based on the 1987 movie of the same name, RoboCop allows the player to control RoboCop. The majority of the game is a side scroller. RoboCop can punch unarmed citizens and shoot armed citizens. He can move left, right and duck but can not jump. Different weapons can be picked up from enemies, and power-ups to restore health and/or energy. Following levels, RoboCop will have to match a criminal's face to the proper mugshot and engage in a first-person shooting bonus round.
Amiga and Atari ST versions are faithful to the original level design of the Arcade game, but they also add first-person rescue hostage scenarios, and a mini-game where the player needs to match the suspects' faces

meta.name: Robot Rascals
meta.desc:
Control a Robot Rascal in a turn-based scavenger hunt that mixes strategy, action and board-game elements.
The game included a set of physical cards, which told you what you needed to hunt for in the field or Laustenfownd. The first to find all the objects in their hand of cards wins.
Luck cards can affect the outcome of the game. They give the player the chance to modify their hand, often at the expense of another player. Steal card, Force Swap among players or pass your trash either left or right are examples of the effects of a Luck Card. Each player must draw a Luck card at the beginning of their turn.
The game allows you to choose among 10 different types of robots to use in your hunt, each with their own personality and style. You must also mind the type of terrain you wander into, because some types may damage your robot.
The game also featured handicapping options, and, like most common card games, featured many variations of the original game by changing the way the cards are discarded, exchanged or displayed on the board.

meta.name: Robotron : 2084
meta.desc: Eres un humano mutante que, por alguna rareza de la naturaleza, tiene la capacidad de disparar pulsos de energía desde su cuerpo en ocho direcciones diferentes. Tu trabajo es simple: salvar a la humanidad de su propia creación: ¡los ROBOTRONS! La jugabilidad es bastante única para la época. Puedes moverte y disparar en cualquiera de las ocho direcciones. Te lanzan a una habitación con varios enemigos malvados esparcidos, tienes que matar a todos los robots y, al mismo tiempo, encontrar la manera de agarrar humanos y evitar la muerte.

p: Robots of Dawn (1984)(Epyx)[cr Atlantic Pirates Guild][48K].zip
n: Robots of Dawn (1984)(Epyx)[cr Atlantic Pirates Guild][48K]

meta.name: Robots of Dawn
meta.desc:
In this text adventure based on Isaac Asimov's science fiction detective novel The Robots of Dawn, the player takes the role of Earth detective Elijah Baley, who is sent to the Spacer world of Aurora to investigate the murder of Dr. Han Falstofe, a prominent roboticist and constructor of humaniform robots. Suspects include Falstofe's political and professional rival Dr. Kelden Amadiro, his surrogate daughter Gladia, his biological daughter Vasilia, or ladies' man Gremionis, who was rejected by Gladia. Even Baley's robot friend and partner, R. Daneel Olivaw is not free of suspicion. In addition to the investigation, Baley must also deal with his fear of open spaces on Aurora, a result of having spent his entire life in enclosed spaces on the overcrowded Earth. Also, most Aurorans will not exactly be willing to help Baley due to anti-Earth sentiment.
As typical with adventure games in the detective genre, puzzles are less based on item manipulation but more on gathering evidence and questioning suspects. In each game played, the murderer might be a different one - the location of items and people's reaction to questions differ accordingly. To convict the killer, Baley must determine motive, means and opportunity and present his evidence to the Chairman of Aurora.

meta.name: Robotwar
meta.desc:
RobotWar is a programming strategy game in which players design and code combat robots to battle autonomously in a futuristic arena. Set in the year 2002, where human warfare has been deemed too dangerous, nations settle disputes through robot combat. Using a built-in programming language, players create robots capable of moving, scanning, and attacking without direct human control during matches.
Battles take place in an arena where robots must locate opponents using radar pulses, as they have no direct knowledge of enemy positions or movement. Success depends on clever programming, tactical decision-making, and the ability to predict an opponent's actions. Multiple robots can compete simultaneously, with the last surviving machine declared the winner. Originally created by Silas Warner on the PLATO system in the 1970s, RobotWar was later adapted for the Apple II and published by Muse Software in 1981, becoming one of the earliest and most influential programming games.

p: Rocky Horror Show, The (1986)(Activision)(II+)[cr Digital Gang][64K].zip
n: Rocky Horror Show, The (1986)(Activision)(II+)[cr Digital Gang][64K]

meta.name: The Rocky Horror Show
meta.desc:
Play as either Brad or Janet to rescue the other, in this computer adaptation of the hit Broadway Musical The Rocky Horror Picture Show.
Search the castle for pieces of the Medusa machine to free your partner from stone and escape the castle before it blasts off. Each one must be taken to the De-Medusa machine, only carrying one at a time. You must collect keys and disable force fields along the way.
Other characters in the game will hinder your progress by stealing and hiding your clothes or other objects you may be carrying. Watch out for Riff-Raff or he'll shoot you with his laser gun. And if Eddie thaws out and escapes from the freezer, he'll run you over with his motorcycle.

meta.name: Roulette
meta.desc: Roulette is a one player roulette game written in Laser C. You start with a bank roll of $10K and you have five different markers to place bets with: $1,$5, $25, $100 and $500. There is no goal or high score and when you run out of money, there is no way to continue so you have to restart the computer.

meta.name: Round About
meta.desc: Round About es un shoot 'em up de pantalla fija. Lo que lo diferencia de otros shooters de su época es que el jugador no está restringido a las partes inferiores de la pantalla. Aquí puede moverse a lo largo de cada lado de la pantalla, rodeando a los enemigos que se mueven en el centro. Los enemigos son formas abstractas que se mueven en manadas, pululando por la pantalla en patrones específicos mientras el jugador intenta golpearlos. Una vez que un enjambre ha sido eliminado, otro toma su lugar. El jugador tiene tres vidas al principio y gana nuevas después de completar cada pocos niveles. El juego tiene 24 niveles en total y después de eso vuelve a empezar.

meta.name: Run for the Money
meta.desc:
Run for the Money is an educational game about running a business. The game is for two players who share the same screen to move around their characters. The characters are bizlings, space travellers who have crash landed on Planet Simian. The crash has caused damage to the ship's paint shield and before they can leave they need to acquire paint.
To buy the needed paint it is first necessary to make some money. This is done by producing and selling synannas (synthetic bananas) to the Simian population. First the players have to acquire a raw material called ruf which is done by moving the bizling to one of the six rufhouses located on the screen. Each rufhouse has its own price which indicates its quality. After entering a rufhouse the player can wait for the price to drop but there is a risk that the seller will decide to not sell. After buying the rufs they can be brought to the space ship and manufactured into synannas which the Simians then will pick up and pay for. Sometimes it's necessary to advertise which is done by turning on a sign.

meta.name: Russki Duck
meta.desc: Russki Duck is an espionage game. The CIA building is the starting point and the single screen city is where you have to search for top-secret plans which have been hidden in, of all things, a rubber ducky. Who would ever suspect a rubber ducky after all? The player must navigate the city and it's stores to find this rubber ducky while avoiding the enemy spies while in the stores. You can find objects which you can use, otherwise you need to bring things you find back to the laser-protected room in the CIA building.

meta.name: Sabotage
meta.desc: El jugador controla una torreta de cañón en la parte inferior de la pantalla. La torreta puede girar para cubrir una gran área de la pantalla, pero no puede moverse de su base. Helicópteros vuelan a lo largo de la pantalla a diferentes alturas, dejando caer paracaidistas. El cañón puede disparar múltiples proyectiles a la vez, y los disparos pueden destruir helicópteros o derribar paracaidistas. Los paracaidistas pueden ser desintegrados por un impacto directo, o sus paracaídas pueden ser alcanzados, en cuyo caso caerán en picado a la tierra (despedazándose y muriendo si estaban lo suficientemente altos cuando el disparo impactó).

meta.name: The Count
meta.desc: You awaken in a sealed wing of the Count's Transylvanian castle, seemingly restrictive but yielding further avenues of secret passages and unexpected exits to the intrepid and timely explorer. Over the course of a few days you need to cultivate a familiarity with the layout of the castle and the nocturnal activities of its primary resident, who chooses to make midnight snacks of you rather than a main course. An unpredictable day/night cycle forces your hand, necessitating finding a reliable light source and some means of staying awake -- or else you awaken the next morning back in bed, your possessions pilfered by the kleptomaniacal creature of the night and your neck boasting irritated bite marks. Each day you must incrementally approach your goal of overcoming the Count, lest you wind up as one more dried-out apéritif.

p: SAGA #13 - The Sorcerer of Claymorgue Castle v2.3-126 (1984)(Adventure International)(II+)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Zapman].zip
n: SAGA #13 - The Sorcerer of Claymorgue Castle v2.3-126 (1984)(Adventure International)(II+)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Zapman]

p: SAGA #2 - Pirate Adventure v2.1-408 (1982)(Adventure International)(II+)(US)(Side A)[48K].zip
n: SAGA #2 - Pirate Adventure v2.1-408 (1982)(Adventure International)(II+)(US)(Side A)[48K]
