p: Sands of Mars, The (1980)(Crystal Software)[48K][req 13-sector drive].zip
n: Sands of Mars, The (1980)(Crystal Software)[48K][req 13-sector drive]

p: Sargon III (1983)(Hayden Book Company)(II+)[cr Reset Vector][48K].zip
n: Sargon III (1983)(Hayden Book Company)(II+)[cr Reset Vector][48K]

meta.name: Sargon III
meta.desc: The Sargon series were the connoisseur's chess games at the time. This edition includes variable move times, ranging from 5 seconds to infinity. Moves can be undone and you can swap sides during the game. The search feature displays its calculations as you play, which can be useful to learn from. Special tactical situations, opening move sequences and checkmate practices can be loaded in for learning purposes.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Sargon III (1983)(Hayden Book Company)(II+)[cr Reset Vector][48K]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Scary Football
meta.desc: Scary Version IIII is an arcade-style American football game that promises all the excitement of NFL action without timeouts, injuries, referee controversies, or labor disputes. The game can be played solo against the computer, with two players, or simply watched as the computer plays itself. Controls support either a joystick or keyboard, with customizable key options available.Players control the offense and defense directly on the field, with special commands for kicking and long "Bomb" passes that can gain large chunks of yardage but carry a higher risk of sacks and interceptions. Standard football rules such as punts, field goals, downs, and timeouts are included. The game also features an adjustable handicap system, allowing players to make the computer stronger or weaker. Detailed end-of-game statistics are provided, and the game's challenging AI offers a tough opponent for football fans.

meta.name: Sea Dragon
meta.desc: Sea Dragon is an arcade style side scrolling shooter. The player pilots a submarine through treacherous underwater caves in an attempt to destroy a heavily guarded reactor. Mines, gun turrets, and a variety of enemies provide obstacles to this goal. The submarine is armed with missiles, torpedoes, and a 'Big Kill' weapon (this affects everything onscreen) to help the player in their mission. The air supply is limited, so when possible the player must surface to replenish their air supply.

meta.name: Secret Agent - Mission One
meta.desc:
Weapons physicist Robert H. Melton has built the Energy Synchronizer, a weapon capable of unleashing devastating power anywhere on Earth. From his island fortress near Batangali, he demands world domination within 48 hours or he will begin destroying major cities. As a secret agent traveling under the alias Jose Caldera, you must infiltrate his operation, disable the automatic firing system that would activate the weapon if the island is attacked, and then signal your superiors to destroy the base. Once the bombing begins, you will have only 30 minutes to escape.
Your mission starts when your flight to Batangali is attacked, forcing you to parachute to safety. After escaping from a hospital, you explore Batangali for clues while evading Melton's thugs. Hidden inside a cargo crate, you reach the island and infiltrate the heavily trapped fortress. There you must overcome deadly hazards, disable the firing system, and ensure the Energy Synchronizer is destroyed before time runs out.

p: Secret of Easter Island (1983)(Nucleus Software)(Disk 1 of 2).zip
n: Secret of Easter Island (1983)(Nucleus Software)(Disk 1 of 2)

meta.name: Secret of Easter Island
meta.desc:
As an anthropologist visiting Easter Island, you learn of a legendary idol said to stop a volcano whose eruption threatens to destroy the island. Hoping to save the natives and earn the promised reward of immense riches, you set out to find the idol despite arriving with only a gold ring and three dollars after losing your supplies in a storm.
Secret of Easter Island is a graphical adventure game that uses VERB NOUN text commands. Players explore beaches, villages, deserts, jungles, castles, and volcanic caverns while solving inventory-based puzzles under a turn limit before the volcano erupts. Along the way, they battle creatures such as a salamander, poisonous spider, and scorpion, eventually transforming a witch doctor into the sacred idol. The final challenge is carrying the idol deep into the volcano and placing it on a pedestal to halt the eruption. Success saves the island and rewards the player with a fortune in diamonds.

p: Serpent's Star, The v1.0 (1983)(Brøderbund)(Side A)[cr Clean Crack Band].zip
n: Serpent's Star, The v1.0 (1983)(Brøderbund)(Side A)[cr Clean Crack Band]

meta.path: ./Serpent's Star, The v1.0 (1983)(Brøderbund)(Side A)[cr Clean Crack Band].zip
meta.name: Serpent's Star, The
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Serpent's Star, The v1.0 (1983)(Brøderbund)(Side A)[cr Clean Crack Band]-image.jpg
meta.video: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/videos/Serpent's Star, The v1.0 (1983)(Brøderbund)(Side A)[cr Clean Crack Band]-video.mp4
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Serpent's Star, The v1.0 (1983)(Brøderbund)(Side A)[cr Clean Crack Band]-marquee.png
meta.thumbnail: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Serpent's Star, The v1.0 (1983)(Brøderbund)(Side A)[cr Clean Crack Band]-thumb.jpg
meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Serpent's Star, The v1.0 (1983)(Brøderbund)(Side A)[cr Clean Crack Band]-boxback.jpg

meta.name: Serpentine
meta.desc: In Serpentine you control a multi-segmented snake in a maze; your goal is to survive and earn as many points as possible. At the beginning of each level, three enemy snakes will also enter the maze. To complete the level, you need to eat all of the enemy snakes. If an enemy snake has more segments than your snake, you can only eat segments that form the tail of the snake; a collision with the head is lethal! Each time you eat a segment, the enemy snake will become smaller until eventually you can devour it completely. For enemy snakes that have fewer segments than you, you can eat the tail segments or the head segment. From time to time, a frog will appear in the maze; eating this will cause your snake to grow. Both your snake and enemy snakes will leave eggs behind occasionally; left alone, these will hatch (your eggs earn you an extra life, enemy eggs will hatch another snake). Eggs can also be eaten to remove them from the playfield, so it's usually a good idea to eat enemy eggs before a new snake hatches! The maze changes for each level, and the enemy snakes progressively become larger and tougher to catch.

meta.name: Seventh Fleet
meta.desc:
Seventh Fleet is a strategic naval wargame simulating a hypothetical 1996 conflict between U.S. and Soviet forces in the Pacific. Playable by one or two players, each side commands fleets, aircraft, and submarines, with a computer opponent available for solo play.
Acting as a theater commander, the player organizes ships into task forces, assigns missions, directs movement, and reacts to intelligence reports. The text-only game advances in half-day turns starting on June 1, 1996. Players can split or combine task forces, monitor unit status, assign missions, anti-submarine operations, shore bombardment, or shadowing enemy fleets, and coordinate air and submarine support.
Intelligence reports reveal enemy activity and allow attacks against detected targets. As combat intensifies, the conflict may escalate into global nuclear war, ending the game immediately. Otherwise, the war concludes with a ceasefire, and both sides are scored on their overall command performance.

p: Shadows of Mordor, The (1988)(Addison-Wesley Publishing Company)(Side A).zip
n: Shadows of Mordor, The (1988)(Addison-Wesley Publishing Company)(Side A)

meta.path: ./Shadows of Mordor, The (1988)(Addison-Wesley Publishing Company)(Side A).zip
meta.name: The Shadows of Mordor
meta.desc:
The Shadows of Mordor is an officially licensed J.R.R. Tolkien "Lord of the Rings" text adventure game based on the second part of the trilogy - "The Two Towers".
You can select to play as either Frodo or Sam. The game follows the plot of the book closely and many locations have accompanying illustrations. Unusually for a text adventure game, the action happens in real time: if you sit there thinking of what to do next, things will happen in the game, e.g. creatures will move from location to location.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Shadows of Mordor, The (1988)(Addison-Wesley Publishing Company)(Side A)-image.jpg
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Shadows of Mordor, The (1988)(Addison-Wesley Publishing Company)(Side A)-marquee.png
meta.thumbnail: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Shadows of Mordor, The (1988)(Addison-Wesley Publishing Company)(Side A)-thumb.jpg
meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Shadows of Mordor, The (1988)(Addison-Wesley Publishing Company)(Side A)-boxback.jpg

meta.name: Shamus
meta.desc: Infiltrate the lair of the elusive Shadow, shoot and sneak your way through his army of robotic minions, and hunt him down before he does the same to you. Starring in the titular role of Shamus, you will have to find your way through over 120 rooms in this cross between Berzerk's "robots in a maze" action and Adventure's exploration of a predefined game world.

meta.name: Shanghai
meta.desc:
Shanghai is one of the first video game adaptations of the tile-based game played with the Chinese mahjong stones - essentially a puzzle game that has little to do with real mahjong rules.
The gameplay involves 144 tiles, each depicting different images, which have to be removed by matching them into pairs. The tiles are arranged in a gridded pattern, which is higher near the centre. A tile can only be removed if it has no tile next to it on at least one side.
This computer implementation can generate a random board layout to take on and features five preset challenges, some of which have time limits of five or ten minutes, as well as a multiplayer mode.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Shanghai (1986)(Activision)(II+)[cr First Class][48K]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Sieg in Africa
meta.desc:
Sieg in Afrika is a tactical World War II wargame covering the North African campaign between the Axis and Allied forces. Beginning in June 1940, players command the major ground formations fighting across Libya and Egypt.
Designed for one or two players, the game can be played against another person or a computer opponent. Acting as a corps-level commander, players issue orders, manage supplies, and respond to battlefield developments.
The game is entirely text-based and originally included a board and counters to track unit locations. Campaigns advance in monthly turns, each featuring intelligence and command phases. Players gather information on enemy activity before ordering forces to attack, hold, move, rush, search and sweep, or disengage. Limited fuel, food, and ammunition make logistics and reinforcement decisions critical. After each turn, radio reports summarize combat results and enemy movements.

p: Sierra Championship Boxing (1986)(Sierra On-Line)[cr Digital Gang].zip
n: Sierra Championship Boxing (1986)(Sierra On-Line)[cr Digital Gang]

meta.name: Sierra Championship Boxing
meta.desc:
One of the early Sierra On-Line releases, this game lets you design your own boxer before taking him/her/it to the ring. You can play as a black or a white guy, a cartoonish character or a kangaroo (!).
Three play modes exist:Completely interactive: You control your fighter's every move
Coaching: You tell your fighter how to fight (aggressive, jabbing, defensive, etc.) in between rounds
Completely simulated: The computer does all the fightingStats are input in text variables, allowing you to not only customize your boxer very specifically (reach, height, weight, etc.) but also recreate some of history's best fighters and pit them together in a simulation.
Two-player mode supported.

meta.name: Silent Service
meta.desc: Silent Service es una simulación de submarinos. Asumes el papel de un miembro de las fuerzas submarinas americanas y estás a cargo del contraataque contra Japón. Con diferentes niveles de realismo, y por tanto de dificultad, controla parámetros como la profundidad máxima, los torpedos, etc. El juego cuenta con un modo Campaña, además de los modos Entrenamiento y Misión Individual, que te permitirán ganar experiencia mientras te mantengas con vida.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Silent Service (1985)(Microprose)[cr First Class]-manual.pdf

p: Simulating the Great Depression 1929-1933 (1986)(Yohe, William P.).zip
n: Simulating the Great Depression 1929-1933 (1986)(Yohe, William P.)

meta.name: Simulating the Great Depression 1929-1933
meta.desc: This game uses an extension of the Anderson-Butkiewicz Model to allow the player to see the underlying model, track what happened through the time period and allows the player to conduct monetary policy differently and see the results, including changing how spending and exports are handled and adjust the housing stock to modify outcomes.

meta.name: Ski Crazed
meta.desc:
In Ski Crazed the player gets to compete in the Kilimanjaro Annual International Skiing Tournament. The game is viewed from the side and the player has to control his skier down the slope without crashing into various hazards. The player has to make his way past moguls, flags, jumps and patches of ice. The game is played with a joystick and to pass moguls and flags it has to be moved up or down depending on which kind or obstacle it is. There are two kinds of jumps, small and large ones. The small ones are of little problem but the large jumps have ice after them that has to be avoided or the player will crash. To avoid the ice, the player can do a stunt, a back scratcher or a daffy, which extends how long he is in the air. Once one slope is completed the player moves on to a harder one. 
The game also features an editor where the player can create his own slopes.

meta.name: Sky Destroyer
meta.desc: Sky Destroyer es un juego de combate aéreo. Los jugadores asumen el papel de un avión de combate que tiene que derrotar a varios combatientes enemigos en el aire y en el mar. El avión de combate del jugador está equipado con ametralladoras y también puede disparar torpedos. El objetivo principal es destruir una isla fortificada al final de cada escena disparándole un torpedo.

meta.name: Smooth Max
meta.desc:
You take the role of Max, a computer operator.
Your job is to activate all computers, which will activate inactive (white) platforms and to exit the levels, which are symbolized by a crystal.
But beware of the hammers, which go up and down!

meta.name: SnakeBYTE
meta.desc:
The object of Snake Byte is to guide your snake to the apples that appear on the screen. Gobble up an apple, and another appears. Your snake also grows longer. Gobble up ten apples without hitting a wall or any part of your own ever-growing body, and a door to the next level opens. Thread your way through the door and you start over again, this time on a more complicated screen. As you move from level to level, the obstacles become more difficult.
As you play, bars on each side of the display inch toward the top of the screen. Should they complete their journey before you've eaten an apple, you are penalized: Three more apples are added to the total you must eat before completing the level.

meta.name: Sneakers
meta.desc: Sneakers es una variación del clásico Space Invaders de Atari. Mueve tu nave a la izquierda y a la derecha y dispara a los enemigos que intentan caer y matarte. Hay ocho tipos de enemigos, cada uno usando una táctica diferente para destruirte. Comienzas con los relativamente inofensivos Sneakers y avanzas gradualmente a través de Cyclops, Saucers, Fangs, H-Wings, Meteors, Scrambles y Scrubs. Después de acabar con los Scrubs, pasas al nivel 2, que sigue el mismo patrón, pero con un nivel de dificultad incrementado.

meta.name: Snoggle (1981-06-30)(Brøderbund)[cr Sowbug][48K]
meta.desc: A clone of Pac-Man, where you as jaws must collect all points and escape lethal heads. There are also magical points to break heads by your jaws during some time. You have to collect the points, magical items, and progress from level to level.

p: Snooper Troops and the Case of the Granite Point Ghost (1982)(Spinnaker Software)[b].zip
n: Snooper Troops and the Case of the Granite Point Ghost (1982)(Spinnaker Software)[b]

meta.path: ./Snooper Troops and the Case of the Granite Point Ghost (1982)(Spinnaker Software)[b].zip
meta.name: Snooper Troops and the Case of the Granite Point Ghost (1982)(Spinnaker Software)[b]
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Snooper Troops and the Case of the Granite Point Ghost (1982)(Spinnaker Software)[b]-marquee.png

meta.name: Softporn Adventure
meta.desc: Softporn Adventure is a comedic, adult-oriented adventure game, originally written by Chuck Benton for Apple II and became the inspiration for the animated Leisure Suit Larry series. Players must type the verb and the noun to do different actions and finally break virginity of protagonist. The player's name is not yet Larry, but the plot of the game is very similar to Leisure Suit Larry in the Land of the Lounge Lizards. The player has to collect items to win over women in creative ways.

meta.name: Soko-Ban
meta.desc: Sokoban (también Soko-ban, Soko-Ban y Soukoban) es la transcripción de un término japonés que significa algo parecido a «guarda de almacén», pero ante todo, es el nombre de un tipo de rompecabezas en forma de videojuego en el que hay que guiar a un personajillo a través de una habitación para que empuje unas cajas hasta situarlas en ciertos puntos de destino. Las reglas son muy simples: Para empujar, el personaje debe estar detrás de la caja. No puede empujar desde un lado ni tirar hacia sí. No puede empujar dos cajas a la vez (pesan demasiado). Tampoco puede subirse a las cajas para abrirse camino, debe pasar limpiamente entre ellas y las paredes.

meta.name: Solitaire
meta.desc: Solitaire is a game offering the player a chance to play Klondike. Klondike Solitaire is a game of moving cards of alternate suits onto each other in an effort to move them off the tableau. In the game, you have the option to draw one or three cards and you may also change the color and the backgrounds of the playing cards with a limited set of design and color choices.

meta.name: Solo Flight
meta.desc: Solo Flight allows you to fly a small plane, the Ryan ST-A with a new look cockpit instrument panel. At the start of the game you have two options, whether to fly the plane for practise or fly the plane for a mail run. You have the choice of six American states to fly over (instead of three in the original) which are Kansas, Washington, Colorado, Michigan, Massachusetts or Texas, each having their own weather and terrain. The games viewpoint is a 3rd person perspective behind the plane again with the joystick and keyboard to control your plane. The game also included speech to help you in flying by giving instructions as you fly.

meta.name: Sorry!
meta.desc: Sorry! is a licensed computer implementation of the Parker Brothers board game. Four players take turns drawing cards from a stack, in an attempt to be the first player to move their four pieces from the starting location around the board, and back to the home location. Initially, a player must draw a 1 or a 2 to move their piece out of the starting location. Sliding pieces allow the player to move an extra distance, and if two pieces land on the same location, the original occupying piece is sent back to their home to restart their journey. The player can set any number of the 4 players to be controlled by the computer. Other unique cards include the seven, whose moves can be divided between multiple players, and the four, which allows the player to move backwards. There is also the "Sorry" card, which allows the player to switch position with an opponent piece.

p: South Pacific Quest (1985)(Ward, Peter)[cr Black Magic].zip
n: South Pacific Quest (1985)(Ward, Peter)[cr Black Magic]

meta.name: South Pacific Quest
meta.desc: The object of the game is to capture or kill Lord Futz. In order to do this, you must conquer the Futz soldiers with the aid of various devices, passing through 16 screens of hi-res action.

meta.name: Space I
meta.desc:
Players create a character in a futuristic interstellar society and enlist in a military branch such as the Navy, Army, Scouts, Merchant Marines, or other services. During four-year terms, characters can train in skills including brawling, bribery, swordsmanship, computers, navigation, and starship piloting, while also improving their physical and mental attributes.
At the end of each enlistment, surviving characters may retire or continue serving. Once retired, they can participate in various scenarios included with the game. These adventures can earn wealth, equipment, and other rewards, though they generally cannot improve character abilities. As time passes, aging gradually reduces many attributes. Death carries permanent consequences: if a character dies during a scenario, their character file is immediately deleted from the game disk.

meta.name: Space Dodge
meta.desc: Space Dodge is a 1-player space-themed arcade game for the Apple II. The player controls a spaceship which can move horizontally and fire vertically. The player confronts a number of spaceships as asteroids stream towards the player. Alien vessels fire towards the player, and the player must dodge this. When the player destroys all enemy vessels, they progress to a more difficult challenge. The player has 3 ships, and when all are lost it's game over.

meta.name: Space II
meta.desc:
This expansion of Edu-Ware's Space role-playing game includes two new scenarios:
* Shaman, in which your character launches a career on an untamed planet as a religious practitioner trying to amass followers.
* Psychodelia, in which you can enhance your characters' attributes through mind-alerting drugs at the risk of physical danger.
meta.developer: David Mullich, Steven W. Pederson, Sherwin A. Steffin

p: Space Shuttle - A Journey Into Space (1985)(Activision)[48K].zip
n: Space Shuttle - A Journey Into Space (1985)(Activision)[48K]

meta.name: Space Shuttle - A Journey Into Space (1985)(Activision)[48K]
Space Spikes
meta.desc:
Space Spikes is a space-themed arcade shooter for the Apple II featuring three distinct stages.
In the first stage, the player controls a ship that moves left and right while firing upward or sideways. Diamond-shaped enemies descend from above and attack from the flanks. The objective is to clear a path and launch through a gap in the enemy formation.
The second stage takes place in space. The player's ship moves vertically and fires left while battling spike-shaped alien vessels. Enemy ships fire back and launch homing missiles that must be shot down. Each alien craft requires three hits to destroy, and the stage ends when all have been eliminated.
In the final stage, the player pilots a larger ship capable of moving in all directions. The goal is to bomb enemy installations at the bottom of the screen while avoiding attack ships, powerful lasers, and heavy defensive fire. Destroying all enemy structures wins the game.
The player has three lives; losing them all ends the game.

meta.name: Space Station Zulu
meta.desc: You are in command of Space Station Zulu and its 43 man crew orbiting a huge gaseous giant in a farflung corner of the galaxy. Shortly after an exploration ship leaves the station the onboard computer notifies you that the sanctity of the station has been violated by unknown alien lifeforms ( which find your crew very tasty). Now you must direct your crew in a desperate struggle against this deadly peril. There is only one way to win and that is to rid Space Station Zulu of all the alien intruders. The aliens win by taking over the ship. There are 3 levels of difficulty (normal, difficult, and little hope) and this is a one player versus the computer game.

meta.name: Space Vikings
meta.desc: In Space Vikings the player flies a space craft with the ability to shoot at other ships. The goal of the game is to take over a planet so you can loot it to get things to sell for credits which can be used to buy troops, tanks, missiles, transports, and fighters.

meta.name: Speedway Classics
meta.desc: The player controls a vehicle reminiscent of Formula 1 cars, with the goal of maximizing score by attempting to finish first on a short race track. Played from a perspective behind the vehicle, the player can move left, right, or straighten their car. There are 4 difficulty levels, which determine the number of laps, the speed of opponent cars, and the presence of oil slicks. If the player strikes hard behind another vehicle, they explode, but if they bump the side, they spark, and are tossed to the side of the screen. At the end of each race, the player is rewarded a score for their time and position, but are docked points for touching the edge of the track, sparking with other vehicles, spinouts, and fire collisions.

meta.name: Spindizzy
meta.desc:
Como GERALD (Gyroscopic ExploRAtion Landing Devic) debes explorar 385 pantallas de Hangworld, usando combustible limitado. El juego se ve de forma isométrica, lo que significa que los controles se giran 90 grados. Esto, junto con el hecho de que muchas pantallas son enormes y con trayectorias diagonales, significa que al principio es difícil llegar lejos.
Gerald debe recoger cristales para recargar su energía y conseguir completar el nivel. Al moverte por cuadrados de colores, se abren puertas que te permiten avanzar a través de los niveles. Cada uno de ellos tiene diez pantallas diferentes que recorrer.

p: Spirit of Glennmore Castle, The (1984)(On Target)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Racketeers].zip
n: Spirit of Glennmore Castle, The (1984)(On Target)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Racketeers]

meta.path: ./Spirit of Glennmore Castle, The (1984)(On Target)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Racketeers].zip
meta.name: The Spirit of Glennmore Castle
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Spirit of Glennmore Castle, The (1984)(On Target)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Racketeers]-image.jpg
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Spirit of Glennmore Castle, The (1984)(On Target)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Racketeers]-marquee.png

meta.name: Spud And Mug Shoot
meta.desc:
Spud and Mug Shot are a series of two arcade games for the Apple II.
Spud is a 2-player combat game, where each player controls a turret that can rotate 360 degrees and fire. Each turret is surrounded by a shield on 3 sides with 3 openings. Moving on the screen are 3 bouncy balls and a large central circle. Each player tries to shoot their opponent while the obstacles and shields get in their way. Each game lasts 500 time segments, or until their opponent is destroyed by losing 300 health segments. Intermittently a green border will show up in the middle of the screen, which keeps the circle on that player's side of the screen.
Mug Shot is an 1-4 player game where players take turns. The player once again controls a turret, but this time shoots at bouncing objects on the left side of the screen. When an object is shot it moves over to the left of the screen. If one of the objects bounces through a crack in the player's shield, it is game over.

meta.name: Spy Hunter
meta.desc: En Spy Hunter, la acción se desarrolla desde arriba. Tu coche está equipado con ametralladoras que te ayudarán a eliminar a los numerosos enemigos de la carretera, pero ten cuidado de no disparar a civiles. De vez en cuando, te cruzarás con un furgón de armas y, si te topas con él, tu coche estará equipado con una segunda arma. En varios puntos, la carretera se divide y puedes entrar en un hangar para convertir temporalmente tu coche en un barco. Los coches intentarán sacarte de la carretera, los enemigos te dispararán desde la parte trasera de una limusina y los helicópteros te lanzarán bombas.

p: Squire - The Financial Planning Simulation v1.1 (1985)(Blue Chip Software)(Side A).zip
n: Squire - The Financial Planning Simulation v1.1 (1985)(Blue Chip Software)(Side A)

meta.path: ./Squire - The Financial Planning Simulation v1.1 (1985)(Blue Chip Software)(Side A).zip
meta.name: Squire - The Financial Planning Simulation
meta.desc:
Squire: The Financial Planning Simulation is a 1-player financial simulation game.
The player starts off with a $70,500 investment at the age of 36, and an initial $500 monthly disposable income. The goal is to maximize the player's personal finances by the time they're ready to retire at the age of 65. The goal is to invest in a variety of sources, including: blue chip stocks, growth stocks, AA corporate bonds, B corporate bonds, IRA, the money market, collectibles, commodities, real estate, oil & gas, and cattle feeding. To help guide their investing, the player can plot the performance of different stocks and commodities, as well as receive updates on possible stock movements. The player can closely watch their investment profile, and sell or buy new investments on a monthly basis. The player can choose to retire at any point they feel their finances are sufficient.
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/Squire - The Financial Planning Simulation v1.1 (1985)(Blue Chip Software)(Side A)-marquee.png

meta.name: Star Ball
meta.desc: In this budget Breakout-style game use your bat to hit the bouncing ball in an attempt to destroy a wall of bricks. Each wall cleared moves you to the next colorful level. As the game progresses alien enemies appear which can be destroyed with your ball, though interfering with its path. Use falling pickups to enhance your bat's abilities, adding features like speed-up, lasers, and level jump.

meta.name: Star Blazer
meta.desc: Star Blazer es un shooter de desplazamiento lateral de estilo similar al Super Cobra de Konami. Eres un piloto de caza en varias misiones peligrosas. En cada nivel tienes que cumplir una misión, como bombardear el radar, destruir el tanque, etc. El escenario, con edificios y almacenes, y los objetos en el aire, como aviones a reacción y cohetes, pueden destruir tu jet. Tu avión de combate está armado con un suministro limitado de balas para una ametralladora a bordo y con bombas, también con un suministro limitado. Las bombas solo se pueden lanzar desde muy baja altitud.

p: Star Fleet I v2.1 (1986-07-31)(Interstel)(Side A)[cr Blade].zip
n: Star Fleet I v2.1 (1986-07-31)(Interstel)(Side A)[cr Blade]

meta.name: Star Fleet I - The War Begins!
meta.desc:
Star Fleet I: The War Begins is a space strategy and combat simulation in which the player assumes command of a Federation starship after graduating from Starfleet Academy. The Federation is at war with the hostile Krellan and Zaldron empires, giving the player a galaxy full of missions, exploration, and battles.
Presented entirely with colorful ASCII graphics, gameplay focuses on navigation and tactical combat. From the ship's main computer interface, players explore a large star map, track enemy activity, manage ship systems, and travel between star systems either manually or through automated navigation. Combat requires careful use of weapons, shields, sensors, and energy resources to defeat enemy vessels.
Combining strategic planning, exploration, and detailed starship management, Star Fleet I expanded on the classic Star Trek-style text games and became the first entry in the long-running Star Fleet series.

p: Star League Baseball (19xx)(Gamestar Software)[cr Hi-Res Hijackers - Club 68000].zip
n: Star League Baseball (19xx)(Gamestar Software)[cr Hi-Res Hijackers - Club 68000]

meta.path: ./Star League Baseball (19xx)(Gamestar Software)[cr Hi-Res Hijackers - Club 68000].zip
meta.name: Star League Baseball
meta.desc:
Star League Baseball is a baseball simulation released in 1983 by Gamestar, Inc. for home computers. While its teams featured generic rosters and a simple statistics engine, the game introduced several innovations that helped shape future sports simulations. Presentation mimicked a televised baseball broadcast, featuring attendance figures, trivia, and scores from other games between innings. More importantly, it introduced pitcher fatigue, bringing a human element to gameplay beyond basic statistics.
The game still had limitations. Relief pitchers could only enter in the seventh inning or at the start of extra innings, all players were right-handed, and every baserunner had the same speed. Despite these restrictions, Star League Baseball marked a major step toward realistic sports simulation by modeling aspects of the game beyond raw numbers. Its innovations helped pave the way for modern baseball and sports management games.

meta.name: Star Maze
meta.desc:
Star Maze is an arcade-style space action game set within a sprawling 16-level labyrinth. The player pilots an explorer ship through maze-like corridors, searching each level for nine power jewels that must be delivered to a mothership before fuel runs out or hostile aliens destroy the ship.
Only a portion of the maze is visible at any time, with the environment scrolling smoothly around the player as they explore. The maze is filled with fortress-like walls, obstacles, and dangers, creating a constant challenge as players navigate the labyrinth. Featuring colorful graphics, multidirectional scrolling, and fast-paced gameplay, Star Maze combines exploration, resource management, and arcade action. Easy to learn but difficult to master, the game encourages repeated play as players uncover the secrets hidden throughout its vast and dangerous universe.

p: Star Trek - Strategic Operations Simulator (1983)(Sega)[cr Dr. Micro - Freeze][t +3][48K].zip
n: Star Trek - Strategic Operations Simulator (1983)(Sega)[cr Dr. Micro - Freeze][t +3][48K]

meta.path: ./Star Trek - Strategic Operations Simulator (1983)(Sega)[cr Dr. Micro - Freeze][t +3][48K].zip
meta.name: Star Trek : Strategic Operations Simulator
meta.desc:
Star Trek: Strategic Operations Simulator (also known as Star Trek: SOS and Star Trek Arcade) is a space combat simulation arcade game based on the original Star Trek television program and movie series, and released by Sega in 1983.
Unlike most arcade games of the time, the player is presented with multiple views of the play field. Throughout the game, survival depends on the player's ability to effectively use and manage shield energy, photon torpedoes, and warp energy. These are replenished by docking with starbases, which sometimes must be saved from destruction at the hands of the Klingons.

p: Star Trek - The Kobayashi Alternative (1985)(Simon & Schuster Interactive)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Gadget Master - Captain Avatar].zip
n: Star Trek - The Kobayashi Alternative (1985)(Simon & Schuster Interactive)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Gadget Master - Captain Avatar]

meta.path: ./Star Trek - The Kobayashi Alternative (1985)(Simon & Schuster Interactive)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Gadget Master - Captain Avatar].zip
meta.name: Star Trek : The Kobayashi Alternative
meta.desc: Star Trek: The Kobayashi Alternative was a Star Trek themed computer software game, designed for the Apple II Plus, Apple IIe, and Apple IIc. The game was also available for the Commodore 64, Macintosh and IBM PC. This text adventure was first published in 1985 by Simon & Schuster. The player assumes the role of Captain James T. Kirk. As Kirk, the player commands the actions of the Enterprise crew, as well as the Enterprise itself. The game was expansive and ambitious, but also very buggy.

p: Star Wars (19xx)(Frontuto, John)[see Eamon Adventure #006 - The Death Star].zip
n: Star Wars (19xx)(Frontuto, John)[see Eamon Adventure #006 - The Death Star]

meta.path: ./Star Wars (19xx)(Frontuto, John)[see Eamon Adventure #006 - The Death Star].zip
meta.name: Star Wars
meta.desc:
Star Wars is a first-person shooter based around the final scenes of the original Star Wars film. It first appeared for arcade coin-operated machines and was subsequently converted to other gaming platforms.
You take on the role of Luke Skywalker, aiming to destroy the Death Star. In the first phase you have to pass swarms of TIE Fighters, including dogfighting the one piloted by Darth Vader himself. In the second phase the station's surface is protected by laser towers, third involves attacking its weakest spot - the unprotected exhaust port - with proton torpedos. Your X-Wing starts with six shields, but more can be awarded for good performance. Complete the game and it loops back around at ever-increasing difficulty.
The game uses vector graphics, which allow lots of action at high speed on comparatively slow systems.

meta.name: Starglider
meta.desc:
The Planet Novenia has been attacked by the Egrons, who snuck in by disguising their craft as the protected Starglider species, which Novenia's automated defenses were programmed not to shoot. 
Your task is to fight them off using only an obsolete Airborne Ground Attack Vehicle. This was designed as the ultimate combat machine, with sophisticated hardware up to Draziw Yarg's high standards. It has a fully-rechargeable shield, plasma drive unit, an inbuilt scanner and 10,000-sector map, a top speed of 2550 Urads and two homing missiles.
The game is viewed from within AGAV's cockpit, with control over speed, weapon targeting and firing. There are several control options to decide exactly how the craft works, with fixed or floating sighting, and the option of automatic centering, allowing you to balance the game between easy sighting and advanced shooting ranges.
Repair depots must be visited frequently, as they can refill the weapon bays as well as repairing the ship. Crucially, the Egron Stargliders need 3 missiles to shoot down, so at least 1 visit to a depot is required to complete this job, at which point you move up to the next, harder, level.

meta.name: Starmines
meta.desc:
Starmines is a 1-player space-themed arcade shooter for the Apple II.
The player must guide their spacecraft through an endless minefield in space. The mines come out of nowhere, and the player must destroy them with their single laser cannon. The player can move quickly to the left and right and can fight vertically to destroy mines as they approach. After destroying all mines within a quadrant, the player will jump into hyperspace to move to the next quadrant. There are a total of five levels. If the player is struck by a mine, they are destroyed.

p: Starquest - Rescue at Rigel (1980)(Epyx)[no boot][32K Cassette Version].zip
n: Starquest - Rescue at Rigel (1980)(Epyx)[no boot][32K Cassette Version]

meta.path: ./Starquest - Rescue at Rigel (1980)(Epyx)[no boot][32K Cassette Version].zip
meta.name: Starquest - Rescue at Rigel
meta.desc:
Rescue at Rigel is a 1980 science fiction computer role-playing game written and published by Automated Simulations (later known as Epyx). It is based on a modified version of their Temple of Apshai game engine, which was part of most of their releases in this era. The game was released for the Apple II, IBM PC (PC Booter), TRS-80, VIC-20, and Atari 8-bit family.
The game requires the player to search out a space fortress looking for ten hostages. Presented in a top-down view, the player can only see the area immediately around them, so the entire base has to be searched room by room. There is a 60 minute time limit on the mission.
Rescue at Rigel was soon followed by Star Warrior, and the two rebranded to be part of their "Starquest" series, although Star Warrior used a more heavily modified game engine than Rigel.

meta.name: Starship Commander
meta.desc:
Starship Commander is a 1-player space-themed strategy game for the Apple II.
The player controls the USS Ranger in turn-based space combat against 1 - 3 enemy Vegan vessels. All gameplay is via Apple paddles from menu commands. The player controls movement of the vessel in 2-dimensional space, and must control direction and acceleration of the ship. The player controls the communication, defense, engineering, life support, navigation, science, and weapons stations of the their vessel. The player's crew has a total of 120 crew members, who must be assigned to different stations. The player can attempt to bluff or threaten enemy vessels, and must carefully control the different stations, the number of crew devoted to each area to keep their ship in fighting condition.

meta.name: Station Five
meta.desc:
Action-packed arcade gameplay set on the Moon's last surviving nuclear power station. After four lunar power plants were destroyed by asteroid storms, a lone repair droid must keep the final facility operational while generating and transmitting energy to Earth before another storm arrives.
The game consists of four arcade-style stages. First, players manage reactor cooling ducts while avoiding meteors and preventing a catastrophic meltdown. Next, they build transmission towers by collecting parts and dodging hazards to speed power delivery. In the Transporter Room, players must activate all 32 floor switches while avoiding enemies that can undo their progress. Finally, at an orbiting relay station, they repair damaged satellite arrays, collect fuel and spare parts, and avoid space debris while maintaining the energy beam to Earth.
Players begin with three lives. Successfully transmitting all stored energy advances them to increasingly difficult challenge levels.

meta.name: Stellar 7
meta.desc: Gir Draxon, Supreme Overlord of the Arcturan Empire has declared war on the Terran Federation. The leaders of Earth respond by sending the Terran Fleet on their way to Arcturus to intercept Draxon's armada. But this was a deadly mistake: the Arcturans have discovered warplinks, which allow them to transport troops from one link to another instantaneously, even across vast interstellar distances. With the fleet halfway across the galaxy, the enemy has already reached the Sol system and occupied Saturn's moon Titan. Now, humanity's only hope is the experimental Raven armored vehicle. One courageous pilot must take the Raven through seven different star systems, from Titan to Arcturus, defeating any and all Arcturans, using the warplinks to travel from one system to the next.

meta.name: Stellar Trek
meta.desc:
A Stellar Trek is an early Star Trek variant for the Apple II.
The player takes control of the Enterprise, as they try to destroy all Klingon vessels located in a 64 quadrant galaxy that is further divided into 10 x 10 sectors. The galaxy included Federations and Romulan starbases, stars, planets, and black holes. The player controls all ship functions, including: movement via warp and impulse engines, use of starship weaponry, maintenance of energy reserves, and repair of incurred damage. The player has access to short ranges scanners which investigate the nearby sectors and long range scanners that provide limited information on surrounding quadrants. Local planets can be mined for dilithium to increase energy. There are 5 levels of difficulty and 3 game lengths available.

meta.name: Strip Poker - David & Tony
meta.desc: A second data disk for Strip Poker: A Sizzling Game of Chance containing two new male opponents: David and Tony (Ernie and Earl in Atari version). The master disk of the main game is required to use this data disk.

meta.name: Strip Poker - Marlena & Candi
meta.desc: A first data disk for Strip Poker: A Sizzling Game of Chance containing two new female opponents: Candi and Marlena. The master disk of the main game is required to use this data disk.

meta.name: Sub Mission
meta.desc:
An evil alien warlord has kidnapped two Earthlings, Peter and Sigourney. He agrees to free them only on one condition: somebody must defeat him in a dangerous game that consists of piloting submarines with a remote control. Who is brave enough to stand up to this challenge? And who can assume this grave responsibility - since the destruction of the submarine leads to the death of the two humans?...
Sub Mission incorporates elements of a submarine simulation and action. The player must preserve the submarine intact, find the two kidnapped humans, and safely bring them home. It is possible to assign robots as the submarine crew; their destruction won't automatically end the game. However, should the player fail, e.g. have his/her submarine destroyed, Peter and Sigourney will die. The game actually erases these characters from the disk, with only one chance at resurrection; should the player fail this chance as well, the game will be over permanently, i.e. it will be impossible to play again.

meta.name: Submarine Commander
meta.desc: You're the commander of a submarine in enemy waters.  Use your skills, radar, and luck to take down the enemy ships.  With 8 difficulty settings there is a lot of the game to master as you must start to keep an eye on your fuel, torpedoes, radar, and enemy while avoiding depth charges as the difficulty rises.  You can fire up to two torpedoes at a time. While enemy shows go by your periscope sink as many as you can.  The periscope can turn 360 degrees and look off into the horizon or close to your sub.  Some ships move slower than others, and one of the ships moves so fast that the only way to hit it is by tracking it by radar instead of visually.

meta.name: Super Bunny
meta.desc:
Un juego de acción simple en el que el jugador es un conejo que debe cruzar la pantalla inicialmente de izquierda a derecha evitando las diversas criaturas desagradables en su camino.
Cuando el jugador llega al lado derecho de la pantalla, su objetivo es reclamar la zanahoria que se cuelga delante de ellos. Si obtienen la zanahoria, se transforman en Super Bunny y deben comenzar su viaje de regreso a través de la pantalla. En esta forma súper poderosa, el jugador puede vengarse y derrotar a las criaturas que se interponen en su camino.
Super Bunny es similar a los juegos "cross the road", como el juego arcade Frogger y Activision's Freeway .

meta.name: Super Huey UH-IX
meta.desc: Super Huey UH-IX is a helicopter simulation game. You are the pilot of the experimental helicopter UH-1XA and you need to test it out in several game modes. In the flight school mode, you can learn how to fly. The computer will guide you through takeoff, flight, and landing. In reality mode, you will need to fly a mission to locate and bomb an enemy base and return home safely. The last mode is arcade mode where you have an unlimited amount of ammo to try and shoot down as many enemy aircraft as possible. The game is played from a first person view of the cockpit which is complete with radar and various instruments to help you fly.

meta.name: Super Puckman v2.3 (19xx)(Ado-Soft)[cr]
meta.desc:
Super Puckman is a 1-player Pac-Man clone for the Apple II.
The player moves about a maze, trying to pick up all the pellets. They can be Pack-Man or Pack-Gril. The player is chased by 4 ghosts, who become temporarily vulnerable if the player picks up one of the power pellets in the corners of the maze. The player starts with 3 lives. There are 9 levels of difficulty, and the player can control game speed and color.

meta.name: Super Stellar Trek
meta.desc: The player controls the USS Enterprise as they try to eliminate all of the Klingon vessels which threaten the Federation. The player's actions are timed, as they explore a large galaxy arrayed into 64 quadrants in an 8 x 8 grid. Each quadrant is an 8 x 8 grid, which can contain Romulan vessels, black holes, space stations, and planets. The player names the officer who controls the various ship stations, and the player must enact ship repairs, operate the helm, weapons, and other aspects of the ship to control its function. The Enterprise is armed with phasors and a limited torpedo supply to battle enemy vessels. The player can visit a variety of planets via shuttle craft or transporter, and can mine for dilithium crystals on these sites. All command are entered via text prompts. The player controls the length of their game, which effects the relative size of the galaxy, and chooses from 5 difficulty levels.

meta.name: Super Sunday
meta.desc: A strategy, turn based football game with teams and player stats based off of real life football. Play against the computer or human opponent while selecting teams and plays.

meta.name: Swashbuckler
meta.desc:
Swashbuckler is an early fighting game.  The whole game takes place on the same screen.  You're in the center and enemies such as humans, snakes, and rats come at you from either side.  You've got a sword that can strike high, low or center, or block. 
As the game progresses the number and speed of the enemies increase until they're too fast for a human to handle.
This was pretty similar to another Datamost title, "Aztec" which was an Indiana Jones type side-scroller.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Swashbuckler (1981)(Datamost)(II+)[m instructions Sledge Hammer][48K]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Sword of Kadash
meta.desc:
After traveling from the Mediterranean to Persia in search of fortune, you are betrayed and captured by brigands. To save your life, you must enter the Fortress of the Dragon and recover the legendary Sword of Kadash.
This top-down action-adventure takes place in a maze of rooms and corridors filled with monsters, traps, and treasure. The player moves in eight directions and attacks with ricocheting darts, which can defeat enemies and interact with walls. Defeating monsters earns experience, increasing levels and hit points over time.
Weapons include daggers, morning-stars, axes, and swords, which can be enchanted up to +3. Better armor, rings, and weapons are equipped automatically when found. Players can also collect healing items, keys, scrolls, and holy symbols. Scrolls cast spells that temporarily frighten monsters, while cursed items remain cursed until cleansed. Secret passages, invisible walls, alarms, and hidden traps reward careful exploration.

p: SwordThrust 1 - The King's Testing Ground (1981)(CE Software)(II+)(Master Disk)[48K].zip
n: SwordThrust 1 - The King's Testing Ground (1981)(CE Software)(II+)(Master Disk)[48K]

meta.path: ./SwordThrust 1 - The King's Testing Ground (1981)(CE Software)(II+)(Master Disk)[48K].zip
meta.name: SwordThrust 1 - The King's Testing Ground (1981)(CE Software)(II+)(Master Disk)[48K]
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/images/SwordThrust 1 - The King's Testing Ground (1981)(CE Software)(II+)(Master Disk)[48K]-marquee.png

meta.name: Tactical Armour Command
meta.desc:
TAC: Tactical Armor Command is a game of WWII tactical armored combat. You pick a nation (Britain, USA, Germany or Russia). Build a combat team of tanks, assault guns and tank destroyers and command the team you have created against the forces of the enemy.
The computer handles the technical details which leaves the decision-making to you. You search for the enemy, find them, aim your gun, knock them out and the results of the combat are determined by the computer. The game can be played solitaire or by two people.

meta.name: Taipan
meta.desc:
Loosely based on Clavell's Taipan, this game is a trading simulation set in the Far East during the 1800s. The player is based out of Hong Kong and has a single trading ship with which he can transport one of four cargoes from place to place. There are different cargoes to be traded, from the cheaper general goods to the more expensive arms and silks, culminating in the opportunity to trade in the illegal opium trade.
     Generally, the strategy here was buy low, sell high. To add some tension to the game, there is a random chance that you will encounter several obstacles to your trading empire. Random pirates, pickpockets, and thieves who rob you and your warehouse are all out there for their own benefit (and your detriment!) Li Yuen acts as the local mafia boss, extorting increasing sums of money for his "protection". Local authorities will seize your opium and fine you if you're caught, ruining an otherwise successful voyage. Local market conditions can render a price extremely high or low, giving you a chance to make a quick buck.
     You have the option to trade up to a larger ship as time goes buy, as well as outfit your current ship with guns. However, beware! The larger the ship, the more pirate ships it attracts! Also, the larger ships are more expensive to repair. 
     You can play as long as you want--you're given the opportunity to retire once you reach one million. You're given a score at the end of the game based on your net worth, your ship size, and a few other factors.     
     All in all, this is a pretty decent trading simulation, even if the monochrome graphics left something to be desired. Although some parents may want to avoid a game with opium in it, overall it's great for teaching basic economic concepts to kids.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Taipan (1982)(Avalanche)-manual.pdf

meta.name: Tanktics
meta.desc: Tanktics is a game in which you are required to design and create tanks which can then be used to defend your base. To carry this assignment out you must gather resources which can then be dropped into a crazy machine called the Part-o-Matic. This machine also acts as your base. This game seems to be aimed at more casual or younger gamers, as the graphics are very simple and, when complete, you can give your tanks just two commands: move and shoot.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Tanktics (1981)(Avalon Hill)-manual.pdf

meta.name: Tapper
meta.desc:
Tapper is an action game where you're a beer tapper (barman) and have to serve beer to demanding customers. There are four customers, each has its own lane lengthier than the previous one, whom you have to keep at bay. If they reach the end of the lane without receiving their beer, you're a goner; if you miss one and accidentally spill beer needlessly you lose a life and should one of them throw you back the mug and you fail to catch it, you lose a life as well.
Occasionally a bizarre icon will appear on one of the lines and, should you grab it, will invoke a "cabarette"-style show which will keep some of the customers occupied.
The Xbox 360 version includes bonus rounds, as well as co-op and versus multiplayer modes, also online, along with achievements.

meta.name: Tarturian
meta.desc:
Tarturian was a follow up to  Highlands Computer Service's first game, Oldorf's Revenge. It was largely an expansion of the ideas introduced in Oldorf's Revenge.  This was a very early hi-res vector graphics and text adventure game of the same era and style as Mystery House.  Intriguingly, Tarturian, like Oldorf's Revenge,  allowed you to choose to switch between various types of characters, such as the Wizard, Thief, Strongman, Elf, etc.  The game has two mechanics limiting the number of times you can "change" into each character, which layered on an element of resource management that was unique for an adventure game of its day.  Each of these characters allowed certain specialized text parser commands.  For example, only the Wizard can "cast" (a spell) while only the Thief can "unlock", "pick", "open", etc.  This list of text commands is only knowable if you have the instruction manual for the game.  This also allows for multiple solutions to the same puzzle.  For example, the initial door can be unlocked by the Thief, or blasted off it's hinges if the Wizard casts a spell.  This is very much unlike more popular games of the era like Mystery House, which allowed for only one solution to each puzzle.  
Additionally, the manual provided other info about how to play that is essential knowledge for gameplay. (Unlike the modern gaming era, in 1980 it was expected that players would read everything provided with a game.) 
From that point on, Tarturian becomes a graphics + text adventure game, with some interesting elements not commonly found in more standard examples of the era.  For example, the game introduces randomized monster encounters that can instantly kill the character you're playing as, forcing you to switch to another character to proceed.
The game finishes with what is one of the very first "boss monsters" found in computer gaming, with a very satisfying animated conclusion.

meta.name: Taxman
meta.desc:
Taxman is a pretty accurate clone of the arcade smash hit Pac-Man where the player takes the role of the "Taxman" and has to collect tax from the rebellious citizens.
You control the now famous circular dot muncher that has to go around the maze and collect all the "tax pellets".  He is chased by four ghosts that will kill the poor taxman on touch. If the Taxman eats a power pellet, the roles are reversed and he can chase the ghosts and eat them in return. Levels repeat with increased difficulty and once three lives are lost, it is Game Over.

meta.name: Telengard
meta.desc:
Telengard is an earlier example of a "dungeon crawler" role-playing game--albeit with a top-down view- with either real time movement (DOS version) or turn-based movement and turn based combat. Telengard is also played in real time, which means that monsters can attack even when the player's character is not moving. 
The player controls a single adventurer, selecting randomly-rolled sets of attributes: Strength, Intelligence, Wisdom, Constitution, Dexterity, and Charisma. The vast Telengard dungeon consists of 50 levels down and 200 by 200 rooms each level. Experience is awarded not only for defeating enemies, but also for finding treasure. When the player character gains a level, their attributes increase, and they gain the ability to cast higher-leveled spells.
meta.genre: Juego de Rol / RPG de Mazmorras

p: Tempete sur les Bermudes (1986)(Froggy Software)(FR)(Side A).zip
n: Tempete sur les Bermudes (1986)(Froggy Software)(FR)(Side A)

meta.name: Tempete sur les Bermudes
meta.desc:
Your strong taste for adventure and sardines in oil led you to participate in the solo deckchair. Everything is bathed in plankton, until this day when an unexpected and sneaky storm surprises you from behind, the traitor.
Before you can even say "unconstitutionally", a blade is sweeping your deck. Lack of pot, it's just the moment you are there! In the next minute you lose consciousness, your boat, your balance and your head.
But Neptune is a nice god and rather than renewing the coup of the Rainbow Warrior, he drops you groggy (software) on a deserted beach.
When you open an eye clouded with algae, you see that you are on an island. Your boat ran aground not far from there. Do not panic. It is a question of repairing as quickly as possible so that we can start again. It's your turn !
Adventure game entirely in French and in colors made by Christophe Bourrier. The cover design is by Jean Solé, the graphics by Philippe Bousquet.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Tempete sur les Bermudes (1986)(Froggy Software)(FR)(Side A)-manual.pdf

n: Tennis Championship (1985)(Levy, Jean-Claude)(fr)[cr Association of Broadcasting Crackers]

meta.name: Tennis Championship
meta.desc:
Tennis Championship is a 1 - 2 player sports game.
A 1 on 1 or versus computer tennis game that follows standard tennis rules, including starting serve and shots out of bounds. Players choose to play as famous tennis players from the mid 1980s. There are options for clay, grass, or hard court, and games can be saved in progress.

meta.name: Terrorist Game System
meta.desc: Terrorist is a two-player negotiation strategy game for the Apple II that pits a government against a terrorist organization in a high-stakes crisis. Scenarios include a hijacked airliner, a threatened nuclear attack, or a hostage-filled building. Before play begins, the terrorist player secretly creates a terrorist cell, defining its ideology, demands, and operational capabilities. Both players then issue commands simultaneously using Apple Paddles, creating uncertainty and the potential for miscommunication. The government player must balance meeting demands to protect civilians against taking decisive action that could increase casualties. The terrorist player seeks to maximize concessions while keeping the organization intact. Success depends on negotiation, risk assessment, and anticipating the opponent's actions.

meta.name: Tharolian Tunnels
meta.desc: Tharolian Tunnels es un juego de acción que contiene cinco partes con jugabilidad variada. La tarea del jugador es entrar al planeta Tharolia a través de un túnel y alcanzar su centro, donde su sistema de defensa tiene que ser destruido. En el camino hacia el centro, el jugador tiene que evitar ser asesinado por las defensas y evitar chocar contra las paredes del túnel o quedarse sin combustible.

meta.name: Theatre Europe
meta.desc:
Este juego cubre los primeros 30 días de lo que entonces se temía que fuera la próxima guerra en Europa. Puedes elegir ser el comandante supremo de la OTAN o del Pacto de Varsovia. Controla las unidades aéreas y terrestres. Estos son apoyados por su capacidad de lanzar ataques estratégicos nucleares/químicos.
Como comandante del Pacto de Varsovia, tu tarea es romper las líneas de la alianza de la OTAN, a la que ves como una poderosa fuerza invasora, posicionada en tu frontera. Su primera tarea es tomar el control de Alemania Occidental.
Como comandante de la OTAN, su tarea es detener la invasión rusa. Mantener las líneas en la frontera de Alemania Occidental. Lo conseguirá impidiendo la ocupación de Alemania Occidental.
El título es un juego de estrategia por turnos.

meta.name: Theseus And the Minotaur
meta.desc:
This is one of the earliest electronic adaptation of the Theseus and the Minotaur pencil-and-paper maze puzzle by Robert Abbott, based on the concept of logic mazes where certain limitations in movement apply.
The game consists of ten mazes to solve, designed by the original author as well as Toby Nelson. Players need to guide Theseus (represented by a red circle) towards the exit of the maze, while avoiding the minotaur (represented by a black circle). The goal of each level is to reach the exit, but this cannot be done by travelling towards it directly. Theseus is only able to move one tile at at time, while the minotaur can do two. To reach the exit, Theseus has to outsmart the minotaur by luring him into a specific position, exploiting the internal movement logic. The minotaur will not chase after Theseus directly, but approaches him based on rules of vertical and horizontal movement. For instance, when Theseus is on the same horizontal line as the minotaur, but with a wall between them, he is able to move left and right as much as he wants without having the minotaur move. At certain moments it is also needed to delay an action or skip the turn, to lure the minotaur into a certain path without moving Theseus.
The game is entirely controlled by the keyboard for movement. Other keys are used to reset the maze, move on to the previous or the next one, and delay a turn (sometimes needed to lure the minotaur into a specific direction). The basic graphical design of the game went through different stages over the years.

meta.name: Thexder
meta.desc: Thexder, un shooter de plataformas de Japón, ofrece muchos niveles y diversos enemigos que aumentan gradualmente en dificultad. Pilotas un robot al estilo Battletech capaz de cambiar de un 'mech a un jet en cualquier momento. Su arma apunta automáticamente, pero consume una cantidad fija de energía recargable; Si te quedas sin esta energía, tendrás que esperar unos segundos antes de poder disparar de nuevo. Tu 'mech también contiene un escudo que puede activarse para protegerte de cualquier daño, pero esto dura unos segundos y consume parte de tu energía vital.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Thexder (1987)(Synergistic Software)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Blade]-manual.pdf

n: TimeQuest - Inca (1985)(Hayden Software)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Six Pack][h Disk Doctor]

meta.path: ./TimeQuest - Inca (1985)(Hayden Software)(Disk 1 of 2)[cr Six Pack][h Disk Doctor].zip
meta.name: TimeQuest - Inca
meta.desc:
You are a space pilot who can travel through time. Now your mission is to land in South America at the time of the European invasion, and find the mysterious treasure of the great Inca civilization.
Inca is a game that combines adventure and action, or to put it more correctly, it's an adventure games with many action elements (such as space combat).

meta.name: Titan Empire
meta.desc:
Your ship has photon torpedoes and a warp drive, it "beams" stuff up and down, it's shaped like a saucer with two protruding nacelles... yeah, you guessed it - it's another space combat/strategy game which has nothing to do with Star Trek at all!  Rather, it takes place in the solar system, which is on the verge of being conquered by the forces of Titan.  The oily Saturnian moon is home to an evil expansionist regime, which has already annexed a few planetary bodies, and is hellbent on capturing them all.
The remaining free worlds have chosen you to lead the resistance.  Your task - defend friendly planets and moons, crush the Titan fleets and retake the lost colonies.
You may travel freely throughout the continuous game world, and between its planets and moons, each of which is a potential ally or foe.  Everything is carried out in real time; a radar screen, a map and a planetary info sheet help keep track of the proceedings. Even the positions of planets and moons are continuously updated as they creep along on their orbits.
Conquest is accomplished by transporting armies from friendly locations down to enemy worlds; you'll have to take into account planetary defenses, refuel and repair at friendly bases as needed, and directly engage hostile spacecraft using your shields, torpedoes and tracking missiles. Meanwhile, enemy forces will attempt conquests of their own, and your presence will often be required for defense.  Should your ship be lost in battle, it will respawn at the nearest friendly planet, but any personnel it might have carried is lost forever.
For each new game, you may choose to use the same solar system from your previous session, or start with a new randomized configuration.

meta.name: Tomahawk
meta.desc:
Flight simulation specialists Digital Integration turned their attention to helicopter flight in this recreation of the AH-64 Apache. All the controls are realistically simulated, so the airspeed is dependent on the height of the vehicle, and you can move horizontally using the rudder option as well as rolling left and right. If using a Sinclair joystick interface you can use both ports to have all these controls accessible on dual joysticks, just like the real thing. Either way, a full instrument panel allows monitoring of these.
You are armed with cannons, rockets and anti-tank missiles, each of which has its target set up independently, but only one can be loaded at once. Flight options include day/night, clear/cloudy and air turbulence on/off, allowing beginners and experts to find a suitable setting. Scenery includes trees, mountains and pylons, while enemy targets include helicopters, turrets and tanks.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Tomahawk (1987)(Datasoft)(II+)[cr 6502 Crew][64K]-manual.pdf

meta.name: Top Fuel Eliminator
meta.desc:
Top Fuel Eliminator is a drag racing game.  Select your tires, engine, and other stats before the race and try not to crash in the final race.
 It has both a first person view (at the bottom) and a third person side view (at the top) during the actual race.
 You are entered into a 16 race bracket, win four races to take the title.

meta.name: Towers of Hanoi
meta.desc: Towers of Hanoi is a puzzle game where the idea is to move a pyramid of seven disks of varying sizes in order from Peg 1 to Peg 3. You can move a disk to Peg 1, 2 or 3 but you can't place a disk on another disk that is smaller. As you move a disk your moves counter increases so the aim is to complete the puzzle in the fewest moves.

meta.name: Track Attack
meta.desc: Track Attack is a futuristic 3D racing game in the style of Hi-Octane. The player races through futuristic cities and tunnels trying to beat their opponents. This game also features damage, and some special upgrades. Perhaps the most innovative feature is the Dynamic Track System, which allows the 39 circuits to change during the race. There are arrows the player can drive over which make the track behind them change for their opponent (obstacles popping up for example), therefore making it harder for a driver to catch up.

meta.name: Tranquility Base
meta.desc:
Experience the thrills of space flight with this realistic space flight simulation.  You pilot the Lunar Excursion Module (LEM), a sophisticated cargo vehicle that shuttles astronauts and equipment to and from the moon.  Colorful graphics and real-life controls make every flight an adventure.  Land on the foreboding lunar surface or rocket from Tranquility Base into moon orbit.
Tranquility Base portrays space flight and lunar landings with true-to-life accuracy.  The laws of motion and gravitation affect the LEM spacecraft much like they influence an actual spacecraft in outer space.  Using keyboard or joystick controls, you can increase or decrease engine thrust, change the pitch -90 to +90 degrees, and roll from -10 to +10 degrees, refuel your spacecraft, and start the engines.

meta.name: Transylvania
meta.desc:
Transylvania is an adventure with optional graphics, and a VERB NOUN interface. Two weeks ago, Princess Sabrina suddenly vanished and remained unseen since. King John the Good asks any travellers to help in locating her - you volunteer. 
As you begin your quest in the dark forests of Transylvania, beware of the evil that lurks. You will encounter strange creatures like a vampire and a goblin, and experience strange things. Most importantly, a werewolf is constantly chasing you, until you find the pistol and bullets necessary to kill it. Remember fellow traveller, take care...

meta.name: Treasure Island
meta.desc: Treasure Island is a text adventure, an adaptation of the homonymous classic 1883 Scottish novel. Unlike the book, the game is written in second person. Otherwise, it follows the original plot closely: the player assumes the role of Jim Hawkins, and has to perform the same actions as the character in the novel. This leads to a more claustrophobic design, as players without knowledge of the book have to guess what to do next. To help alleviate that problem, other characters act as some sort of on-line hint system. The player can ASK them ABOUT anything and they will provide clues when these are available. The parser accepts most of the Infocom's standard features like pronouns and chain commands.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Treasure Island (1985)(Windham Classics)(Disk 1 of 3)-manual.pdf

p: Tresor de Zonguldek, Le (1984)(Didier, Arnaud)(FR)(Side A).zip
n: Tresor de Zonguldek, Le (1984)(Didier, Arnaud)(FR)(Side A)

meta.name: Troll's Tale
meta.desc: An evil troll has hidden all of the treasures of Mark, The Dwarf King. There are 15 treasures hidden throughout the land that you need to help locate. If you locate a treasure, but the troll is on the screen at the time, you will need to come back and find a way to make him leave. Troll's Tale is an adventure game which requires minimal typing, using the spacebar to select from several possible actions which are listed on the screen and pressing enter to perform the selected action.

meta.name: Tron
meta.desc:
Based on the 1982 Midway arcade game based off the Disney film, players take control of TRON in four different game grid scenarios:
Light Cycles: race TRON's blue Light Cycle against one or more enemy Cycles in an attempt to have them crash and de-rez against their own light walls.
I/O Tower: blast through numerous Grid Bugs in an attempt to get into the I/O Tower.
MCP Cone: blast through the defensive walls of the MCP in order to get into its central cone.
Battle Tanks: maneuver TRON's tank against one or more enemy tanks (or Recognizers) to be the last one standing.
The Xbox Live version offers competitive or co-op play to go for the highest score, and a mode that features enhanced graphics.

meta.name: Tubeway
meta.desc: Estás explorando los confines del espacio, cuando seres de un extraño universo geométrico comienzan a irrumpir en tu realidad con la intención de invadir la Tierra. Los escudos de fuerza alienígenas te han encerrado en el borde exterior de sus túneles geométricos, donde debes derribar las naves enemigas antes de que puedan entrar en tu dimensión.

meta.name: Tycoon : The Commodity Market Simulation
meta.desc: Like its predecessor Millionaire, Tycoon is a business simulation casting you in the role of an investor - this time in the commodity market. You start out with $10,000 in cash, and your quest is to turn that into much more by buying and selling various commodities. Each game turn represents one week, and allows you to survey the market by taking note of changing prices, reading the news, and reviewing the contracts on offer before you determine your actions. Your success is measured by your net worth; to win, you'll have to attain the status of Tycoon by amassing one million dollars.

meta.name: Ultima III : Exodus
meta.desc:
El tercer título de la serie Ultima, este fue el primer juego de la serie que contó con un grupo de aventureros en lugar de un solo jugador. También tenía ciudades grandes y detalladas con muchas personas con las que hablar, y un motor de combate separado donde tu grupo luchó contra múltiples monstruos en un sistema basado en turnos.
Después de la derrota del malvado mago Mondain y su amante Minax en las dos Ultimas, la paz ha regresado a la tierra de Sorsaria. El tiempo pasa y, finalmente, se producen alteraciones geológicas y un resurgimiento de la población de monstruos, y pronto se descubre que Mondain y Minax habían concebido un niño llamado "Exodus" antes de su muerte. Ahora ese niño se ha convertido en un adulto con el poder de vengar la muerte de sus padres. Obviamente, cae sobre ti el héroe para encontrarlo y detenerlo.
meta.manual: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/apple2/manuals/Ultima III - Exodus (1983)(Origin Systems)(Side A)[48K]-manual.pdf

p: Ultima IV - Quest of the Avatar (1985)(Origin Systems)(II+)(Disk 1 of 4)[cr Digital Gang][64K].zip
n: Ultima IV - Quest of the Avatar (1985)(Origin Systems)(II+)(Disk 1 of 4)[cr Digital Gang][64K]

meta.path: ./Ultima IV - Quest of the Avatar (1985)(Origin Systems)(II+)(Disk 1 of 4)[cr Digital Gang][64K].zip
meta.name: Ultima IV : Quest of the Avatar
meta.desc:
En una hermosa tarde, te recuestas contra un árbol y dormitas, cuando de repente aparece una especie de puerta azul no muy lejos de ti, de la que caen una cruz Ankh, un mapa de tela y dos libros, uno que describe el mundo de Britannia, el otro que habla de magia. Al levantarte, observas un campamento de nómadas, que sorprendentemente no habías visto antes, y eres conducido al carro del adivino, que te hace una serie de preguntas, tras lo cual te desmayas. Y cuando despiertas, el mundo de Britannia está ante ti. ¿Podrás demostrar tu valía y ser digno de ser el Avatar de las Virtudes?
Ultima IV es un juego de arriba abajo, con la excepción de las mazmorras, que están en una vista en primera persona. Las fases de combate son por turnos. Britannia es un vasto mundo que puedes explorar a tu antojo.

n: Ulysses and the Golden Fleece (1981)(On-Line Systems)(Side A)[48K][Hi-Res Adventure #4]

meta.name: Ulysses and the Golden Fleece
meta.desc: Odiseo y el vellocino de oro es un juego de aventuras en el que encarnas a Odiseo en la antigua Grecia. El rey te ha encomendado la tarea de encontrar el Vellocino de Oro, un tesoro legendario protegido por los dioses. Comienzas tu búsqueda en una pequeña ciudad, y tendrás que viajar a varios países lejanos para encontrar el vellocino. En el camino se encontrarán muchos personajes de la mitología antigua, como el rey Neptuno, las sirenas, los cíclopes y muchos más. Para tener éxito, tendrás que encontrar la manera de pasar a estos personajes, así como superar muchos obstáculos en mar y tierra.

meta.name: Under Fire!
meta.desc: Under Fire is a tactical simulation of WW II land combat. Men and weapons from the United States, Germany and Russia are represented. There are nine scenarios in the game which range from open field firefights to house to house conflict. Victory is a subjective affair in Under Fire as there is no declaration of victory. The victory screen basically tallies up losses and objectives gained the rest is left to argument. Under Fire can be played by one or two players. Playing time can range from 10 minutes for a two squad battle to five hours  for a 32 squad firefight.

p: United States Adventure (19xx)(First Star Software)[cr Mr. Krac-Man].zip
n: United States Adventure (19xx)(First Star Software)[cr Mr. Krac-Man]

meta.path: ./United States Adventure (19xx)(First Star Software)[cr Mr. Krac-Man].zip
meta.name: United States Adventure
meta.desc: United States Adventure is an educational game, that takes place entirely in the USA. You walk from state to state and have to find out when important events happened in each state. You gain score by knowing what direction to move to go to different states or for different events. You also have an inventory of helpful items but if you select an event that didn't occur in a certain state you will lose score and may lose stuff from your inventory.
